They go from wagon to wagon, seeing the freaks; R approves of the Blue Lady's callousness.

The freaks try to kidnap the heroes.

They meet The Twins, who are afraid of Patchwork (the stitched-soul man who runs the circus).

The CCK, Blue Lady, and R are captured.

name: 1. Title Card
text:
**The CCK, The Blue Lady, and R the Killer find a caravan of wagons on the road, in the middle of a terrible "Sideways Storm".**

Introduce the CCK, The Blue Lady, and R the Killer, 1-by-1

All the wagons are large as houses, tall as towers, leaning and crooked (figure out how to introduce without *texture*)

Heroes "flutter/chirp" at each other

Worry about the conflict *(warn audience - this is the last chance to leave)*

They see "freaks" staring at them from the canvas flaps.

Dialogue: one of the freaks introduces The Coffin Rollers.

- They are invited in.

name: 2. Arrival of the Foe
text:
**"The foe (*strings*) of the episode appears; the heroes are surprised; the conflict is set up."**

Foreshadow final resolution Focus on foe (*strings*), magic (*brass*), and heroes (*winds*) in that order

One small texture (*drums*) about midpoint (*save for next part*)

Lure the reader in with rising conflict - surrender, surrender, surrender! (*repeat a sentence, with variants*)

**The Heroes see various 'circus' acts by the different wagons.**

Give it mishmashed, off-kilter rhythm.

Small texture of wax golems (foreshadowing)

Some acts (possibly bold text list):

- Elongated leg-bone man
- Hypnotist toadlike woman (makes CCK uncomfortable)
- Carousel of Bones

name: 3. Enter the Arena
text:
**The Heroes enter The Silk Maze.**

Largest wagon in the caravan (bigger on the inside than the outside)

name: 4. False Hopes
text:
**The Dancing Conversation of The Twins.**

*Use this scene to give exposition on the Rollers, their lifestyle, and Patchwork.*

The heroes listen to a circling back-and-forth dialogue between two twin girls, who are afraid of the 'Stitched Soul Man'

- R interrupts them at one point, but the twins draw him in with some promise or information
- The Twins get progressively louder and more frightened *(huge texture (drums), introduce rising conflict(brass))*

name: 5. Trapped, Battle Dance
text:
**The freaks find the heroes in The Silk Maze, and give chase.**

Random list of treasures here, which the heroes can find in maze.

name: 6. The Master Appears
text:
**The heroes *hear* Patchwork.**

A many-faceted breathing, that rustles all the silks, coming from behind a curtain of rattling black beads.

name: 7. The Enemy Triumphs
text:
**"The heroes are beaten in a desperate direct conflict with the master foe."**

Huge, unique textural description (*drum*) brings us into aggressive finale

Environmental threats (*bass/cellos*) keep up vulgar rhythm

The Lackeys (*violas/horns*) surrounding and attacking

The Master (*trumpets/violins*) glorying in power

Whooping and shrieking, building and layering, to climax

The heroes are overwhelmed

**The heroes are captured by the freaks.**

The CCK and lady are suddenly grabbed; R the Killer is nowhere to be seen.

CCK fights the freaks (Action here)

The Strongman freak grabs the lady; the CCK is overcome by The Contortionist.

name: The Heroics
text:
The Contortionist (mad scientist) wants to melt the heroes, and put their bones inside of wax golems.

The CCK and R kill the contortionist, search for The Blue lady in The Endless Wagon (think Gwyndolin's arena), and fight the Strongman, Twins, and Patchwork.

name: Heroics - Chapter 1
text:
The Contortionist (mad scientist) wants to melt the heroes, and put their bones inside of wax golems.

The CCK and R kill the contortionist, search for The Blue lady in The Endless Wagon (think Gwyndolin's arena), and fight the Strongman, Twins, and Patchwork.

name: 8. Introduction
text:
**The Contortionist (mad scientist) has the CCK brought to The Chemical Show.**

A wagon specifically-purposed for scientific necromancy

The CCK is bound, his sword/shield taken

CCK learns the Coffin Rollers melt off skin and put people into wax golems.

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot
text:
**R the Killer is brought in bound too; and the two heroes are set beside each-other.**

The CCK sees his stuff set down by a shelf

The CCK realizes he can cut R's bonds with his rusty armor (very slowly).

Contortionist: "Your fingers are lovely. Your beauty is... very unusual."

name: 10. The Second Conflict
text:
**R the Killer kills the Contortionist (what a sentence...).**

Brutal morningstar work (*11-note death-rhythm texture*)

CCK gets his gear.

They run (Killing All The Way; most-savage battle yet)

name: 11. The Relieved Breath
text:
**The two stop and consider; the CCK determines to go after the lady, and (somehow, figure out) gets R to help.**

R stops, and initially suggests they get out of dodge.

name: 12. The Hero and the Master
text:
**The CCK and R hunt for the lady through the caravan.**

The many-breath of Patchwork gets louder and louder

Eventually, within the endless wagon (think Dark Sun Gwyndolin), they find The Lady (asleep), The Twins (now undead), The Strongman, and Patchwork.

name: 13. The Finale
text:
**Epic, brutal, final conflict.**

R+CCK VS The Freaks.

Patchwork is invincible while possessing the twins.

R fight the Strongman while CCK deals with the head honcho.

Structure like a multi-stage boss (foreshadow duel with old weaponsmaster).

- "You have never fought so difficult a battle in all your lives. You hope you never shall again."

name: The Soul Sense
text:
**A single, long dream-struggle of the CCK, where he is trying to save his soul-sense.**

The CCKs senses are no longer strong enough for his body; he has to have another person's senses stitched to his soul.

- Each person is made up of Body, Soul, and Senses
- The CCK's body is weak - made up of pieces
- His soul is still just barely strong enough to hold itself within the cracked vessel, but his Senses are too old and weak
- A series of self-contained vignettes/scenes; act as foils to one-another; very little contrast
- "Is my virtue - or my sin - still mine? When my body is in pieces, and my senses are another's?" *(Ship of Theseus)*

name: Planets Sole Act
text:
**A single, long dream-struggle of the CCK, where he is trying to save his soul-sense.**

The CCKs senses are no longer strong enough for his body; he has to have another person's senses stitched to his soul.

- Each person is made up of Body, Soul, and Senses
- The CCK's body is weak - made up of pieces
- His soul is still just barely strong enough to hold itself within the cracked vessel, but his Senses are too old and weak
- A series of self-contained vignettes/scenes; act as foils to one-another; very little contrast
- "Is my virtue - or my sin - still mine? When my body is in pieces, and my senses are another's?" *(Ship of Theseus)*

name: Planets Sole Chapter
text:
**A single, long dream-struggle of the CCK, where he is trying to save his soul-sense.**

The CCKs senses are no longer strong enough for his body; he has to have another person's senses stitched to his soul.

- Each person is made up of Body, Soul, and Senses
- The CCK's body is weak - made up of pieces
- His soul is still just barely strong enough to hold itself within the cracked vessel, but his Senses are too old and weak
- A series of self-contained vignettes/scenes; act as foils to one-another; very little contrast
- "Is my virtue - or my sin - still mine? When my body is in pieces, and my senses are another's?" *(Ship of Theseus)*

name: 1 - Battle (Mars)
text:
**The CCK opens with intense pain; he find himself suddenly drawn with his body, as his senses present mutinous figments.**

Quiet opening - a twinge, then electric agony that surges into his brain

The CCK suddenly finds himself in a vision of the founding of the road of graves:

- "You remember the first stone laid upon the road of graves. It happened before your life."

- The warring sorcerer-kings laid waste to each others' kingdoms, with armies of half-demon pig-men

- Violence and Sheer Terror

Each part of the road is like a part of the CCK's senses; each death is a death of a synapse

- CCK sees the world going dark, and knows his body is doing the same; *Experimental, craggy rhythm; clashing keys; pulsing drums*

CCK prays to the necromancy gods, as the undead close in around him

- One of the gods approaches (manifestation) - the worst one; the Red King; *Huge brass, singing strings, rolling drums*

CCK and manifestation of the red king do battle (for the road of graves, which is the CCK's body)

- Victory swaying, uncertain, hangs in the balance

CCK kills The Red King, then the worlds shrinks, and all his senses are lost

name: 2 - Rest (Venus)
text:
**The Lady calls the CCK back to his body; she tells him that she has reached out to Othelmedir.**

"I will hold your hands."

CCK's senses keep fading in/out - he is awake, but only for now.

name: 3 - Capers (Mercury)
text:
**CCK enters another vision being brought before his lady's ancient clan keep, back in The Lake of Tomatoes town.**

Open with weaponmaster ordering the CCK to display his skill, then attacking (a memory)

- CCK startled by his sudden youth, defends himself, realizes it is a memory, but it feels real

Sudden joy at sight of old weaponsmaster, and the old clan keep; tempered by a desperate fight

CCK is hard-pressed, looks like he will be slain; then his old lord emerged and calls weaponmaster off

Description of weaponmaster looking him over up close, lord looking at him from afar

- weaponmaster helps him up, quips, chuckles severely

name: 4 - Prestige (Jupiter)
text:
**CCK is inducted into his lord's service as a young man - his body is whole and strong**

Open with the knight winning in a duel (foreshadowing) vs a fellow noble who insulted him.

The knight turns to the lord, lady, and visiting family at the high table

The visiting nobles rise, one by one, grab the hilts of their knives, turn to the lord

The knight relaxes; the weaponmaster tells him he did well, earned his rank

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn)
text:
**The CCK sees a vision of Othelmedir, who explain that the CCK's soul-sense connection is tearing.**

The CCK is abruptly jerked out of his happy memory of home.

Dialogue: Othelmedir gives lore/exposition on body/soul/sense (*Set unsettling tone with Ticking Clock)*

Reflection: Low point - CCK feels distraught and hopeless

Dialogue: Othelmedir says that every soul must leave its body eventually, or they go mad (build clock speed to second half)

Reflection: CCK has to give up his own sense, it isn't strong enough

End: Othelmedir says he has given what guidance he can, and leaves

name: 6 - Spellcasting (Uranus)
text:
**CCK finds himself searches for senses on an upside-down RoG; all the dead are floating above him, staring down.**

Open with bold text: four distinct - enormous tombs under the road which the CCK notices on the road

The CCK begins to run along the road - passing the ghosts of other dead - the lost souls that still linger, uncollected, un-necromanced

The CCK hears the aspect of The Red King again - pursuing him (climactic, *epic* battle)

One of the Red King's champion spirits wraps the CCK like a poisonous cloud serpent

With his new sense, the imagined world vanishes

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune)
text:
**The CCK finds another set of senses; with the turn, he fades back into reality, which is somehow alien.**

Description: The CCK opens his eyes, and behold his Time of Dying

The knight reflects alone for a moment

The CCK realizes he is alone - but the lady rushes up at just that moment

She hold his hands again - do not say whether they are the same or not

The knight looks away from his lady, at the sun; then he lets go of her hands, rises, and goes to retrieve his armor and sword

name: Queen of the Hill
text:
**A witch story**

A woman and her daughter from pre-WWI England are stopped in Germany on way to meet up with the husband in Russia

A cult of witches tries to indoctrinate the daughter

name: Diablery Act 1
text:
Addie Pinnick and Penny Pinnick are stopped in the village of Saint Six (Sankt Sechs) when their train breaks down on the way to meet Addie's husband, Lionel Pinnick, a minster in St. Petersburg.

They end up staying in the schoolhouse of Frau Helena Kettel.

Addie has some reservations about Frau Kettel, but nothing concrete.

name: Diablery Chapter 1
text:
Addie Pinnick and Penny Pinnick are stopped in the village of Saint Six when their train breaks down on the way to meet Addie's husband, Lionel Pinnick, a minster in St. Petersburg.

They end up staying in the schoolhouse of Frau Helena Kettel.

Addie has some reservations about Frau Kettel, but nothing concrete.

name: Diablery Act 2
text:
Penny is gradually seduced by Helena into witchcraft; attending a Sabbath.

Meanwhile, Addie keeps trying to get in touch with the rail authorities, checking with other passengers, learning about the village and the witches.

name: Diablery Chapter 2
text:
Penny is gradually seduced by Helena into witchcraft; attending a Sabbath.

Meanwhile, Addie keeps trying to get in touch with the rail authorities, checking with other passengers, learning about the village and the witches.

name: Diablery Act 3
text:
Penny Pinnick is taken by the witch, Frau Kettel, to mate with the demon panther in the woods of The Hill - the old napoleonic fort.

Addie takes the rifle from Frau Kettel's house, and goes to save her daughter.

In the epilogue, the witches are dead, the daughter is found (but is having trouble keeping food down, keeps throwing up - dundundun) and Addie is still missing.

name: Diablery Chapter 3
text:
Penny Pinnick is taken by the witch, Frau Kettel, to mate with the demon panther in the woods of The Hill - the old napoleonic fort.

Addie takes the rifle from Frau Kettel's house, and goes to save her daughter.

In the epilogue, the witches are dead, the daughter is found (but is having trouble keeping food down, keeps throwing up - dundundun) and Addie is still missing.

name: Scene 1
text:
**Letter from Lionel Pinnick (British foreign minister to Russia) to his wife, Adelaide "Addie" Pinnick, detailing her trip from England to St. Petersburg.**

- Gives date/time
- Mentions passing through european countryside: "I daresay you will make me quite jelous with the stories of...
- Mentions daughter, Penny Pinnick - how she will love St. Petersburg
  - Life is easy, the place is practically modern; much easier than Africa

name: Scene 2
text:
**Diary entry of Addie Pinnick; written aboard the stopped train car in the middle of the german black forest**

- Start with a Julie Murphy joke
  - What runs on an even number of wheels, stands perfectly still on an odd number, and walked confusedly upon two legs in a crisis?
  - It must be the German rail industry.
- Wondering what has stopped their train
- Eager to see her husband again; thinks he can't function without her
- Worries over her daughter - too precocious; glad her daughter has her father's looks
  - "God grant me patience with the girl. Where she gets her willfullness from, I do not know."
- An addendum; train is broken down - it appears they must find accomodations until the train is fixed

name: Scene 3
text:
**Letter from stationmaster; asking for a crew/parts to fix the train.**

- Short
- Gives description of Addie - he describes her as a busy harridan
- "Thin as the last wheat stalk standing in a November field."
- Recommended her to The Green Man to get rid of her

name: Scene 4
text:
**Diary Entry of Addie; written in the old schoolhouse in St. Sechs, where she is staying with Frau Helena Kessel.**

- Initially looked for lodgings at The Green Man - did not like the look of the innkeeper
- Luckily, a younger woman came along - they got on so famously (not really a woman's word...) that she is staying at the young woman's house

name: Scene 5
text:
**Diary Entry of Addie; later that day.**

- Addie has taken after Penny, and spent the remainder of the day exploring the town - (*use as exposition on all the BIG SET PIECES for this story*)
- Some curious things about the young woman's schoolhouse - the empty space where a cross should have been
- Young woman also told a ghastly story about a panther in the forest; "He's a stranger here, like yourselves." (not really afternoon tea fayre).
- Addie isn't going to suggest they leave (not polite), but she is worried about penny getting too involved with the young woman
- She is comforted by a rifle over the fireplace, though she certainly hopes she does not have to shoot such a device; Lionel has shown her how, but she is a poor marksman
- Crows that perch on the top of the house

name: Scene 6
text:
**Telegraph from Lionel; he has heard about the train breakdown by telelgraph.**

- Comforts Addie, saying he is jealous of her chance to do a little rustic living - hopes she won't go rural on him.
- Asks her to telegraph back when she is able

name: Scene 1
text:
Addie's journal - today was trying

- woke up to find that Penny and Frau Hettel were gone
- found them later, they had been in the woods
- afraid to ask addie not to go with the frau, it will encourage her
- worse, train is still broke, finds out it may be another day

name: Scene 2
text:
Police Report of authorities in Karlsruhe

- Vandalism of goat herd in small town of Sankt Sechs
- Several goats torn apart by soem massive cat
- One goat missing

name: Scene 3
text:
Diary Entry of Addie (Evening): More activity: can't believe that rail fixes still aren't done, parts not even here; did reserve rooms at inn (keeper was nice)

- "Will that crow never stop Tap, Tap, Tapping?

name: Scene 4
text: Diary Entry of Addie (2 days later): argument with Penny/falling-out, she tried to get the journal

name: Scene 5
text: Telegram from Lionel: asking if she is okay again

name: Scene 6
text:
Diary Entry of Addie: next day

Addie feels bad about not responding to Lionel, will send a telegraph when they reach Karlsruhe

started asking about Helena, found out she was new, found out about other witches; went to talk to carpenter, he never got asked to make a new cross; postscript - Penny got back late, tells mother that she wants to go to a Sabat; Mother asks what a Sabat is; it's a certain kind of black magic ritual." Mother lectures, and Penny bites lip but nods

- They plan to leave after lunch

name: Scene 1
text: Diary entry of Addie: (Penny seems to have taken ill; has fallen asleep; there is a druggist in now; Addie is certain that Penny is sleeping; she is going to run over and fetch some aspirin)

name: Scene 2
text:
Note from Innkeeper to police: Addie came in almost in tears, asking about police, daughter missing

- My son, I hope you will know who is right to talk to. I wish that you will help this Englishwoman, for she a bossy but nice lady.
- In closing, I hope that all is well with you. Your father, TODO

name: Scene 3
text: Letter from Addie to police: left at the schoolhouse, she has taken the rifle, found out her daughter is was taken to "The Hill" (how?) is going after her

name: Scene 4
text:
Excerpt from a witchcraft ritual book, describing ritual of transferring soul

- Bring back Goat and Panther

name: Scene 5
text:
Police report from the scene of the crime: dead cat at entrance, one dead woman in the fort, trail leads to woods, another dead woman, many scattered tracks

- Railroad operator was the one who clued investigators in (out of genuine concern)

- 2 dead women, one shot through the chest, one with multiple stab wounds, one local women (not mother/daughter)

- 1 dead panther with a bullet hole in its skull

- Brisk description of crime scene by german police officer

- Two women still missing, lots of blood leading from scene, but trail goes cold in the woods

name: Scene 6
text: Letter from Lionel to friend; grieving, then finds out his daughter was found, but has amnesia

name: The Sewer Under Khoroduhn
text:
**The CCK sneaks into the Weige Family Castle in Khoroduhn, kills the family's Majordomo, and escapes the city with R the Killer and his Lady.**

Based on Khogorun/sixth-house; use for inspiration.

The Weige family worships The Red King.

name: Mother Goose Act
text:
**The CCK sneaks into the Weige Family Castle in Khoroduhn, kills the family's Majordomo, and escapes the city with R the Killer and his Lady.**

Based on Khogorun/sixth-house; use for inspiration.

The Weige family worships The Red King.

name: Mother Goose Chapter
text:
**The CCK sneaks into the Weige Family Castle in Khoroduhn, kills the family's Majordomo, and escapes the city with R the Killer and his Lady.**

Based on Khogorun/sixth-house; use for inspiration.

The Weige family worships The Red King.

name: 1 - Prelude
text:
**Dark-souls style prologue, introducing the Weige family, Khoroduhn, and the sewers below it.**

Opens with **RAIN**; encompasses the story (*two flutes and bassoon*)

- "In March, the rain always came down heavily over Khoroduhn.

*Flashes/vignettes of the scenes to come; set the tone, show the setting*

Zoom gradually in; create atmosphere of expectation; lead into…

name: 2 - Opening Conflict
text: **The CCK kills the Weige's Majordomo, Pigburne, and flees his quarters in the Weige castle.**

name: 3 - Mentor
text: **The CCK join's forces with R the Killer as he is escaping.**

name: 4 - First Transition
text: **Brief exposition - the CCK and R go to the Inn**

name: 5 - New Lover
text:
**The CCK retrieves the Blue Lady from the Inn, and the three of them enter Khoroduhn's Sewer.**

- The Blue Lady was waiting for CCK to return, disguised at an inn


- Lady and R hate each other; use to bring home the change in Tower of Silence
- The Gates are watched, and the family is put on alert - they must get out through the sewer

name: 6 - Second Transition
text: **They enter the sewer.**

name: 7 - Wilderness
text:
**Describe the various scenes in the sewer, the monstrous creatures, Lovecraftian/Demonic design, bubbling pools, satanic worship.**

Huge wilderness textures (*trills/cuckoos of violins*)

- Sewer has a colony of “ failed stitched souls” madmen; foreshadow Soul-Sense

- *(Sixth House Cultists)*

- The Stone Lanterns (Giant Lantern sculptures)

name: 8 - Third Transition
text:
**Introduce next scene.**

Brief hero conversation/conflict

- Return of **main motive**

Textures hint at the true conflict, the mysterious true foe (*harp/celesta*)

Sudden action by the foe puts heroes in desperate situation (*strings falling in chromatic line*)

Ominous, mysterious textures lead into…

name: 9 - Mysterious Master Foe
text:
**Battle with Giant Uthok.**

Open with shimmer, colorful textures of the arena; the final battle site

One boss-fight: Silk-Giant: a type of monster - name is Uthok (*Dagoth Uthol*)

- Skittering, wall-climbing giant

- Weaves at an enormous loom

A sudden change in scene/texture (*double tam-tam*) creates a last opportunity for the heroes

One last exchange and battle between the heroes and foe; musical, many elements converging

End on huge crashing finale, as the foe collapses

name: 10 - Fourth Transition
text: **With the Gigan Uthok dead, a hidden door opens, leading out of the sewer.**

name: 11 - Future and Finale
text:
**The CCK and Blue Lady emerge from the sewer; R tells them about Middlemoss, well beyond the Weige Family's influence.**

Begin with the **Rain** - recomposed, or seen in a new light

The hero and lover awaken to a new understanding of one another

They stare at the wonderful new landscape around them

- **Rain** echoed here again, once more seen in a new light

Characters from the other sections gather around

Main character finally achieves acceptance/enlightenment

Culmination of the **Main Motive**; Huge climax

- “They live happily ever after.”

name: The Graveside Inn
text:
***(Transition 1)* The CCK and his Lady stay at a manor-turned-inn, but find out that not all the guests are as living as they seem.**

A mystery - the guests are killed 1-by-1

The CCK first hears of the Lady's stepson here - they agree to search for him.

*The Secret is that all the guests are already dead.*

- *A dead witch’s curse was placed on the inn; under a Harridan’s Moon long ago*

name: Single Act
text:
***(Transition 1)* The CCK and his Lady stay at a manor-turned-inn, but find out that not all the guests are as living as they seem.**

A mystery - the guests are killed 1-by-1

The CCK first hears of the Lady's stepson here - they agree to search for him.

*The Secret is that all the guests are already dead.*

- *A dead witch’s curse was placed on the inn; under a Harridan’s Moon long ago*

name: Single Chapter
text:
***(Transition 1)* The CCK and his Lady stay at a manor-turned-inn, but find out that not all the guests are as living as they seem.**

A mystery - the guests are killed 1-by-1

The CCK first hears of the Lady's stepson here - they agree to search for him.

*The Secret is that all the guests are already dead.*

- *A dead witch’s curse was placed on the inn; under a Harridan’s Moon long ago*

name: Setup
text:
**Introduce the Graveside Inn, The CCK/Blue Lady, and the mystery of the murders.**

Open with quotation of some scholar.

Describe Lady's poor sleep; then they are asked downstairs

Introduce Seven other guests in a bold text list

They are locked in the setting, knowing that one of them is the killer...

name: Conflict
text:
**The CCK tries to leave with his lady, but the Steward refuses to let them go until the murderer is found.**

The other residents go to eat breakfast in the dining hall; Meanwhile, the Lady wants to go to the murder room; CCK accompanies her.

- They find signs of an undead monster (The master foe) - there is some evil in the mansion

Another guest is murdered; the steward and several others were split up; the stewards knife was used

- The Steward plans to destroy the murderer

Another guest dies (You cannot save...); The Poet speaks

CCK is knocked out and separated

name: Drive
text:
**The CCK is seperated from his Lady by the monster.**

The CCK is woken up and tied up by the steward; his lady is missing, and another is dead.

- He convinces the steward to let him go, but the steward won't give his sword back.
- He is DESPERATE to find his lady

Random Deaths; CCK and Steward become seperated

The Sweetmeats Room; The Late Poet is scattered (CCK assumes him dead - he will be back at the end).

name: Finale
text:
**CCK kills monster, rescues lady; they are left alone amidst the bodies; then everything resets, and they go in search of her lost son.**

Action: CCK realizes killer is a monster, not one of the guests, starts hunting

Description: wander for hours, then finally find lady and triggers final battle

Description: undead scarecrow

Action: Battle steps

Description: Monster Vanishes, Guests reset, Poet is fine

Dialogue: Late poet mention's Lady's son

In The Time of Dying...

name: The Temple of Lady Horsehair
text:
**The CCK and his Lady seek information about her stepson at the Temple of Lady Horsehair.**

- Plot based on **Spirited Away.**

name: Adoration of the Foe
text:
The CCK and Lady learn that the head priestess of The Emerald house, The Temple, may have the info they need.

The Priestesses say that the price is a painting by The Blue Lady (they want to steal her soul through her art)

- As she paints, the color drains from her, flows into the dream-like painting (something like salvadore dali)

When the CCK tries to stop the painting, the High Priestess the Exhibition of Wasted Paint (used/expended phylactery-paintings)

name: Adoration - Chapter 1
text:
The CCK and Lady learn that the head priestess of The Emerald house, The Temple, may have the info they need.

The Priestesses say that the price is a painting by The Blue Lady (they want to steal her soul through her art)

- As she paints, the color drains from her, flows into the dream-like painting (something like salvadore dali)

When the CCK tries to stop the painting, the High Priestess has him detained.

name: 1. Title Card
text:
**The CCK and Blue Lady decide to visit The Emerald House to find out about The Lady's son.**

Start with warning from The Late Poet

As they enter: The Lady offends a monk from The Tower of Silence (foreshadow)

On the other side, they see a green-painted marble sepulcher

name: 2. Arrival of the Foe
text:
**The CCK and Blue Lady meet a priestess of Lady Horsehair ; she leads them through the temple**

Foreshadow final resolution Focus on foe (*strings*), magic (*brass*), and heroes (*winds*) in that order

Pass down one corridor of paintings (really, phylacteries - hint at this)

Lure the reader in with rising conflict - surrender, surrender, surrender! (*repeat a sentence, with variants*)

name: 3. Enter the Arena
text:
**Arrival at the FANE; an art studio with balconies, where the priestess can watch a person paint.**

- Huge wall paintings of some_undead (Secretly real)

Open with huge textural description (*drums*) of the scene, followed by magic (*brass*)

Focus on vivid descriptions most; the rest, a little of heroes, foe, and magic/action

name: 4. False Hopes
text:
**The CCK and Blue Lady are told that the temple has information, all they want is a painting; lady starts to paint.**

The Blue Lady explains what she wants, and the priestess considers and responds

A lore backdrop on the temple.

name: 5. Trapped, Battle Dance
text:
**The CCK sees his lady straining; he tries to beg that she is sick; the priestesses say the painting has already started.**

The canvas immediately begins to draw out a tether to The Blue Lady's soul.

The six priestesses stop the CCK from disrupting the ceremony, threatne profaning godliness.

name: 6. The Master Appears
text:
**The Lady keeps adding more paint; the canvas seems to be absorbing both the paints' color and hers.**

The Other priestesses bring in a special set of "paint" (really, the binding oil of Lady Horsehair).

name: 7. The Enemy Triumphs
text:
**The CCK tries to rescue his lady, seeing her color drain; the priestesses summon their painted undead guardians, and take him away.**

Huge, unique textural description (*drum*) brings us into aggressive finale

Environmental threats (*bass/cellos*) keep up vulgar rhythm

The Lackeys (*violas/horns*) surrounding and attacking

The Master (*trumpets/violins*) glorying in power

Whooping and shrieking, building and layering, to climax

The heroes are overwhelmed

name: The Heroics
text:
**CCK finds himself trapped with The Late Poet.**

List of priestly “classes” (really: fighting styles, professions, careers; can do a few of these)

name: Heroics - Chapter 1
text:
CCK finds himself trapped with The Late Poet.

List of priestly “classes” (really: fighting styles, professions, careers; can do a few of these)

name: 8. Introduction
text:
**The CCK finds himself trapped in the Exhibition of Wasted Paint (used/expended phylactery-paintings).**

He is with The Late Poet (who thought the temple might have his star(muse))

Describe the control pattern of the golems (action)

Describe the washed-out portraits of The Exhibition (*minor texture*)

Reflection: CCK considers how he can get the keys to the door from one of the golems

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot
text:
**The CCK speaks with a discarded chemist who designed the special paints; he tells the CCK of the Unhue - an acid which can break the binding.**

The CCK meets the Chemist named Squide - he suggests the Unhue as an option.

A slow-paced, quiet scene; heroes (*winds*) take turns in speaking/acting Very little texture (*drums*)

Magic/tech/action (*brass*) acts as exclamation-point; leads back to throbbing chords

The CCK recognizes one of the paintings as a mimic, a false painting, probably concealing a door.

name: 10. The Second Conflict
text:
**The CCK kills the mimic and escapes through the secret passage.**

Bold text: 11 rounds with the old man below

Describe dead mimic, and escape through the tunnel.

name: 11. The Relieved Breath
text:
**The CCK finds a garden with a trellis that could be climbed to the outside, above Middlemoss, limned in moonlight.**

The CCK takes a moment to rest, knowing that enemies still lie below

name: 12. The Hero and the Master
text:
**The CCK can sneak or fight his way through the galleries (present as two options); he finds the special paint.**

Finds his Steel

Heroes and foes try action/magic (*brass*)

Victory sways in the balance between the two forces

A Slow dance, neither side willing to show their hand; a "cold conflict"

Ends on the muscle (*bass clarinet*) bubbly and excited for the finale

List of priestly “classes”

name: 13. The Finale
text:
**The CCK steps back into the ceremony chamber (empty), washes the canvas of his lady, then has to fight the twin Painted Golems of the temple.**

The CCK re-enters the easel room; his Lady lying unconscious nearby, CCK plans to pick her up and carry her out.

The priestesses crowd onto the balcony; the golems emerge

The CCK fights the golems, saves his lady, and leaves

name: Floating Rib
text:
**Fairy Tale:** *The Singing Bone* (ATU 780)\
**Setting:** Inside a giant's digestive system\
**Main Character:** Blind painter who can see the future

name: Floating Rib
text:
**Fairy Tale:** *The Singing Bone* (ATU 780)\
**Setting:** Inside a giant's digestive system\
**Main Character:** Blind painter who can see the future

name: Chapter
text:
**Fairy Tale:** *The Singing Bone* (ATU 780)\
**Setting:** Inside a giant's digestive system\
**Main Character:** Blind painter who can see the future

name: Setup
text:
**Introduce the Setting, the Hero, and the Plot.**

Edda (blind painter hero) is holding her hand to the calcified wall of the tunnel, brush held pensively in hand

The Doctors (medicine men) ask what she has seen

End with her wanting to find a way of informing Jakob about her *real* vision - his disintigration in the Hissing Green Sea

name: Conflict
text:
**Create problems and enemies.**

Open with a dialogue between two commoners working at the artery field

Jump to scene of Lutz leading a trial  of Edda

The Doctor's are sympathetic, but they agree that her talents "better serve the dwellers of those lower tunnels" (the intestines, exile)

name: Drive
text:
**Draw the opponents closer together.**

Edda is stumbling in the dark intestines, lost, blind

Dwellers debate killing her - little food makes its way all the way to *The Way Dark and Long*

name: Finale
text:
**End the story with a Knockout blow.**

Open with Lutz's doctor voice, refusing to buy her back:

Edda finally interrupts, repeats what the bone has been saying in a whisper

A gurgle - the elder says that her words match an old prophecy of a way out of the body

name: The Canopy of the Ents
text:
**The Lady and CCK must cross the Canopy of the Ents.**

They first escape the mist of the soulwood.

They cross the canopy, and eventually find a dying Ent.

At the heart of the dying Ent, the CCK does battle with a mutant stag.

They climb down, out of the Soulwood.

name: Planets Sole Act
text:
**The Lady and CCK must cross the Canopy of the Ents.**

They first escape the mist of the soulwood.

They cross the canopy, and eventually find a dying Ent.

At the heart of the dying Ent, the CCK does battle with a mutant stag.

They climb down, out of the Soulwood.

name: Planets Sole Chapter
text:
**The Lady and CCK must cross the Canopy of the Ents.**

They first escape the mist of the soulwood.

They cross the canopy, and eventually find a dying Ent.

At the heart of the dying Ent, the CCK does battle with a mutant stag.

They climb down, out of the Soulwood.

name: 1 - Battle (Mars)
text:
**The CCK and Lady must climb through a dense, upward, cliff-like thicket of ivy while pursued by a poisonous fog from The Old Soulwood.**

Quiet opening - they pause on the ledge of the tree.

Textural description of the rising fog, climbing the forest.

CCK starts cutting a way though, while Lady worries; they are blocked by a thorny bramble.

Lady speaks, feeling the fog hissing up her legs

With a last blow, sunlight breaks through the dense thicket, and they climb out into the canopy

name: 2 - Rest (Venus)
text:
**CCK and Lady stand atop the canopy, and see the swaying, shifting, connected archtrees stretching out before them.**

Describe the vast sweep of the canopy; the Ent-trees dominating the regular soulwood Below.

Some worldbuilding; do a lore backdrop on the Ents - sentient, unmoving trees

The Lady speaks about the colors/textures of the Ent-trees

Reflection: the CCK ponders about the swaying trees; he knows they are alive

Action: Describe how the CCK and Lady can stand and admire the scene for many hours

name: 3 - Capers (Mercury)
text:
**CCK and Lady's tree begins to move; they run to a steady-looking pine, a limb that stretches like the bridge of a castle-city.**

Action: as dusk approaches, the tree begins to shift and quake beneath them

Description: Fast-paced action of CCK and lady running for the steady, unmoving tree, with the grey shapes that they see are pinecones

name: 4 - Prestige (Jupiter)
text:
**The CCK and Lady come to a "grove within a grove"; a place of peace and sunlight in the hollow center of a dead tree.**

Description: The CCK and Lady walk down the stiff branch, into the silent, tree like a cathedral.

Dialogue: Lady wonders how this tree died

Description: they step into the "grove within a grove" (bold text).

Dialogue: They meet The Late Poet.

Action: Bold text "The Late Poet's Fork"

Dialogue: Lady mentions how the fog has rolled back, they have crossed the soulwood ravine.

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn)
text:
**They Climb down through the canopy, and find younger trees dead as well (within the large tree); they hear the steady ticking of some great, sick heart.**

Nothing you do now matters. (*Set unsettling tone with Ticking Clock)*

Reflection: The knights looks at his hands, and thinks that they are backwords

One long sequence of beating ticks of the heart - where your text gets larger/louder (sometimes echoing, play with the format)

Bold Text: Dread

Description: They descend deeper and deeper in the vein-tunnel of the wood

name: 6 - Spellcasting (Uranus)
text:
**In the dead heart of the canopy of the Ents, they find the mutant, rooted stag. It closes off their escape, and the CCK battles it.**

Description: Bold text of The Four-Pronged Heart - describe the mutant stag-head suspended from the dome of the ceiling.

Action: The rooted stag-thief-heart hybrid closes off the exits, and attacks.

Heroes begin desperate, insane fight with the foe; everything exaggerated

In order to win, you must cut the thief-gauntlets off of the pursing thief, don them, and pluck the other eye from the four-pronged heart

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune)
text:
**The CCK and Lady descend to the forest floor; the Ents are perfectly still around them; they have left the Soulwood, and returned to a sadder world.**

The CCK and Lady awaken in the piles sawdust on the forest floor

They meet with miners of the glow moss - paint the trunks of the trees in glowing moss

Reflection: The CCK wonders about his Lady - why she was unresponsive in the tree.

Description: A chill wind blows through the trees, and they emerge from the trunks into a barren, open land

Dialogue: Lady finally speaks

In The Time of Dying...

name: The Stone Cocoon
text:
***(Transition 3)* The CCK and his Blue Lady must take shelter from a Snowstorm inside The Stone Cocoon.**

A volcanic hot spring, surrounded by ice, in a grotto.

The romantic episode.

name: Mother Goose Act
text:
***(Transition 3)* The CCK and his Blue Lady must take shelter from a Snowstorm inside The Stone Cocoon.**

A volcanic hot spring, surrounded by ice, in a grotto.

The romantic episode.

name: Mother Goose Chapter
text:
***(Transition 3)* The CCK and his Blue Lady must take shelter from a Snowstorm inside The Stone Cocoon.**

A volcanic hot spring, surrounded by ice, in a grotto.

The romantic episode.

name: 1 - Prelude
text:
**Open with Lore exposition: describe the ancient battle fought here, one of the last of the demon kings destroyed by the combined power of the necromancers.**

Opens with **The Cocoon (main motive)**; lore backdrop on how it is the symbol of love in The Time of Dying

Describe the great battle; think Dark souls intro

"Only the bones of the great demon remain." - a single horn protruding from its head

Land is now barren; but, it has a habit of hallucinations - dangerous, hateful ones

Zoom gradually in; create atmosphere of expectation; lead into…

name: 2 - Opening Conflict
text:
**Jump to CCK and Lady caught in snowstorm, freezing, looking for shelter.**

Illusions keep trying to pull the Lady away, the CCK has to guide her along.

Reflection of the CCK - at first he feels strong, able to lead his lady, almost happy that she is weak

Bold text of two options: Approach the vision of your old lord

Bold Text description: The Demon King's Sword

Lady Dialogue

name: 3 - Mentor
text:
**The CCK hears footsteps crunching in the snow.**

Briefly describe the hill, from the perspective of The Mother of Worms.

name: 4 - First Transition
text:
**They see the mouth of the cave, under the detached jaw of the demon skull.**

Present a list of actions.

Or possibly bold text of different visions he might see if he follows the footsteps.

Ends softly/hopefully, with brief return of **Main Motive**

name: 5 - New Lover
text:
**The CCK and Lady take shelter near the entrance of the cocoon; but a mad Whiteye Walker warns them of the dangers; then, he attacks.**

Describe the sulfurous odor of the cave.

The CCK fends off the Lady's advances.

They are interrupted by the Mad Whiteeye Walker, who warns them of danger in the cave.

The Walker attacks them; short action fight description - how to beat this enemy.

The Lady shows even greater affection, treating the CCK like her savior.

name: 6 - Second Transition
text:
**The Lady suddenly flees deeper into the smoky cave.**

Describe the rising fire in the ice wall, like burning cathedrals.

The CCK drags the madman's body away.

Bends down to inspect a carpet of silk, woven into a scene, on the floor.

Hears a shout, and sees his lady running deeper into the cave.

name: 7 - Wilderness
text:
**The CCK and Lady Flee from visions; one of them is The Lady's Daughter.**

Description: The CCK runs after the lady, but immediately becomes lost in the caverns.

Action: The CCK has to fight his way down various paths in the caverns.

Description: The CCK finds his lady huddled down in HUGE_TEXTURE_ROOM

Dialogue: Reveal here that the daughter was the one who had her whole family destroyed.

Action: The CCK should cut down the daughter's ghost

name: 8 - Third Transition
text:
**The CCK and Lady, while running, hear squeaking (mushrooms underfoot).**

Brief Lady dialogue, she is terrified.

Blue light spreads slowly around them from mushrooms; lady is blissfully happy to see CCK.

name: 9 - Mysterious Master Foe
text:
**The CCK and Lady enter the Stone Cocoon; she tries to bed him.**

Open with shimmer, colorful texture of the grotto.

Dialogue: The Lady shows her affection to the CCK

Reflection: The CCK admits to himself what is happening; she is "settling for him".

Action: The CCK must resist her.

Dialogue: Weeping, the Lady asks the knight to turn away. She is tired.

name: 10 - Fourth Transition
text:
**The Lady goes to the grotto pool; she stares at it for a while; CCK reflects that she must be watching her daughter.**

They go to sleep, seperately.

name: 11 - Future and Finale
text:
**Jump to their exit from the cave; neither is speaking; they wander farther east, bidding farewell to the illusions**

The CCK and Lady navigate back through the fast-freezing stone cocoon.

The CCK reflect on whether or not he did the right thing.

Description of their emergence from the cave.

Dialogue: The Lady apologizes for her behavior, and thanks her knight for his righteousness.

Action: No matter which direction you go...

In the Time of Dying...

name: The Harridan's Moon
text:
**Under a Harridan's Moon, the CCK must carry his Blue Lady to a witch on the moor, to cure the Lady of a fatal poison.**

Setup: Lady has been bitten by a Nickel Beetle - the same poisonous beetle that killed Wrathmaige The Walker. The CCK sees the lonely hut on the moor.

Conflict: The witch lures the CCK and Lady into her hut with

Drive: The CCK follows and fights his way after the hag-hut, across the moor.

Finale: The CCK defeats both the hut and the hag, and rescues his lady.

name: 4-Part Act
text:
**Under a Harridan's Moon, the CCK must carry his Blue Lady to a witch on the moor, to cure the Lady of a fatal poison.**

Setup: Lady has been bitten by a Nickel Beetle - the same poisonous beetle that killed Wrathmaige The Walker. The CCK sees the lonely hut on the moor.

Conflict: The witch lures the CCK and Lady into her hut with

Drive: The CCK follows and fights his way after the hag-hut, across the moor.

Finale: The CCK defeats both the hut and the hag, and rescues his lady.

name: Single Chapter
text:
**Under a Harridan's Moon, the CCK must carry his Blue Lady to a witch on the moor, to cure the Lady of a fatal poison.**

Setup: Lady has been bitten by a Nickel Beetle - the same poisonous beetle that killed Wrathmaige The Walker. The CCK sees the lonely hut on the moor.

Conflict: The witch lures the CCK and Lady into her hut with

Drive: The CCK follows and fights his way after the hag-hut, across the moor.

Finale: The CCK defeats both the hut and the hag, and rescues his lady.

name: Setup
text:
**Introduce the Setting, the Hero, and the Plot.**

Description: CCK is running across the moors, moon high above, the road is far behind them.

Dialogue: Lady expresses her gratitude.

Reflection: CCK knows what she has been bitten by - he is desperate to find help somewhere.

Action: If you have come this far into the wilderness, it is useless to turn back.

The CCK sees the lonely hut on the moor.

Dialogue: The Hag's voice drifts from the glowing crack in the door.

name: Conflict
text:
**The Witch welcomes the CCK and Lady into her hut; she tricks the knight, and flees with the lady in her walking hut.**

Description: The Hag's hut.

Dialogue: The Hag tells the CCK about the poison and it's cures, and why she lives on the moor.

Reflection: the CCK doesn't trust her. Thinks she might be a witch, because they live in such places.

Action: CCK must decide to give up his sword, or cut this old woman down.

Dialogue: The old hag cackles once she has the knight's sword; tells him he shouldn't make deals with strangers.

Action: The hag will now give you one of three tasks...

Dialogue: Hag gives the CCK the task of retrieving water from the well.

Description: Hut kicks CCK into the pond.

name: Drive
text:
**The CCK tracks the hut across the moor, and fights a pack of spider-wolves.**

Description: Exposition of the hag.

Description: CCK nearly drowns - when he climbs out, the hut is gone.

Reflection: The CCK berates himself for falling for the trick of the old woman in a lonely place.

Long Action: CCK has to fight spider-wolf pack.

Short Reflection: CCK feels week, his hand/ankle is wounded.

Description: The goat-hut has fled to the highland mountain, to the orchard-copse.

name: Finale
text:
**CCK cuts the hag-hut down, storm inside, and kills the hag.**

Dialogue: As CCK goes to enter the hut, the hag's voice drifts from the door.

Description: Set the stage for the battle.

Description: Bold text of The Witch-Beast of Windy Moor

Action: Part 1 of the final fight.

Action: Part 2 of the final fight.

Action: Part 3 of the final fight.

In the time of dying...

name: The Tower of Silence
text:
**The CCK must rescue his Lady from The Monks of The Lidless, in The Tower of Silence.**

The Lady undergoes a change; she is more direct in her speech (less dreamy).

The monks of Lidlessness gather souls for The Scarab Mines; making silent beetles.

R, the killer is here, and notes how the lady has changed.

name: Adoration of the Foe
text:
**The CCK sees as his lady is taken by The Monks of The Lidless; he joins forces with R and infiltrates their Tower of Silence.**

Open with CCK looking out upon Middlemoss ravine from tavern/hostel window.

CCK sees his Lady taken by The Monks of The Lidless; The Monks enter The Tower of Silence.

CCK meets with R the Killer; they sneak in through The Wine Chrypt; fight the "Senseless Ones".

Climaxes in a battle with the Senseless King and Queen.

name: Adoration - Chapter 1
text:
**The CCK sees as his lady is taken by The Monks of The Lidless; he joins forces with R and infiltrates their Tower of Silence.**

Open with CCK looking out upon Middlemoss ravine from tavern/hostel window.

CCK sees his Lady taken by The Monks of The Lidless; The Monks enter The Tower of Silence.

CCK meets with R the Killer; they sneak in through The Wine Chrypt; fight the "Senseless Ones".

Climaxes in a battle with the Senseless King and Queen.

name: 1. Title Card
text:
**The CCK looks out upon Middlemoss; he sees the approaching Monks, and watches as his Lady is taken.**

Open with CCK reflecting on failure to find Stepson in Middlemoss.

Description: Bold text list of pedestrians/sights visible out on the street from the boarding house window.

Dialogue: Someone in the street shouting for people to make way for the men from Tower of silence (they are revered).

Reflection of the CCK as he sees Lady approaching; she looks sorrowful

Short Description: CCK watches as lady is taken.

name: 2. Arrival of the Foe
text:
**The CCK pursues the monks through the catacomb streets of Middlemoss.**

Action: CCK emerges onto the street, but monks have disappeared.

Dialogue: Idiosyncratic pedestrian notices the CCK's distress.

Description: List of treasures that will work.

Action: After you receive your direction or find an alternative way, you'll need to...

name: 3. Enter the Arena
text: **CCK sees the monks again too late; the monks have entered The Tower.**

name: 4. False Hopes
text:
**The CCK meets R, who suggests sneaking in through The Wine Chrypt.**

Dialogue: Bold Text of R the Killer:

Description: The Wine Chrypt.

Action: The CCK and R are spotted, and have to fight several of the monks

Dialogue: R blames CCK for their discovery, says it was his armor

name: 5. Trapped, Battle Dance
text:
**"The heroes are trapped in the arena; forced into direct conflict with the foe."**

Different elements compete for attention

- Enemy (*strings*) keep up chunga, chunga, chunga rhythm
- Magic/action/tech (*brass*) call back/forth
- Heroes (*winds*) chirp a happy tune

Action: They have to fight several more monks.

Description: They end up in a "chess-like mini-game".

name: 6. The Master Appears
text: **The Senseless King and Queen rise from their vaults in the floor.**

name: 7. The Enemy Triumphs
text:
**The CCK and R battle the Senseless King and Senseless Queen.**

LONG Action: The CCK and R kill the King and Queen.

name: The Heroics
text:
**The CCK and R regroup with The Lady, flee/fight/sneak-by the monks, then fight The Lidless in the tower Owlery.**

CCK and R sneak up from The Wine Chrypt.

They eventually find the Blue Lady chained to a chair in The Silent Theater.

The three are discovered as they plot escape.

They have to flee from one of the monks' patrolling Will-o-Wisps.

The three climb the tower, trying to avoid The Monks.

At the Apex of the tower, in The Owlery, the CCK and R have to fight "The Lidless".

name: Heroics - Chapter 1
text:
**The CCK and R regroup with The Lady, flee/fight/sneak-by the monks, then fight The Lidless in the tower Owlery.**

CCK and R sneak up from The Wine Chrypt.

They eventually find the Blue Lady chained to a chair in The Silent Theater.

The three are discovered as they plot escape.

They have to flee from one of the monks' patrolling Will-o-Wisps.

The three climb the tower, trying to avoid The Monks.

At the Apex of the tower, in The Owlery, the CCK and R have to fight "The Lidless".

name: 8. Introduction
text:
**The Temple undercroft "lapses into silence"; R and the CCK venture up into The Tower.**

Reflection: CCK wonders what the monks use to guard their sanctum, if those were their outcasts.

Dialogue: R asks CCK to be quieter this time around.

Reflection: CCK wonder's at R's motivations.

Action: Quiet description of routes through the tower

Description: They eventually find the Blue Lady chained to a chair in The Silent Theater.

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot
text:
**R, the CCK, and the Blue Lady plot to escape the tower; they are discovered just as CCK breaks the Blue Lady's bonds.**

Dialogue: The Blue lady is thankful that they've come.

Action: CCK will have to go behind the curtain.

Dialogue: Once CCK unlocks the shackles, Lady is freed.

Description: Slow approach of the light from the corridor.

name: 10. The Second Conflict
text:
**The three flee from a Will-o-Wisp.**

Action: 11 Rounds with The Old Man Below.

Action: They have to flee from one of the monks' patrolling Wisps.

name: 11. The Relieved Breath
text:
**A brief bit of dialogue between R and the Blue Lady.**

Dialogue: The Lady speaks directly about escape.

Dialogue: R notes how the lady has changed.

name: 12. The Hero and the Master
text:
**The three have to sneak up through the tower, trying to avoid the monks and the Will-o-wisps.**

Action: Present three different routes/paths the three might take through the tower.

Description: R finds the mirror he wanted in a room flooded with an inch of water

Action: SLOW action of how the trio makes the final ascent to the owlery

Dialogue: R takes a few test swings before they enter the Owlery.

name: 13. The Finale
text:
**The CCK and R fight "The Lidless" in the tower Owlery.**

Description of Arena and The Lidless.

Action: Fight, part 1.

Action: Fight, part 2.

Action: Fight, part 3.

In The Time of Dying...

name: The Sisterhood of the Yellow Smile
text:
**The CCK must choose between his to his Blue Lady, and his oath to "Maintain the sanctity of The Road."**

The Lady is prematurely buried by The Sisterhood of the Yellow Smile.

R the Killer meets his ultimate fate in this episode (Trapped by the sisterhood in a glass illusion?).

name: Planets Sole Act
text:
**The CCK must choose between his to his Blue Lady, and his oath to "Maintain the sanctity of The Road."**

The Lady is prematurely buried by The Sisterhood of the Yellow Smile.

R the Killer meets his ultimate fate in this episode (Trapped by the sisterhood in a glass illusion?).

name: Planets Sole Chapter
text:
**The CCK must choose between his to his Blue Lady, and his oath to "Maintain the sanctity of The Road."**

The Lady is prematurely buried by The Sisterhood of the Yellow Smile.

R the Killer meets his ultimate fate in this episode (Trapped by the sisterhood in a glass illusion?).

name: 1 - Battle (Mars)
text:
**The CCK serves his duty as a knight of The Road, slaying stonecats on the outskirts of Hornwater.**

Description: Quiet descriptive opening of The Buried Forest

Action: CCK fighting graverobbers

Description: Graverobbers led by a necromancer atop a "Tolling Hearse" (bold text)

Action: The CCK and R kill the necromancer and the Hearse

Dialogue: R finishes off the necromancer

name: 2 - Rest (Venus)
text:
**The CCK and R return to The Scattered Shrine, and report to the Deacon Necromancer.**

Description: Set the scene of their approach to the edge of Hornwater.

Reflection: CCK wonder's about his Lady not coming out to meet him, and about the sullen mood of R.

Action: The CCK and R meet with a Proud Necromancer (one who follows sanctity of road) and complete their "optional quest"

Dialogue: CCK and R have a single heart-to-heart moment (since R will be dead soon).

Description: Describe the last departure of R.

name: 3 - Capers (Mercury)
text:
**The CCK travels into Hornwater in search of his Blue Lady.**

Description: Describe the city (based on venice; castles, islands, lakes, etc).

Dialogue: A Living Bones walks by with a cart of wares (hawking).

Action: Knight tries to buy information on his lady's whereabouts from a nearby Emerald House (horsehair temple).

name: 4 - Prestige (Jupiter)
text:
**The CCK is waylaid, and invited to be a court knight at The Palace at Burning Shoals.**

Description: The CCK is stopped by a royal procession.

Dialogue: The CCK is invited to Palace at Burning Shells (an island in the city lake).

Description: Loading-screen-like exposition on the guilds of Hornwater.

Reflection: The CCK wonders why he has been invited.

Description: CCK marches down the Palace Coral Reception (bold text).

Dialogue: The Guildlords (royal family) there have heard of his honor, his fidelity to the tenets of the road.

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn)
text:
**The CCK finds out his Lady has been taken by The Sisterhood of the Yellow Smile, and that R is dead.**

Description: CCK is given a note for a funeral service.

Reflection: CCK won't believe it; he thinks they have kidnapped her; blames himself.

Description: A bold text list of different sisterhood's/brotherhoods of the road, and the fates they promise.

Action: The CCK goes to the sisterhood's temple.

Dialogue: The prioress refuses to let him see the lady, or to reveal her location until after she is buried.

Description: The CCK sees the fate of men who accept "payment for death".

name: 6 - Spellcasting (Uranus)
text:
**The CCK fights the sisterhood, abandoning his oath to the sacred laws of The Road.**

Action: The CCK goes to rescue his lady from The Sisterhood.

Description: The Veilbend (covered section of road for buried alive).

Dialogue: The prioress sees him draw his sword.

Action: Many of the enemies are glass illusions - ghosts trapped in glass, with illusory strength, controlled by the sisterhood.

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune)
text:
**The Lady emerges from her tomb; she and the CCK wander among the streets of Hornwater.**

Action: The last glass illusion crackles to silence.

Dialogue: Lady emerges from her tomb, cries, thanks CCK

Reflection: CCK examines himself, but instead of addressing himself (you), he addresses the necromancer-gods directly.

Description: The pair beat a hasty exit from the priory.

Dialogue: As they emerge, the lady wants to know the CCK's mind.

Reflection: The CCK tries to decide where to go in Hornwater.

In the Time of Dying...

name: The Weapon Master
text:
**The CCK must face his old master-knight, kept living by necromancy, serving another Lady.**

Crosscut between old bond and new rivalry.

name: Mother Goose Act
text:
**The CCK must face his old master-knight, kept living by necromancy, serving another Lady.**

Crosscut between old bond and new rivalry.

name: Mother Goose Chapter
text:
**The CCK must face his old master-knight, kept living by necromancy, serving another Lady.**

Crosscut between old bond and new rivalry.

name: 1 - Prelude
text:
**An "opening at the end": dialogue from the weaponsmaster; mixed with reflection by the CCK.**

Dialogue: Weaponmaster taunts CCK; encompasses entire narrative.

Description: Describe the old weaponmaster in detail.

Reflection: CCK won't believe that this is the man he knew.

name: 2 - Opening Conflict
text:
**The present: the CCK is fighting off assassins from the Long Knives guild.**

Action: Open in the middle of an action sequence.

Description: The CCK and Lady are in the middle of Hornwater's lake town.

Dialogue: The Lady tries to reassure the CCK that his soul is still a good one.

name: 3 - Mentor
text:
**The CCK sees his weapon master; the Blue Lady recognizes him too.**

Description: Bold text: a familiar face in the crowd.

name: 4 - First Transition
text:
**Jump back to present: The weaponmaster has captured the CCK's Lady.**

The Weapon master has the Lady bound/gagged.

name: 5 - New Lover
text:
**The CCK meets and talks to the Weaponmaster, who explains that his own Lady is missing.**

Dialogue: The weaponmaster notices the CCK and Lady; he storms over to them.

Reflection: CCK looks back to fond memories of training in Clan Fathmail.

Dialogue: The Lady speaks (in aside) to the CCK.

Action: The weaponmaster guides them through the crowded city market stalls; to hide from assassins.

name: 6 - Second Transition
text:
**Jump back to present: Description of the Redbud Grove where CCK and Weaponmaster face one another.**

Description: Redbud grove orchard that the canals pass through (where CCK slays his mentor).

name: 7 - Wilderness
text:
**The CCK and Lady search The High Sewers with the weaponmaster.**

Action: The weaponmaster suggests three possible locations to search.

Dialogue: As they search, weaponmaster reveals more of his story and his mysterious Lady (a goddess? Madness?)

Description: CCK gets separated from the Weaponmaster here.

name: 8 - Third Transition
text:
**Jump back to present: The CCK reflects on how the weaponmaster's soul has truly broken.**

Reflection: CCK now realizes that the old weaponmaster has a ‘leaky soul’; his body finally broke.

name: 9 - Mysterious Master Foe
text:
**The CCK cuts his way through members of the Long Knife guild.**

Action: Visceral description of CCK cutting his way through the Long Knives, as he escapes from the Channelloft.

Dialogue: One of the Long Knives, dying, reveals why they were after him.

Description: Get CCK from the high sewers to the redbud grove.

name: 10 - Fourth Transition
text:
**Jump back to present: Final taunt from the broken weaponmaster; he won't kill Lady, but he WILL kill CCK.**

 Dialogue: The Weaponmaster, bleeding, says he's ready for another 'correction' to a failed apprentice.

name: 11 - Future and Finale
text:
**Final 3-stage battle between the CCK and the Weaponmaster.**

Fight - part 1: Despite his cracked soul, old weaponmaster will be a formidable foe.

Fight - part 2: Weaponmaster's cracked soul ignites with wrath.

Fight - part 3: Most terrifying form yet; weaponmaster's body splits beyind recognition as human.

Dialogue: Lady thanks knight, and says she has seen her son.

In the Time of Dying...

name: The Court of the Leather Dragon
text:
**The CCK and his Blue find the Stepson, Grauf, leader of the Leather Dragon; they fight.**

Based on Vaughan Williams' Symphony no. 3.

The CCK and Blue Lady go to the Court of the Leather Dragon, knowing that they may die.

They meet the knights, and Grauf the Stepson, who are preparing for a guild war.

Grauf recognizes the Lady, and decides he must remove her stain from his history.

The CCK kills Grauf, but is mortally wounded; he has the Blue Lady resurrect her stepson, so they can be together.

name: 4-Part Act
text:
**The CCK and his Blue find the Stepson, Grauf, leader of the Leather Dragon; they fight.**

Based on Vaughan Williams' Symphony no. 3.

The CCK and Blue Lady go to the Court of the Leather Dragon, knowing that they may die.

They meet the knights, and Grauf the Stepson, who are preparing for a guild war.

Grauf recognizes the Lady, and decides he must remove her stain from his history.

The CCK kills Grauf, but is mortally wounded; he has the Blue Lady resurrect her stepson, so they can be together.

name: Single Chapter
text:
**The CCK and his Blue find the Stepson, Grauf, leader of the Leather Dragon; they fight.**

Based on Vaughan Williams' Symphony no. 3.

The CCK and Blue Lady go to the Court of the Leather Dragon, knowing that they may die.

They meet the knights, and Grauf the Stepson, who are preparing for a guild war.

Grauf recognizes the Lady, and decides he must remove her stain from his history.

The CCK kills Grauf, but is mortally wounded; he has the Blue Lady resurrect her stepson, so they can be together.

name: Setup
text:
**The CCK and Blue Lady go to the Court of the Leather Dragon, knowing that they may die.**

Description: Pastoral Opening- the CCK and Blue Lady are in a gondola, crossing the Hornwater Lake

Dialogue: The Lady explains to the CCK that her son is the leader

Reflection: CCK thinks back to when the Lady thought she loved him.

Description: Exposition on the Court of the Leather Dragon

Dialogue: The Lady brings up her Resurrection Stone.

Reflection: The CCK decides that he MUST install his Lady with her stepson.

name: Conflict
text:
**They meet the knights, and Grauf the Stepson, who are preparing for a guild war.**

Action: CCK and Lady's boat is noticed; they are captured when they land.

Description: Bold text - The Black Wood Garden.

Dialogue: Briefly describe stepson - leader of leather Dragon; mostly through Dialogue.

Description: A horn calls the army to begin assembly; describe their maneuvers.

Dialogue: Just as the stepson is ready with some form of execution, the Lady speaks.

Description: Stepson and the court are silent.

Reflection: The CCK plans to kill the son, and make Lady use Shelley Stone on son.

Dialogue: The Stepson, after a moment of struck reflection, hardens himself.

name: Drive
text:
**Grauf has recognized the Lady, and decides he must remove her stain from his history; he fights the CCK.**

Description: The Afthimmel - the top of the tower that leans nearest to the sky.

Dialogue: One of the Heralds of the Smoke reads out the rules of the duel.

Description: Describe their preparations for battle.

Dialogue: While being examine, Grauf argues that the Lady is his sworn foe, for casting him out.

Action: Fight Part 1

Action: Fight Part 2

Action: Fight Part 3

name: Finale
text:
**The CCK kills Grauf, but is mortally wounded; he has the Blue Lady resurrect her stepson, so they can be together.**

Description: Suddenly transported - the scene changes slightly.

Dialogue: The Lady Speaks to CCK.

Description/Action: CCK must choose to refuse the resurrections.

Dialogue: The Lady bids farewell to CCK; he hears her voice as his vision goes dark.

Reflection?: Final scenes - fast-forwarded in time.

In the Time of Dying...

name: The Alsoburg Siege
text:
**While in the Tunnel village of Alsoburg seeking information about the Stepson, the CCK must help defend against a siege.**

Assault by an army of Wax Golems, led by a necromancer.

name: Adoration of the Foe
text:
**The CCK and Blue Lady meet the besieged Engineer Knights at Pump Station Bella; they escape through the main pipeline.**

1 - The CCK and Blue Lady descend by chain cage to the cavern of Spring-Under-Fjall.

2 - Their lift crashes down at Pumping Station Archer, which is under attack.

3 - A description of the great cavern; Modeled off of skyrim’s Blackreach.

4 - The survivors and the CCK bar the door, and are trapped in the valve/pressure tower.

5 - Golems start breaking through door and steam tower grates.

6 - The flesh-dressed necromancer (lich) approaches from below, heralded by the cold.

7 - The pump station survivors/engineers flee through the main pressure pipe, while the golems overwhelm them.

name: Adoration - Chapter 1
text:
**The CCK and Blue Lady meet the besieged Engineer Knights at Pump Station Bella; they escape through the main pipeline.**

1 - The CCK and Blue Lady descend by chain cage to the cavern of Spring-Under-Fjall.

2 - Their lift crashes down at Pumping Station Archer, which is under attack.

3 - A description of the great cavern; Modeled off of skyrim’s Blackreach.

4 - The survivors and the CCK bar the door, and are trapped in the valve/pressure tower.

5 - Golems start breaking through door and steam tower grates.

6 - The flesh-dressed necromancer (lich) approaches from below, heralded by the cold.

7 - The pump station survivors/engineers flee through the main pressure pipe, while the golems overwhelm them.

name: 1. Title Card
text:
**The CCK and Blue Lady descend by chain cage to the cavern of Spring-Under-Fjall.**

Dialogue: Lady tells why they are there.

Dialogue: The Chain Operator talks while descending into Alsoburg.

Dialogue: Advice given by an old trapper, reflected on by the CCK as he tunes out the chain operator.

name: 2. Arrival of the Foe
text:
**2 - Their lift crashes down at Pumping Station Archer, which is under attack by golems.**

Action: places where the CCK can help fight; golems he faces

Description: List of ‘allies’ in a mercenary/fighting group.

name: 3. Enter the Arena
text:
**A description of the great cavern; Modeled off of skyrim’s Blackreach.**

Description: The cavern; Alsoburg.

name: 4. False Hopes
text:
**4 - The survivors and the CCK bar the door, and are trapped in the valve/pressure tower.**

Dialogue: Greek chorus of Engineer Knights explain situation.

Reflection: The CCK looks back on prior underground foes he has fought.

Dialogue: The Blue Lady asks about her stepson, then asks her CCK to help the engineers.

Description: The Lady is interrupted by pounding on the door; then the engineers remember an exit.

name: 5. Trapped, Battle Dance
text:
**5 - Golems start breaking through door and steam tower grates.**

Action: The CCK has to defend the tower while the knights hammer at the stone.

Dialogue: One of the workers says they are nearly through, then warns of The Crawling Silk.

name: 6. The Master Appears
text:
**6 - The Crawling Silk approaches from below, heralded by the cold.**

Description: The Crawling Silk approaches.

name: 7. The Enemy Triumphs
text:
**7 - The pump station survivors/engineers flee through the sewer, while the golems overwhelm them.**

Action: The CCK has to retrieve his Lady, get to the crew at the drain, and fight off the golems before jumping down himself.

name: The Heroics
text:
8 - Defeated, they make long trek through Spring-Under-Fjall toward Alsoburg.

9 - one of engineers suggests hunting down and assassinating lich; cut head from serpent.

10 - The Silk's golems are already attacking; the survivors are ambushed by bonestuds.

11- after killing the bonestuds, the survivors take possession of the bonestud skins, and disguise themselves as servants.

12 - They follow the procession of the lich and his dead enter pompously into Alsoburg.

13 - the pump station engineers attack/distract; the CCK fights necromancer; they learn Stepson is in Hornwater

8 - Defeated, they make long trek through Spring-Under-Fjall toward Alsoburg.

9 - one of engineers suggests hunting down and assassinating lich; cut head from serpent.

10 - The Silk's golems are already attacking; the survivors are ambushed by bonestuds.

name: 8. Introduction
text:
**8 - Defeated, they make long trek through Spring-Under-Fjall toward Alsoburg.**

Description: Begin with the group emerging from pipe; they can’t see city, but see great sun disc.

Dialogue: One of the knights expositions; gives lore on the cave.

Description/Exposition: The Crawling Silk.

Reflection: The Knight knows what kind of enemy they are up against, and plans to get his lady as far from cave as possible.

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot
text:
**9 - one of engineers suggests hunting down and assassinating lich; cut head from serpent.**

Dialogue: An engineer suggests that killing the silk would stop the attack.

Action: Describe three different Path's the knights will take CCK on, through the cave, chosen at random.

Description: They approach toward Alsoburg.

name: 10. The Second Conflict
text:
**10 - The survivors are ambushed by hides.**

Action: 11 Strokes with the Old Man Below; they are ambushed.

Action: Continue with how CCK can dispatch the hide servants.

name: 11. The Relieved Breath
text:
**11- after killing the hides, the survivors take possession of the bone-studded skins, and disguise themselves as servants.**

Dialogue: One of the survivors suggests donning the skin robes, sneaking in with the lich's forces.

name: 12. The Hero and the Master
text:
**12 - They follow the procession of the lich and his dead enter pompously into Alsoburg.**

Description: the throng of golems and bonestud servants; the procession of The Crawling Silk.

Dialogue: The Chant of the Bonestuds.

Description: Bold text - The Apotheosis.

Description (short): The other engineers have broke off; taken up places on a "Fire Vent" near the gate house.

name: 13. The Finale
text:
**13 - the pump station engineers attack/distract; the CCK fights necromancer; they learn Stepson is in Hornwater.**

Action 1: 3 simples "gesture" combined in various ways.

Action 2: The Silk turns its attention on the CCK; a dream starts the one-on-one in earnest.

Action 3: The Crawling silk summons its golems and wraps itself in a cocoon; the CCK must destroy it before it 'hatches'.

Dialogue: Townsperson reveals Stepson location.

In the Time of Dying...

name: The Duel
text:
**The CCK tries to teach a kindly young knight to win a duel; he fails; the young knight dies.**

The story opens with the CCK locked in a duel before a gathering in Middlemoss.

The CCK and Lady are met by a wayward Apprentice, who wants their help.

The CCK begins training the apprentice at Surveillance.

A list of craft/trade workers is presented, who can aid the apprentice in his Duel.

The CCK and Lady break ties with the Apprentice when he admits he has never met The Lady's stepson.

The CCK and Lady attend the duel, where R the Killer kills The Apprentice.

They bid farewell to the apprentice, who is swallowed by the Numidium.

name: Planets Sole Act
text:

The story opens with the CCK locked in a duel before a gathering in Middlemoss.

name: 1 - Battle (Mars)
text:
**The story opens with the CCK locked in a duel before a gathering in Middlemoss.**

Reflection (short): The CCK appraises his opponent.

Action: CCK is hard-pressed from the start.

Description: Bold Text - Surveillance/Rusting Bones.

Action: CCK has to fight his way back from the maw of the Demon, then slay his foe.

name: 2 - Rest (Venus)
text:
**The CCK and Lady are met by a wayward Apprentice, who wants their help.**

Description: They are recuperating in the park of bare trees in middlemoss...

Dialogue: Apprentice tries to pledge himself to beautiful blue lady.

Action (Short): Apprentice tries to duel the CCK.

Description: They find *list_of_treasures* in the weir.

Dialogue: The apprentice begs forgiveness.

name: 3 - Capers (Mercury)
text:
**The CCK begins training the apprentice at Surveillance.**

Action: Different regiments the CCK can try to teach Skimcalf.

Reflection: CCK observes progress.

Dialogue: Skimcalf gossips with the CCK.

name: 4 - Prestige (Jupiter)
text:
**A list of craft/trade workers is presented, who can aid the apprentice in his Duel.**

Description: One giant 12-part list of craftsmen/people the apprentice can meet to equip himself

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn)
text:
**The CCK and Lady break ties with the Apprentice when he admits he has never met The Lady's stepson.**

Reflection: The CCK notes Skimcalf's heart isn't in the spar this day.

Dialogue: Skimcalf admits he lied about knowing stepson.

Description: Bold Text like Loading Screen - Surveillance's Triumph.

Dialogue: The Lady spurns Skimcalf for his lies, and so must the CCK.

name: 6 - Spellcasting (Uranus)
text:
**The CCK and Lady attend the duel, where R the Killer kills The Apprentice.**

Action: The CCK has to fight to get into a position where he can view the fight.

Description: The opponent turns out to be R; it was a losing proposition from the start.

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune)
text:
**They bid farewell to the apprentice, who is swallowed by the Numidium.**

Dialogue: The apprentice says his farewell to the CCK.

Reflection: Surveillance's jaw comes alive and takes the body of the slain.

Dialogue: R greets the CCK snarkily.

Reflection: Surveillance finishes consuming the body, then warns CCK to flee back to the road.

name: The Tunnel of the Stillbreather
text:
**The Lady and CCK must cross The Cavern of the Stillbreather.**

1 - Introduce the Main Motive of *Metamorphosis.*

2 - Introduce the exterior of the tunnel; a frozen ice cave entrance high in the mountains, they can look back on city.

3 - The CCK and Blue Lady meet Hueless on Kirk (Bold Text: A man-homunculus allied to a Fimbulwolf).

4 - Hueless on Kirk guides them to the entrance of the tunnel, but will not follow them in.

5 - The CCK and Blue Lady find journal pages from the last explorer, who killed The Stillbreather.

6 - The cold begins to disorient/wear them down.

7 - The CCK fights foes that are "himself", but slain in various ways and resurrected.

8 - They find the main tomb of the Stillbreather.

9 - The CCK goes to strike the Stillbreather, but he wakes and fights CCK with a Glowing Fingernail.

10 - When the CCK cuts the egg, it expels a sulfurous gas; the breath of the dead man; his body metamorphses into a regular corpse.

11 - The CCK and Blue Lady reach the other side of the cave; they see an endless see of mountains and canyons before them.
