Even so, this will be a robust battle. The Hearse judders over the craggy epitaph stones at a swift clip, even as you take out its legs. It's clanging bell sinks your skull, into your brain. It will knock you senseless if it rings for too long. 

Additionally, the necromancer atop the box will give a song of deadspeech. This will raise the three stonecats you've already slain - you'll have to cut them down once more.

Keep your Shield raised, your Steel swinging, and stay light on your feet. You will survive.

name: 1 - Battle (Mars) - Blob 5
text:
...

R pulls his morningstar from the skull of the necromancer. He flicks gore from its horns.

"Too easy.

"You and I, fighting together; too easy.

"But I had got the whim on me, and had to see it out. This isn't-

"Ah, enough. Let's go fetch those bosom square coins."

name: 2 - Rest (Venus) - Blob 1
text:
**The Scattered Shrine:**

You emerge from the birdsung, wet undergrowth. The pieces of slate-colored sun, broken like glass by the interlacing branches and leaves, give way to sweeping spotlight rays. They angle from the eastern sky.

The light sweeps over a parapet of ragged and dry stones. A broken clocktower. The remains of the tower - the shingles, the hands of the clock - lie scatter in a yard of waist-high grass.

Behind the scattered shrine, the slate-colored sun gleams against the broad lake of Hornwater. The towers of that near city - unbroken - seem to gently stroke the thin grey clouds.

You step over the fallen iron fence into the yard.

name: 2 - Rest (Venus) - Blob 2
text:
*Why has your Lady not opened the door to greet you? Surely she stood watching at that window there.*

*Or is that pride swirling in your heart? Should such a noble one as her set aside all other thoughts whenever her servant approaches?*

*No. She would come. And you're armor heralds your return.*

*What if some misfortunate struck while you were not at her side. It may have been wrong to leave her with this shrine's necromancer, true to the Road and the seven though he seemed. What business did you have, hunting defilers?*

*But you and she needed a roof, to lay your bedrolls beneath. You served two oaths at once; to your Lady; to the long Road.*

*So you tell yourself.*

*Telling, it is perhaps, that you find yourself so often fighting beside this Killer. Still, his motivation was the coins which the necromancer offered.*

*You strike well together. An easier battle than many you've fought.*

*Yet, the Killer seems sullen all the same.*

name: 2 - Rest (Venus) - Blob 3
text:
The weighty bronze door of the ruined shrine will squeal open at your approach. The threshold manifests Hayz, the necromancer who keeps this ruined shrine at the edge of Hornwater. His  skinny fingers twist about themselves; he hails you in his voice like a hummingbird's buzz.

By helping Hayz's and slaying the robbers on the Road, you have earned the shrine keeper's favor. His shrine will now serve as shelter for you and your Blue Lady.

Additionally, if you ask, the necromancer can give you information on several subjects.

**Your Lady:** "She told me she wished to visit the heart of Hornwater. Three hours past, she took the gondola into the city."

**Necromancy:** "I've summoned only vessels myself. Soulless. We Horner's appease the god-necromancers with simple prayer, and by keeping the shrines."

**The Cult of the Leather Dragon:** "Deadly fighters, I hear. You can see their keep from here; there, through the east window. That is the Island of the Dragon. The only passage is by gondola, through invitation." 

**Namman Tide:** "Five-score men hope to break their bread with a maid this year. Myself? I'm too old for the festival."

**The Sisterhood of the Yellow Smile:** "well, we both know *their* way of worship. You won't catch me anywhere near their grove..."

name: 2 - Rest (Venus) - Blob 4
text:
As soon as he collects his coin from Hayz, R the Killer will shoulder his roadsack and address you.

"I have a job with those yellow sisters myself.

"Maybe they'll have me do the digging on one of their burials. Doesn't matter to me. Their coins feel heavy in the palm.

"Well, I'll hear you if you're ever near again, Steelclad.

"And- luck. To you and your Lady."

name: 2 - Rest (Venus) - Blob 5
text:
R the killer stuffs his pipe, strikes a match, lights, smokes, salutes, and leaves. The bronze door squeals and slams.

You will never see him alive again.

Behind you, Hayz the necromancer climbs creakily down to his knees. He kneels before a put of bone dust. From a pouch at his side, he takes a fire-fork the size of a kitchen ladel. He looks over the fragments in the pit, before selecting a large one, and carefully scooping it into a wooden box with silver trimming. You know he will take the bone to a separate room. He will break it, before returning it to the pit.

There is nothing left for you here. You should charter a gondola into Hornwater.

name: 3 - Capers (Mercury) - Blob 1
text:
For many minutes the only sound is the soft gulping of the lake waves against your slow-moving gondola, and the plash of the helmsman's oar. You sit in your rusted armor, swaying on your bench like a suspended statue.

A vocal babble joins the chorus, as your boat drifts into the Hornwater canals. One city block - a high cube of bricked buildings, packed together like fish, and with their foundation's furry in water-moss - blocks the sun. Somewhere within the block a musician plays a natural horn candenza. The music is slow and ancient.

Your boat plashes onward. You keep your eyes keen for your Lady's blue-black hair and emerald cloak.

You pass by a city garden. Cherry trees shade your passage. Their petals fall like rain around you. One pink petal catches on your visor. You brush it away.

You pass along a sandy embankment. An island. An inner lake of Hornwater runs to your right. To your left, above the bank, a shallow grassy slope rises to an imposing keep with iron walls.

Hornwater is a city of thousands. Sign of your Blue Lady evades you.

name: 3 - Capers (Mercury) - Blob 2
text:
Your boatman plashes through a narrow canal lined in shades stall. One hand of chalky finger-bones reaches from a stall. It clasps your pauldron.

"Life Enriched Water. Witch Batons. Good Golden Oats.

"Have a cure, sir.

"Buy a cure for any ill.

"Buy from a Living Bones. Hehehehe, I've survived death itself. Sir knight, won't-"

You pull your gauntlet free of the peddler.

Your boatman plashes on.

name: 3 - Capers (Mercury) - Blob 3
text:
To find your Lady's whereabouts, you'll need to leave the canals. Pay the gondola-master. Proceed into the landward streets of Hornwater.

You'll likely spend several hours searching. Your oath of silence makes difficult any interrogation of the locals.

You can try to buy the information from the local Emerald House. Strangely, after they understand your request, the priestesses of Lady Horsehair bid you leave. Perhaps you offered offense?

name: 4 - Prestige (Jupiter) - Blob 1
text:
A pageant of royal bearing stops your step.

The common clothed people of Hornwater loiter beside you as a horse-drawn train glides through the street. The citizens have parted for the tapping step of the horse's hooves. A band of five with crashing gongs, also, heralds the regal train.

You see a sigil on banner of the lead carriage. A pearl-hiding clam, with its ridged mouth facing to the right, on a golden fabric.

At the carriage, a lady sits. It is not your Blue Lady; their age is the same, but this woman's hair is shock-white, and she holds it high as she scans the throng in passing. Her eyes are amber and disinterested.

name: 4 - Prestige (Jupiter) - Blob 2
text:
Her amber eyes fix on you as they pass where you stand. She gives a word. The carriage stops.

"You. I have heard of a knight near town, wearing rusted armor, carrying a shield crested with a Pinecone.

"The laundresses say you are a Cracked Cup Knight.

"That is no concern of mine. *I* know your pedigree.

"My coach takes me to my palace at Burning Shells. Sir; would you climb this wagon? Join my procession. The Guildlord wishes for your ear."

\#

name: 4 - Prestige (Jupiter) - Blob 3
text:
**Guilds of Hornwater:**

Hornwater's common folk, they who dwell on the mainland sprawl of town, pledge allegiance to one of the ruling powers among the islands and canals. Chief among these powers are three guilds: Burning Shells, The Guild of Long Knives, and The Builder's Guild.

Each guild has its Guildlord and Guildlady. The Guildlord of Burning Shells is the richest man in Hornwater. His guild owns most of the vinyards on the fertile land by Hornwater lake.

Though the Guildlord has not the strongest militia, The Palace of Burning Shells is an imposing prospect. Perched alone on its island, girt by jagged cliffs, with one long, narrow stone bridge at the end of which sits a heavy iron portcullis, the Palace perches like the nest of some prehistorical flying whale. Stacks of weathered limestone towers stare out from ten thousand window-eyes, gleaming in the sun. At highest point, a cathedral roof caps the monolith.

\#

name: 4 - Prestige (Jupiter) - Blob 4
text:
*Why have this Guildlord and Guildlady beckoned to this rich estate? They may want for one skilled with his Steel.*

*They would look elsewhere, certainly, before they looked to you.*

*This guild may know where your own Lady has vanished to. That must be your driving thought.*

name: 4 - Prestige (Jupiter) - Blob 5
text:
**The Singing Reception:**

A steward in velvet doublet and high black boots leads you down a long and tall chamber. The floors are polished tile. The walls are shelving stone, looking almost like a dried bed of coral. Smoke drifts up the walls from two long troughs on either side of the room, pooling in the shelving stone.

An organ plays. You see its pipe stretching like tree branches from the northeastern corner of the chamber. They snake across the ceiling. The pipes hum a merry jingle.

At the far end there is a crescent moon table. It is made of gritty stone. Around it are twelve stone stools. In two, there sit the Guildlord and Guildlady, wearing matching robes of silver and green.

name: 4 - Prestige (Jupiter) - Blob 6
text:
The Guildlord looks at you, his hand hiding his mouth.

"I've heard of a knight in town. One who keeps his oaths.

"Be not anxious, sir knight. Your silence does us both honor.

"I shall speak shortly. Our guards, our private army, have no skill in their arms. My weaponsmaster, decent man - he fell to the Leather dragon this summer.

"I need a new master. I have heard you guard a Lady. Honorable. Bring her. Let me see her bearing. There may be space for you at Burning Shells. Acceptable, no?"

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn) - Blob 1
text:
By the time you return across the bridge from Burning Shells, dusk is setting. No sooner have you stepped down from the carriage than a courier-woman in sleek leather runs up to you. She shoves a letter into your gauntlet, then sprints away.

The letter is sealed in a square stamp of yellow wax, with a sigil like an apple.

*Knight who bears the Pinecone Crest Shield,*

*This letter gives invitation. Attend the service of your Lady between the hours of Midnight tonight and Midday tomorrow. She has begged and been granted burial at Yellow Smile Priory. Visit her epitaph here, that you may bear witness to her blessed interment.*

*You may find the priory on the terminal Road of Graves, where it winds through the Stallion Gate, on the norwestern corner of Hornwater wall.*

*We shall be be pleased to accomodate your attendance,*

*The Sisterhood of the Yellow Smile.*

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn) - Blob 2
text:
The handwriting of the letter shrinks as it falls from your gauntleted hand. The mud between your sabatons swallows the paper.

*Can these be lies? Your Blue Lady would never turn to The Sisterhood.*

*They have kidnapped her. The blame for this evil is your own. You knew you should have distrusted-*

*No. Search within your mind. The Sisterhood of the Yellow Smile keeps an upright mile.*

*How can your Lady have chosen that fate? To be interred.*

*Alive.*

*She is alive. There is still time to stop her life from turning over. You know your road. To the priory, before the burial.*

*But... her interment is vouchsafed. She has chosen burial under a Sisterhood stone, her place on the road is incontrovertible.*

*You must find a loophole.*

\#

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn) - Blob 3
text:
There are two devoted fraternities and two unblemished sororities in The Time of Dying.

**The Brotherhood of Crimson:** The brothers excoriate themselves in their devotion to The Scratcher. They desire one end only; to be falsely buried under the road, and serve their god in death forever as Mailed Flayers.

**The Sisterhood of Marblevale:** They renounce love, wealth, and name. Their end is under unscraped stones, on a smoothly-surfaced mile of The Road.

**The Brotherhood of the Hoof:** This cloister is settled not far from your former Lord's keep, beside The Lake of Tomatoes. It is devoted to Gallbladder. The brothers eat the dead.

**The Sisterhood of the Yellow Smile:** They bury the living. In such a way is the soul vouchsafed from the mutterings of necromancy.

\#

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn) - Blob 4
text:
The quickest path to reach the Yellow Smile priory, before your Lady's burial, is through the Vekelhause marina. This burg lies in the heart of the town. It is a forsaken waterfront; unkept, rainrotten homes; crumbling, waterswallowed docks.

You'll have to bury concern for your Lady. Beneath the water's surface, three Gasps patrol the district. If they notice you they will rise with a frosted *gasp* from the surface, and attach you with a barrage of arrows. Use your shield to your advantage against them.

When you reach the other of the marina, you'll find yourself standing on The Road. The priory lies only yards away, swallowing The Road under its gate.

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn) - Blob 5
text:
Though you arrive before midnight, your hopes are dashed when you meet the aged prioress.

"See your Lady? Certainly not, sir knight.

"Not until she is buried. Then you may look on her stone.

"Cease your appeals.

"Your Blue Lady had given up on finding that stepson whom you sought. She chose an ending of redemption. Be glad in your heart. Now, go."

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn) - Blob 6
text:
Something in the prioress's speech is potent with witchery. Though you would not heed them, your legs carry from the abbey all the same.

As you go, you pass a hall of sculpted glass. You see the fate of any who take gold from the priory of the Yellow Smile.

**The Thief:** Made of many small green pieces. Hunched. His forever-stilled hand reaching for a purse.

**The Beggar:** Kneeling, her hands form a bowl, her ruby-glass eyes and lips shaped in gratitude.

**The Killer:** It is R the Killer. His sculpture is fresh, gleaming, rounded and smoothed like melted wax. One hand raises his morningstar, no longer horned metal, merely dull red glass.

name: 6 - Spellcasting (Uranus) - Blob 1
text:
Four doors clang shut as you exit the priory. The moon in high and bright in the velvet sky. About you, the crickets have begun to chirr.

You must now choose between two roads.

**Accept your Lady's End:** Keeping your oath to the road, you let your Lady perish underground. You may serve the guild at Burning Shells as a master of arms.

**Break The Road's Tenets:** You choose to turn your will and your Steel against the gods-blessed Sisterhood.

\#

name: 6 - Spellcasting (Uranus) - Blob 2
text:
**The Veilbend:**

Midnight has come and gone when you finish your long trek. You arrive at the opposite entrance to the priory. Marching through an open gate, you step onto the Sisterhood's covered mile of Road.

The ceiling above you is brightly painted. The fresco shows seven kings with golden crowns and silver shields. They kneel, their heads bowed, their shields raised. They offer kingly fealty to a queenly skeleton in a translucent, shining dress. She sits on a throne of golden glass.

The space is lit by a thousand-and-one torches. Your steel boots *tap, tap, tap* over well-swept, well-mortared onyx stones. Each stone is deeply-carved with the epitaph of the cadaver beneath it. You believed, once, that you would have such an epitaph yourself once. Not so deeply-carved, perhaps; not on such a stone of onyx - but, on The Road.

Stone fountains trickle to either side as you march steadily along.

name: 6 - Spellcasting (Uranus) - Blob 3
text:
The sisters stand a quarter-mile down the stretch. When she notices your coming, and sees the shine your Polished Steel Longsword, the ancient prioress raises her voice.

"The gods will curse your soul forever.

"Unless you mean to bury the point of that weapon in the soft earth here, and kneel before your lady's grave, then sheath your cutting edge.

"Stop, knight.

"You will doom yourself. You will doom your Lady. The seven will forsake her!"

name: 6 - Spellcasting (Uranus) - Blob 4
text:
The sisters will raise small kitchen knives or sweeping-brooms against you. But the sisters are not warriors.

You will find their tissue easy purchase for your cutting edge.

Unfortunately, the priory is not without its defenders. As soon as you have slain three Sisters of the Yellow Smile, the rest will flee. Only a second passes before you hear heavy *tinking* sounds approaching, like hammers striking in a giant glass piano.

You'll need to defeat four different glass illusions.

**Empty Robe Man:** You cannot catch his eyes, nor should you want to. His legs are fragile, though hard to hit.

**Crying Child at the end of the Hall:** This one never seems to get any closer, until you look away.

**Body Faker:** No sooner have you shattered this church-window-figure, than it reassembles and attacks again. Must be shattered thirteen times.

**Veilface:** Moves like a jagged fog cloud; slow and cutting. Let your shield do the work here.

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune) - Blob 1
text:
The last of the glass illusions finally shatters into pure silence. You stand alone on the Road of onyx Graves.

You can find your Lady' easily enough. It is clean, unmarked by either your own blood, or that of the sisters. The mortar is fresh. You can pry the stone free easily with your crimson-edged Steel.

To dig with your weapon would be slow and dishonorable. Morover, you know the time is short. The sisterhood left no airholes.

If you search behind the nearest stone fountain, you will find a set of shovels in a leather wrapping. Avoid looking into the fountain pool as you pass; like The Sisterhood's glass, it reflects a deadly illusion.

With a shovel, and strength of arm, the earth over your Lady's coffin is easily moved.

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune) - Blob 2
text:
Your Blue Lady, clad in golden funeral-robe, throws her pales arms around you in an embrace. Her black hair drapes your shoulder, her breast heaves with quiet sobs.

"Oh my Cracked Cup Knight. Thank you.

"I lost belief that we would ever find that boy. The child of my Lord's first wife. I thought by pledge to the sisterhood, my life's misdeed's might be swept away.

"I changed my mind. The earth thudding onto the lid-

"Oh my knight, these bodies. What has my selfish folly cost you?"

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune) - Blob 3
text:
*You seven necromancers who listen for souls - long his this one turned its thoughts from thee, in deference. But this soul is no longer beholden to examine only itself.*

*The oath is broken.*

*This soul would beg no mercy. It has no future under the safeguard of an epitaph. It  hope that the necromancer who catches it might, at least, abuse soul and body to fighting purpose.*

*...*

*Your choice was right. Such are the words, anyway, by which you try to warm your cold bones.*

*Your Lady says she changed her mind. Should that not be enough?*

*But the grave-dirt under a stone of The terminal Road, it is still soiling your hands.*

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune) - Blob 4
text:
You should lead your Blue Lady swiftly from the Priory. Tarry, and sisters, and other glass illusions; they may attack.

You emerge from the easternly gate. Dawn has begun to rise. You notice the morning sun to be strangely-hued. There is a tinge of lilac in the usual slate-blue. The upper arch has just risen over a distant black wood. You see it coloring the belltower of The Scattered Shrine, flushing the broken stone.

If you look back, you will find that all one-thousand-and-one torches in The Veilbend have ceased burning. The covered onyx roadstones are hidden from sight. The priory waits in steady silence, watching to see if you will reenter.

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune) - Blob 5
text:
You feel a motion of your Lady's arm.

"My Cracked Cup Knight. You-

"You have thrown much away. For my sake alone. Will you not say why? My Lord was worthy of your loyalty. He is gone. A ghost.

"Will you remove your helmet? Meet my eye? Will you speak?

"No. Do not. You have sacrificed enough. Only lead me."

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune) - Blob 6
text:
*You seven; does destination endure where your sight does not reach? Is there land in this Time of Dying from which you keep your eyes and ears?*

*Who is the greatest of your number in Hornwater? The priest said that the raising of hte dead was not so much in practice here. Perhaps then, Hornwater will suffer one breaker of oaths.*

*But, there is no place for these two souls with the Guild at Burning Shells.*

*You seven; this Lady took no part in the wrong. This was the soul that broke her grave. Please-*

*Perhaps, for tonight, these souls may find shelter at the priest's shrine.*

*Perhaps these bodies may.*

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune) - Blob 7
text: In the Time of Dying lies a Road of Graves.

name: 1 - Prelude - Blob 1
text:
"Now we once more stand with weapons drawn, old master, old student, tense for a final spar.

"You are another among many students come to re-honor this wise sword. This wise, wise sword. This wise sword will teach you like all others.

"My cracked cup of an apprentice. What a foolish, broken knight you make. Totally broken. Totally broken.

"Every Kingsday you once dazzled the eyes of my Lord and Lady Fathmail. My Lord Fathmail... Now this sword will cut your disgusting soul from the chronicle."

name: 1 - Prelude - Blob 2
text:
Your old Weaponmaster's eyes ratify the lunacy of his polished, cracking voice. Beneath the single, oiled, sharp, grey eyebrow, placed too near his ear on the left side of his face, a bloodshot yellow cat's eye twitches. The right half of his face is expressionless as the face of a Holstein, with a Holstein's eye, and a Holstein's short, black sideburn.

Where your platemail rusts, his glows. It is silver-gilt and scored; fine and tested. Your Weaponmaster wears it with squared shoulders. In his left arm, he holds a shield. It's crest is the old Fathmail Worm-over-Thunderhead.

His sword is sharp and levered your way.

name: 1 - Prelude - Blob 3
text:
*This is not your master. This is not the knight who taught you the gift of the sword. The gift of honorable oath. This is a doppelganger.*

*You seven great necromancers; how has this come to be?*

*You yourself should have seen this man's secret threat. Your Lady saw; now she pays for your blindness.*

*You wished to believe in this false master.*

*And, you let distraction turn your eye. Those Long Knives, they drew the edge of your Steel...*

\#

name: 2 - Opening Conflict - Blob 1
text:
The narrow stone lane beside the Canal Raydwurst seemed almost peaceful when you led your Blue Lady in. The windows dotting the brick homes on your right lay curtained, closed, without reflection in the very-early morn. On your left only a half-dozen small gondolas bobbed quietly in the still water; two city water trappers alone stood on a gondola, loading nets.

They were assassins.

The two men now lie dead in the lane. Your sword has cut them down, and two more besides. You see three caped bravos ahead of you, blocking the exit. There are more behind.

Your present action should be to press your Lady under the cover of a wooden stair. Both teams have crossbows, so keep your Pinecone Crest Shield raised. You should be able to provide your lady full cover between yourself and the stair.

If you try to slay your way to freedom, you will fail. No matter how many killers you lay out on the lane, more follow. You have two options for getting away.

**Comandeer a Gondola:** You'll have to lure out the shots from the two crossbows coming up behind. Quickly usher your Lady into the craft with with red-painted gunnels. This is the smallest and swiftest vessel. Use your sword to cut the line. Have your lady paddle out to the water, then down the canal. You'll need to keep your shield raised for incoming bolts.

**Climb the Wooden Stair:** Draw the fire from the rear group again, but this time quickly mount the creaking stairs to the second-floor balcony of the canalside home. You'll have to break down the door. This path leads to a short battle with a paper ghost (the house is abandoned and labyrinthine).

\#

name: 2 - Opening Conflict - Blob 2
text: You find yourself and your Lady lost. Hornwater's tall loom-towers hum with their heavy treadles all around you. The canal-water ripples with the sycopated rhythm. Workers drag huge skeins of colored cloth onto barges.

name: 2 - Opening Conflict - Blob 3
text:
Your lady speaks.

"We seem to have lost them.

"I saw the cutting blade one held; a long stiletto made of blue metal.

"It is the guild of Long Knives.

"Who has sent the hunters, my knight? Do the knives seek your flesh, or my own?"

name: 3 - Mentor - Blob 1
text:
**A Familiar Face:**

One man draws a flick of your eyes.

An armored, armed knight walks alone amidst a drifting stream of commoners and laborers. He wears an odd face, distorted and old. You have never seen it before.

Yet the strange face somehow mirrors a different reflection, seen only by the eye in your mind.

The city of Hornwater disintegrates.

You are standing, panting within a walled courtyard. The air is heavy with the tang of oil and lilac. The tiled garden of hanging plants is one you know well; it is the inner court of Clan Fathmail. You are knight-apprentice of the Lord of the house.

Your teacher stands before you.

His sword *shinks* into its scabbard. His tight-lipped, imperial, passive expression wrinkles with dissaproval.

You are not good enough.

The memory is passing brief. Hornwater, and that stranger's face which makes a strange reflection, reappear before you.

When you glance your Lady, you see that she recognizes the man too.

\#

name: 4 - First Transition - Blob 1
text:
You focus your attention on the sword. This doppelganger weaponmaster holds its point over the neck of your Blue Lady. He has tied her to a corpse on the ground.

You lock eyes with your Lady, and see the sorrow in hers. You have not drawn your own sharp Steel; you dare not move.

The body on the ground is one of four. Mebers of the Long Knives. All dead cut throats. Precise blows.

The false knight's sword is rimmed with blood. He has slain them. You watch a drop slide down, and fall on your Lady's pale neck.

\#

name: 5 - New Lover - Blob 1
text:
You and your Blue Lady have stood too still for too long in the bustle of the weaving quarter. The man with the strange faces notices you. That face writhes with mometary surprise; then, he storms over to you.

"What? What?! Has The Scratcher's Lash flayed your tongues to silence?

"I see. That emblem carved into your breatplate. The vow of little talk.

"It matters nothing. Matters nothing. Yes, I am your old master. Even had I not seen that shining, blue-black hair of this Lady, I would have know you by your longsword, my old apprentice.

"Is blood, however, that I seen on your pauldron? Is that sweat on your Lady's collar? Your looks want for noble decorum..."

name: 5 - New Lover - Blob 2
text:
*This stranger flourishes all the curt speech of your old weaponmaster. You remember that well, at least.*

*Once you heard such speech before the rising of the sun, each morning. You remember the way it echoed, in that windowed eastern wing, in the hallway with the high stone ceilings. It seemed to shake the dust from the rafters, you thought.*

*You suffered many a cut and blacked skin, from the blunts of that same sword worn by this stranger.*

name: 5 - New Lover - Blob 3
text:
Your Blue Lady stands on her toes and whispers at the neck of your helmet.

"I ill-like this armored and armed one, my cracked cup knight.

"I do not know that we should trust him with our trouble, with The Long Knives. His arrival comes too conveniently on the heels of your late battle.

"He has the right memories. He has a like voice. But, the face is too-much changed.

"My knight, I did not mean that. Of course his body may have been stitched - his soul summon back - more than once."

name: 5 - New Lover - Blob 4
text:
You press your Blue Lady on through the moving crowds. The armed man follows you. Soon the heavy hum of the loom-towers fades.

You can choose not tell this familiar stranger about the recent attack. Even if you withhold it, he will guess that The Long Knives are after you.

You notice the man's disconfigured face smoothing as the moments pass. His wroth seems softened.

After a few minutes walking, the man will suddenly offer to give you shelter. He will use one of two phrased: "from whatever troubles your step," or "from the Long Knives."

The man gives one condition; you must help him to recover his own sworn Lady. She has been kidnapped.

\#

name: 6 - Second Transition - Blob 1
text:
A rogue wind wanders through Hornwater. It soughs in the limbs of the redbuds, in the park, where you face the doppelganger.

Petals drop from the branches. One curtain of ten-thousand rolling petals obscures your views of each other.

On the wind is a distant mourning horn. Someone has died, in Hornwater.

The pink petal curtain falls. The weaponmaster's sword remains poised over your Lady's throat.

You yourself will have stolen the moment, and drawn your Steel.

\#

name: 7 - Wilderness - Blob 1
text:
The man will lead you to three different locations in the search for his mysterious lady. Keep your Steel ready; there is a chance you will be ambushed by Long Knives.

You'll have to find the clue hidden in each of the three locations. The order in which the man visits them is always the same.

**The Old Arena Arcade:** The clue here lies in the riddle, carven into the stone on the flaking mural, where the east sun shines through a crack in the wall. The answer to the riddle is 'crows' feet'.

**The Skylit Hall with Hanging Vines:** Listen to the trilling of the birds in the skylights. There song has a rhythm; bum buuuum, bum bum bumbum.

**The Pond House:** Simpley search the pool rooms for some time. After a few minutes, the man will stare fixedly at his expression in a pool, before twisting a knowing smile upon you.

You are unsure of the significance of each clue, but the man's face wriggles at each revelation. When you've found all three clues, he suggests going to The Channelloft.

name: 7 - Wilderness - Blob 2
text:
As you wander through Hornwater, the armed man will speak more freely in the voice of your old master. Listen; you may pick up some lore.

"Possibly it is these Long Knives that have taken my Lady. A guild of tyros. They have metal, and the muscles to swing it. But they have no oaths. No convictions.

"My lady. She'll have been taken for her beautiful spirit. My Lady. My Lady. She IS strength-of-purpose.

"That strike you took back there, old apprentice; something was wrong in it.

"Listen. Do you here that singing water? We are near the Channelloft of Hornwater. It's where the lost go."

name: 7 - Wilderness - Blob 3
text:
**The Channelloft:**

The cube of discolored, mossy bricks, slit windows, and angular terraces and rooflines rises in zigging and zagging architecture, high over the surrounding city. Water trickles from one-hundred-and-seven elevated drains on each of the four sides.

Inside, The Channelloft is a warren of wastewater tunnels and cavity dwellings. Madmen, gutterchild killers, and few honest men live here.

You may try to keep your Lady close, but it is no use. Sometime, you will turn your eye.

When you look back, the stranger and your Lady are gone.

\#

name: 8 - Third Transition - Blob 1
text:
*Or perhaps this is your old weaponmaster's soul, but this soul that you knew has begun to leak. You yourself know what struggle there is in the spirit, when ones body is broken and stitched back together.*

*This man holds his sword over the throat of your sworn Lady. That Lady is she to whom he first gave your service. Could he let the evil blood of an assassin fall on her skin?*

*Can a soul crack thus?*

*This man rages. In rage he has slain these other killers. Will the buds of these trees bloom redder next year, for having drunk the blood of Long Knives? Will their souls crack, nourished by killers?*

*Why do you lose focus? You must think of an opening for your Lady. Do not lay down your Steel as you did for the harridan. Long Knives have died; by your Steel or his, no matter.*

\#

name: 9 - Mysterious Master Foe - Blob 1
text:
Reverberations of pounding, booted footsteps crash on your ears, ricocheting on the gurgling tunnels, surrounding you in an orchestra of din.

You'll have to cut your way through waves of Long Knives. They seem to have followed your trail.

There are a few factors you should exploit to your gain. The Long Knives haven't been able to perfectly coordinate their convergence. They'll attack in groups of three. Try to kill each group before the next reaches you. The Long Knife members carry thin stillettos and wear painted breastplates over padded coats. Their 'Long Stilettos' are good at ducking through creases in your armor; if you keep moving and keep your Pinecone Crest Shield up, you can avoid all but the rapping on your armor. For your part, aim your Steel at their limbs. Your blows don't need to kill, only clear a path to your Lady.

While fighting, keep moving through The Channelloft. You'll want to riposte, slice, clang and clash your way toward the cry you hear echoed of a local statue seller: "Happy for sale! Scared for sale! Faces in Stone!" His shop is near an exit.

name: 9 - Mysterious Master Foe - Blob 2
text:
Rounding a sewer-bend, flicking blood from your Steel, you see slate-colored daylight ahead. Leaning against the rounded wall of long-dried slime is one of the Long Knives. He coughs blood as you approach.

"You- you were supposed to have no friends in town. *Cough. Cough.*

"One cracked knight. One pale, skinny woman. Kill her before she finds- The Sisterhood said- easy- easy sheathes for knives.

"*Cough. Cough. CoughCoughCough.*

"Where'd you hire the wild man? So- so wrathful. Such a- a blow he- he- deals...."

name: 9 - Mysterious Master Foe - Blob 3
text:
Stepping out into the Hornwater daylight, you shield your eyes.

A commotion across the canal; young children, running; an armored figure. With an iron arm, the man drags your Lady through an arch formed by two redbud trees - a town park.

You'll need to cut through a few more Long Knives, but their numbers are thinner. You find another body as you cross the canal bridge. Another dead Knife, slain with a brutal, cleaving blow to the collarbone.

Even had you not seen him, a red trail leads to the grove. Cut down one last assassin, and enter.

name: 10 - Fourth Transition - Blob 1
text:
Your old master raises his sword from your Lady's neck. He brings it into a forward guard.

"I have worn myself down more than I wished. Running across Hornwater. Slaying your black stalkers. They have managed a small cut on my arm, you see; but I am still fair to cross swords.

"My lady; she IS the spirit of Honor. My Honor. I could not find the beautiful place for her.

"This garden is a beautiful place for you. You will look correct here. I shall please my lady, laying you here.

"This harlot on the ground means nothing. She is no spirit of broken oaths. Broken oaths. Broken oaths.

"The reunion is over, my old student. It is time to spar."

name: 11 - Future and Finale - Blob 1
text:
This battle will test both your strength and your strategy. Over many resurrections, your master's body has soaked in necromancy. He hardly bleeds from even the deepest cuts. A severed tendon or a broken arm will slow him not at all.

At the same time, your old master recognizes that (cracked body or not) you have not lost your own Steel-skill. He will not underestimate you.

In the first act of the fight, the Weaponmaster will circle freely around the grove. You can stun and temporarily slow him with ringing blows upon his breastplate. Eventually, he will try to back up against the canal with an onslaught of quick slashes. Use the redbuds to create distance from this barrage.

name: 11 - Future and Finale - Blob 2
text:
After you've landed a few blows, your old Weaponmaster's cracked soul will ignite with wrath. Liquid fire seeps from each misshapen eye, and beads like burning sweat on his skin. The necromancy that has sewn his soul together is collapsing in upon itself.

In this state your old master's arm falls with thrice the prior force. Your shield has hope against such an arm. The first time to try to block his sword, your Pinecone Crest Shield will splinter apart.

Your only hope is to use your environment. Retreat from this burning soul. Goad the man into swinging at one of the redbuds. Each time he does, his sword will become temporarily lodged in the wood, allowing you to get in a strike or two.

name: 11 - Future and Finale - Blob 3
text:
If you can land a cut on his skin, the disintegrating man's body will completely split apart. No longer human, this thing is a Shambler; a mass of living tissue-parts and dying spirit-matter.

This creature will hurl pieces of its broken steel armor at you. The missiles can easily puncture your own rusted suit; dodge them. Use the roots of the grove to your advantage. Get the creature to chase you over the terrain. When it finally trips, rush in. End this man's torment with a clean chop of your Polished Steel.

Once your former master is beheaded, the wounded soul will burn out of the body. All that is left behind is burned clothing, tissue, and armor; and a lump of slag that was his sword.

name: 11 - Future and Finale - Blob 4
text:
Your Lady offers her thanks as you untie her from the corpse.

"My Knight, I am sorry that this was forced on you.

"I remember the old man who brought you to my service...

"But, My Knight, I have learned something. I have seen *him*.

"My relation.

"My stepson."

name: 11 - Future and Finale - Blob 5
text: In The Time of Dying lies a Road of Graves.

name: Setup - Blob 1
text:
Where lies The Road of Graves? Nowhere in the scope of your sight. For miles around there is only the wide wrinkled, rippling, periwinkle-blue of the Hornwater lake; the small wooden tubs of other gondolas; in the distances, the skirting of wheat hills, and Hornwater Town.

You press the paddle of your gondola through the water. Your craft floats steadily at a island. There is a short white beach. Then, terraces, grass yards and gardens, each terrace raised on walls of large mossy bricks. A fortress - six sides, ten thousand windows, crowned in six ever-burning bonfires - guards the hilltop.

\#

The fort's gargoyles are stone basalisks. They are lifelike, for they once lived. They were forced to gaze on another by the Leather Dragon; the guild that holds court within the six high walls.

\#

name: Setup - Blob 2
text:
From beneath her hood at the front of your gondola, you hear your Blue Lady's wood-thrush voice.

"When that singing skeleton told me that the child, which I had driven from my Lord's house; that he might live: it seemed justice.

"I begged that The Scratcher and The Red King keep their eyes turned from him, and let him have his peace and happiness.

"I only later thought to meet him. Even to look at the face of someone I once knew; that would be more than this ruined Lady deserves.

"But never did it strike me to think that he would be master of a clan like The Leather Dragon."

name: Setup - Blob 3
text:
*This noble being once believed that she loved you. Back in the grotto. You understood her mistake then; she had not seen your face. She-*

*Why do debase her integrity with such a thought? Why even remember it now?*

*You may, at least, congratulate yourself on one account. You have seen your Blue Lady to her stepchild. You have brought her to the one she looked for. In this, you did your duty.*

*The Wiege Family is, as well, far history. You brought Your Lady free from the poison and blades.*

\#

name: Setup - Blob 4
text:
The guildmembers of The Leather Dragon pride themselves on woundless skill. Each apprentice learns the warrior's skills for four years, then trains under a knight for four years more. At the end of their training, each apprentice is made into one of four kinds of guild Knight:

**Flail Knight:** Fights with a long, spiked ball at the end of a chain, plus a shield. It takes time for them to wind up a strike, but the unusual angle of attack make these knights a challenge to ordinary swordsmen.

**Glimmer Knight:** Their shields are mirrors, used to dazzle and faze their foes.

**Laminar Knight:** These warriors wear armor of plated, layered, tough black leather, with ridges and studs designed to give them the aspect of their guild's namesake.

**Stoneraker Knight:** Instead of sword and shield, these knights carry two-handed halberds. The pommel of each halberd is an hourglass, designed such that the grains of white sand inside may only pass from the top to the bottom at a rate of one per day. If a Stoneraker knights goes unwounded for all eleven-hundred days, his halberd is retired into the guild's sacred relic hall.

\#

name: Setup - Blob 5
text:
"My Knight, one word I have before we reach this white beach, and the guild Castle above it.

"That trinket we found in the mines; do you still carry the Shelley Stone?

"If- If my stepson should receive me poorly- If I should die, use not the stone on my body.

"My knight, I am not unhappy with all you've done.

"I think only my daughter's ghost. To pass on would be peace, I think."

name: Setup - Blob 6
text:
*She sees more clearly than you, the danger in those dark stone walls. Already, they shade the slate-colored sun from your gondola. Why did you think you had brought her beyond danger?*

*You are a servant. If there is danger ahead, it is forever your duty to face it.*

*In one thing, your Blue Lady's sight is shortened. For her to die, and you to live; impossible.*

*You must install her with her foster child. You must establish noble standing for her. The cost to yourself is past mattering.*

name: Conflict - Blob 1
text:
Your craft is noticed at once as you draw near the shore. Your prow has just touched the sand, and you have just handed your Blue Lady onto the thin ivory beachhead, when the sound of syncopated, stomping boots echoes round the rocks.

You can try to resist capture, but it is futile. These are trained warriors, not furtive killers or thoughtless dead. Your Polished Steel Longsword may slide through the armor of one, perhaps two guild knights. Then you will be cut down and your Lady will be taken regardless.

The best course is: throw down your Steel on the sand (don't sheath it), but keep your Pinecone Crest Shield up. The guild will have sent arbalests, and it's likely you'll need to a catch a bolt or three before they see you've surrendered.

It might be tempting to run. Don't. The only way to the end of your road is to go willingly.

\#

name: Conflict - Blob 2
text:
Three knight only are your escort through the hot, and candlelit corridors of the castle. You feel as if you have stepped into a monastery modeled on The Sickening Cradle. This feels like a place for men to sleep. The only sounds are your footsteps, the crackle of the torches, and from some distant quarter, a plainchant of mixed growling and hissing voices.

**Swidden of the First Wyrm:**

Soldiers move in syncopated clusters of twenty over the broad field of short, sage-colored grass. They are like wheeling flocks of blackbirds in the air. You recognize that these are not training maneuvers. This is clang and stump of three-hundred armor knights making ready to kill.

Your escorts bring you down from a rampart, into a grove of fourteen tall, black tree stumps. Each stump was sewn, then burnt, by Smooth Sabel, the first Grand Wyrm of the Leather Dragon.

name: Conflict - Blob 3
text:
One man; he that wears the lamellar coat held together by burnt orange stitching, and a helmet like a golden manta ray, with its wings folded down over the sides of his head; this man has stood upon a stage, watching the maneuvers. When he sees you, he and his four attendant knights step down.

"The bells of Hornwater wait tensed for the ringing of the dusk. What spy precedes that hour?

"Poorly mannered, evidently.

"My name is Grauf. I am the earthquake breath. I am the shining orb. I am the Great Wyrm. This; my army. These; my talons. Soon they will drag ruts through the bodies of the other guilds. Your spying is for naught.

"Or is this some feint? I have never scratched a scale, have never never marred my hide on another sword, have never fallen for distraction."

name: Conflict - Blob 4
text:
Three blasts from a towering horn suspend the Great Wyrm's address.

The maneuvering scores of syncopated marching soldiers; the dozen-squads of stmaping horsemen; the rows of arbalests; all turn with uncanny synchronicity towards a wide brick track that run in a straight line through the Swidden. As each group moves up to take its place along a specific quarter of the road, they fall still. You measure their strength at five-hundred foot knights, three-hundred horse knights, and threer-hundred men with arbalests. Most of the soldiers are Laminar Knights; armored as their guil'ds namesake.

You are reminded of a painting you once saw. A man with the head of a crow, dressed in mottled robes, watches as the slate-colored usnlight streams in through a high windows in his sepulchre, and shines over dozens of subservient, mummified kamados.

\#

**Dragon Eye Longsword:** This fabulous weapon's hilt is the shape of a serpent, with its fanged wrapped around a gleaming opal. It was made by a nameless necromancer, who is said to have filled it with the hatred borne by one of the last degenerated dragon against mankind. The Great Wyrm of The Leather Dragon is its bearer.

\#

name: Conflict - Blob 5
text:
As Grauf steps forward to address his soldiers, your Lady speaks. It starts as a whisper for you alone.

"Why did my heart wish for this reunion? For what was all its yearning?

"I could have looked for no happy welcome. Still I desired this, and you brought me in faithful silence. You have persevered your oath to me above all others, my Cracked Cup Knight.

"No matter our fates, my knight, know this in your own heart; I love you.

\*Aloud\* "Sir Grauf, we are not spies. I am the Lady Odette. You knew me, once. My house, and yours, was the Verne Family. It is no more. I, this knight my protector, and you your father's son, are all that remains."

name: Conflict - Blob 6
text:
The memory of that painting flashes in your mind again. It seems as if the knights, indeed the entire scene before your eyes, is a painting.

Only one thin trail of smoke breaks the illusion. It rises from the pipe of a tactician. He stands beside Great Wyrm Grauf, staring with one good eye at your lady. The shield planted at his feet reflects your own image; stripped of your Polished Steel and Pinecone Crest Shield, armor rusting.

The reflection moves, as the tactician pulls he shield up to his arm. He draws his own sword. He tells the Great Wyrm that you and your Lady must be beheaded, and the march to war begun.

name: Conflict - Blob 7
text:
*You see that this foster son agrees with his advisor. He pulls there at the corner of his mustache, as if in contemplation. But his eyes have decided your Lady's fate.*

*He is a knight. A commander. The Road demands an oath from him. Would he incur the Red King's Curse through the slaying of the swordless?*

*He will challenge your Lady. He will challenge you, her champion.*

*You must win. For her. But if you slay him, you slay her last relative. You kill her only link to this Roadbound order. How will you win when the two ways ahead-*

name: Conflict - Blob 8
text:
Grauf's palm raises, breaking your thoughts, checking his subordinate's step toward your Blue Lady.

"No, do not behead these ones. Whether hers is Dragon's Truth or the Erl's Lies, our army would have its first footsteps painted in crimson dishonor, if we acted so.

"Men would whisper that we are scarless from the choice of our foes, not to speed and power of our arms.

"You Old Wyrms, take our knights across the bridge. Begin the clearing by flame of our foes. You know our warplan.

"I myself will give these two foreign agents the honors of address. Take them to the top of the Tower Afthimmel."

name: Drive - Blob 1
text:
**The Afthimmel:**

This wide tower at the top of the Leather Dragon keep leans into the nearness of a wounded-colored sky. Black smoke climbs the blushing hill of sunset. The smoke rises from half-a-dozen burgs in Hornwater. It is strange, to see a city on the water, beside a vast tract of reflective lake, burning.

The knights of The Leather Dragon have begun their war.

Over one distant burg hovers a dome of painted glass. A palace. You watch as, like a volcano, the top of the dome explodes up. A new mushroom of smoke joins the drifts. Glass paints the sky, falls like rain. Even atop The Afthimmel, you feel a draft of that heat.

On a table near the parapet, your Steel waits for you. Your Lady sits nearby, sunken on her knees, her cloak pooling around her, her head hung.

name: Drive - Blob 2
text:
A herald in a robe like puffy steel vapor unwinds a terrific parchment, till it touches his shoes.

"...may by the grace of his blest sword slay his foe.

"By such he shall preserve the life of this woman, Lady Odette, of the family Verne. He shall champion her.

"Or if it is the will of the seven gods, then Great Wyrm Grauf may by the grace of his blest sword slay his foe. By such, he shall decide the fate of this same Lady.

"Moreover, a Leather Dragon shall neither show mercy, nor suffer to be scarred. The guild demands one life from..."

name: Drive - Blob 3
text:
Your Blue Lady buckles one of the sere, cracked pieces of leather that hold your breastplate tight about your chest. You hear a mutter pass her lips. A prayer. You do not to which god or spirit, but she begs some avenue out of this confrontation.

Grauf's apprentice laces the Grand Worm's own shining leather. The armor is scratchless, and often used.

You set aside your whetstone. Taking up a dark-stained cloth, you run it over your Polished Steel Longsword. There is no rust on your Steel, no nick or scratch to mar its shine. As you hold it up and examine it through your visor, the flat of the blade catches dusk's last hints of mellow slate.

Grauf Dragon Eye Longsword needs neither stone nor polish. The hateful drake soul trapped within keeps the weapon sharp enough to cut through rhinoceros hide.

The Guild necromancer- one of few in Hornwater - examines you both.

name: Drive - Blob 4
text:
The Great Wyrm watches you with a fixed eye from beneath the leather horns of his helmet.

"The voice of our necromancer tells me that you have been raised? More than once? Yet there is no surplus necromancy strengthening your marrow.

"I cannot understand. No Leather Dragon's soul is ever raised. No Leather Dragon serving is ever scratched, let alone slain.

"I am sorry to hear that my Lord father is dead. I dreamed once of his face, half chewed by the worms, the skull shining through.

"All men's' fathers must die. I am not that child. Your Lady is not my mother."

name: Drive - Blob 5
text:
You sweep your arm, holding your blade horizon, the flat facing out. Your foe does the same.

This battle will not transpire like your many others. Without your shield you'll need to rely on parrying, dodging, and the limited protection of your rusted armor.

Some of the Great Wyrm's blows will land no matter your prowess. Grauf has no discernable attack pattern. Your only option is to fight reactively. Watch your foe for any twitch or tell, and pray they are not feints.

Landing a cut of your own is almost out-of-the-question. Grauf's seemingly supple leather armor is in reality hard as stone. (It was made keep the dragon both scratchless, and bruiseless).

You will make mistakes against this enemy. Your rusted armor will start to chip and crack.

name: Drive - Blob 6
text:
If you can survive till the sun flashes between the merlons of the tower crenellation, and lights on Graufs Dragon Eye Longsword, you'll see your only chance. Grauf's collar is dark with sweat, his breath comes in gasps. He is not trained for endurance.

By now you bear several wounds, possibly broken ribs. You must ignore the pain. Start some attack routines of your own. Focus on low blows. Nothing you do will land a scratch, but Grauf will grow more and more desperate.

It is at this moment that you will make a fatal mistake. Perhaps you have grown complacent in the wear-down strategy of your battles. Perhaps it is just bad luck. Grauf will land a deep cut to your waist, through a gap in your rusted breastplate.

Back off and keep fighting - you have no other choice. But you must accept the sickening, warm, wetness you feel beneath your leather.

name: Drive - Blob 7
text:
You will see an opening. The neck, beneath the helmet. Your Polished Steel is rightly positioned. You have only the smallest fraction of a second in the endless Time of Dying in which to make your choice.

\#

The Dragon Eye Longsword clatters to the tower stone. Grauf clutches his neck; crimson paints his fingers. You hear your Lady cry in distress.

You are not sure for whom she cries.

You sink to your knees as the suns last arc slides under the distant loam.

\#

name: Finale - Blob 1
text:
You hear the churring music of crickets in the night.

A moist breeze breathes across your neck.

Your heart beats slow, at once supple and heavy, like the sllow-beating wings of a swan.

The tower is gone. You kneel amidst a field of coarse, short grass, beside the shore of Hornwater Lake. The water is purple with dusk. One the far side you can see the black glow of burning Hornwater. Your beach is too distant to hear the clamor of the battle.

If you look to your right, you'll see the embankment of The Road of Graves. Between you and the road, your Blue Lady stares around you, stunned.

Between you and your Blue Lady, Great Wyrm Grauf lies very still.

name: Finale - Blob 2
text:
Your Lady looks from you, to her stepson. She runs to your side.

"My Cracked Cup Knight, your front is covered in blood. Grauf- dead I know.

"What has happened to us? Some wind of dead souls has taken us in its many arms and carried us from the city.

"I led you thus. And him. But: the Shelley Stone! Let me draw it there from your pack. All will be well now, let me press it to your neck as Othelmedir-

"...Why do you reject my touch?"

name: Finale - Blob 3
text:
*Do this. You know this is the right thing. So do it.*

*Your body is a breaking vessel. That too was Othelmedir's sentence on your last revival. Your Last. At last your own Time is over.*

*You must refuse her this. This is your remaining duty. Make it a good one.*

*There. She is putting the stone away. She will not disobey you, look at her eyes.*

*Why does she pull at your helmet? She must not see your disfigured face; she would cast you from her service...*

*No matter now... Cannot stop her...*

*That air from the lake does... Cool on your brow... And She... Blue Lady... Not frightened...*

*Long since your head...*

*Felt Her...*

*Kiss...*

name: Finale - Blob 4
text:
"Thy service is fulfilled, my Cracked Cup Knight. Go now toward that purple western sky.

"Go without misgivings; I will find your place on The Road. Follow that Road, and see- See if you can find its end. Did you not say to me once that you dream of boyhood was to reach the end?

"I take with gladness, back into my own two hands, the responsibility of my fate. Farewell.

"...There. The Stone has worked its magic, just as the necromancer said.

"Grauf, it is the Lady Odette. Breath slowly now. Let your heart catch up. Be still now for a moment, everything is going to be okay. I will hold your hands."

\#

name: Finale - Blob 5
text:
**Sunrise:** Rays of slate color, like cathedral glass, shine down from an eastern dawn, breaking through patchwork clouds. One ray catches and reflects off of a tiger's eye gemstone, in a mancer's charm necklace hanging from the spruce mast of a Hornwater fisherman's boat. A moment later the eye darkens; the boat passes under the shadow of a ruin. The ruined castle of the Leather Dragon.

\#

**Woodcart in the Field:** A Lady with blue-black hair walks alongside a rolling cart pulled by one-horned ox, through a field of rye grass. The back of the cart is filled with unsawn timber, and an axe. The ox is led on a rope by young man with a scar on his neck.

\#

**The Epitaph:** The Time is one that is Dying. The Road is paved over in Graves. Each stone on its winding and indefinite route wears the name of a soul that is buried below it.

One curl of this Road passes under the umbrella of a low, thick-trunked oak. Between of of the oaks twisting roots, propped against the bark, a Polished Steel Longsword sleeps in its scabbard.

One root laces through the soft short grass. Through the Time of Dying it has strethced. It touches one stone on The Road of Graves.

"William."

name: 1. Title Card - Blob 1
text:
The rattle of chains around a gear fades slowly higher and higher. Presently, you hear again your Blue Lady's soft speech.

"Fortune, setback, or only mortal doom? I wonder what we may call that chance meeting with the stranger under the cowl on The Long Road.

"He said to me, 'I have seen like the face of the lord in this locket you present, melancholy lady. Much time ago, and far below, in Alsoburg which lies in Spring-Under-Fjall.' My heart seems to spin in my breast.

"Yet as we walked on from the strange man under the cowl, I recalled suspicion.

"And the deathless, skeletal horse which he led on a rope, it rattled and nickered at once as it went; it sounded in my ear like-"

name: 1. Title Card - Blob 2
text:
A explosive hiss beside your descending cage cuts short your Lady's concern. A gas vent. As it fades above like some great python sliding up a tree, you're Lady's voice has been replaced by the lift operator's. He talks at length, though he too is frequently disrupted.

"-here for eight seasons of easy wintering, myself. One perk of cave-dwelling; it never snows. Not all is sunshine though, hehehe. There issues from the deepest vault, sometimes, a-"

*HISSSSSSSsssssssssssssssssss....*

"-time in Spring-Under-Fjall? A troubadour discovered the cave. A fiddler; a Living Bones who wanted a deep, dark, quiet places to make his songs. But he played too well, and too loudly. Living men heard his music. All sorts followed him down: tailors, mancers, engineers."

*HISSSSSSSsssss....*

"-ever did trust-"

*hiiiiiIIIIIIIISSSSSSSssssssss...*

"-mancy always stronger underground. Closer to the-"

*HIIIIISSSSSSS.*

name: 1. Title Card - Blob 3
text:
The lift rattles. Your knuckles tighten on the handle of your shield. Your mind summons reflections; advice from a chryptsacker; sounds one hears in the caves under the hills and mountains.

**Leatherworm:** "Try to hear the tumbling of boulders down the side of a corge, heard distantly, echoing. Speed it up, make it deeper. That is the leatherworm, which guises itself as a bas-relief, and is the bane of we sackers."

**Herdybey:** "It makes two-tone howls. AHHHH-woooo. In cool caves, good blades'll keep you out of its jaws. But watch for mating bulls in humid holes."

**Golems:** "Marching, heavy steps. Ubiquitous underground. They cannot be molded under sun or stars."

\#

name: 2. Arrival of the Foe - Blob 1
text:
Your cage with plunge through the last four feet of its descent. With a crash of metal on stone, you and your Lady and the man conducting will be thrown from the lift. You'll tumble out onto a long ledge of worked slabs, skirting a huge rectangular chamber of stone and iron architecture. You tumble, into a fight.

**The Battle for Station Archer:**

The engineer-knights of the station are locked in a pitched battle. Their foes - golems. Along every gantry, all across the stone floor, climbing the grated metal stairs that run from level to level; the automatons of wax and bone throw their fists against the defenders.

When you arrive you'll find the knights of the station having the worsts of things. The knights are capable fighters, but not one of them has an arm to match your. Their heraldic aprons make poor defense against golem fists.

Your actions directly determine the number of engineer-knights that will survive the battle. Start by attacking the two golems on the ledge to your right. One of the golems is already walking lopsided, with a portion of its flank carved out. You can hack it in two with a blow or three. The three knights who you save in this way will immediately follow your lead.

From there, your best best is to clear a path to the round steel door, on the ledge one level higher than your own, on the opposite side of the chamber. Rescue as many knights as you can. Understand that, no matter your skill or luck, the station will be lost.

name: 2. Arrival of the Foe - Blob 2
text:
There are a few idiosyncratic members of the engineer-knights. You may wish to spot and rescue these men especially.

**Damon the Pilferer:** A skinny but tall man. Has a good eye. Carrie's the station's only crossbow.

**Old Soldier:** He must be strong to carry that heavy broadsword and pack on that armor. Good with his shield too.

**Forester:** One of the forest cats, giant and tame, guards his shadow.

**Former Mance:** No combat specialty. Has none of necromancy's necessary reagents. Does offer insights on your foes.

name: 3. Enter the Arena - Blob 1
text:
Cave wind whistles through your visor as you emerge onto a studded metal causway between two station towers. Fifteen feet below, the court of natural stone writhes with humming pipes of bronze.

Across fifteen miles, the cavern of Spring-Under-Fjall meanders and crawls.

A roiling stonescape stretches far, far away, like the surface of an ocean frozen under a black hurricane. A small grove giant mushrooms grows just a few yards from Station Archer's metal gates. Another score of golems stamp throug the sundered gate even as you look. Other shadows, maybe golems, maybe not, climb along the darkest ravines of the cave, where no light touches.

Yet light plays over many of the tumbling stones, and the crashing crystal waterfall, and the still lake. A giant yellow disk, a subterranean sun, sways from the ceiling over a raised rocky bluff. Atop that bluff, you can just spy the undercity of Alsoburg.

Station Archer is one of four stations in the cave. The station warm Spring-Under-Fjall by drawning water from deeper hotsprings.

\#

name: 4. False Hopes - Blob 1
text:
One of the station knights slams the round iron door shut and locks it with a spoked wheel on the nearby wall. The metallic reverberations fade quickly, in the closed, galleried cylinder of the pressure tower.

The knights begin to speak at once.

"Death averting, your arrival, sir knight. Spared are we, though not Archer I fear."

"Stations Carringgore and Queen-Lace have already succumbed to the golems of The Crawling Silk. Now three are fallen."

"We'll freeze! Spring-Under-Fjall will be as the coldest winter one ever saw under the days above, should the last station succumb."

"Cheer up boy. We must look to ourselves. We must crawl through one of Archer's hot pipes, I think, and come out well-cooked."

name: 4. False Hopes - Blob 2
text:
*The Crawling Silk. Have you ever swung against any foe so named? Has any warrior you ever met spoken of battle with a thing?*

*It rings with necromancy. Necromancy, that thrives beneath the ground where it is closest to its most necessary material. But what do you remember that has overminded the workings of so many golems at once?*

*There was the grot. Mool. A master of the unliving that you could not face. Yet that one's servants were mechanical dead. Not golems.*

*Why do you waste your thoughts in this way? Your duty is to your Lady. The cause of these men is theirs, not yours. You do not serve your ego; you need not parade your valor with skillful display of Steel.*

*So, how then will you spirit your Lady from this danger?*

name: 4. False Hopes - Blob 3
text:
Your Blue Lady sees the glance behind your visor.

"That wanderer on the road; did he lead us here with purpose I wonder? I may be wrong to ask so openly about my stepson.\
"When one's hope is known it may be exploited.

"We are trapped with these engineers now. I know the peril, my knight, but I ask that you help them.

"You have destroyed the masters of other golems. Does this grant you any intuition?"

name: 4. False Hopes - Blob 4
text:
A gonging noise reverberates from the thick metal door. A young knight standing nearby leaps to the wheel lock. But, the drumming is not repeated. The reverberations fade.

Another knight, a hairy one with a ragged apron and a cut on his brow, recalls the sewers below the station. Each tower has a small drain to the sewer; he points it out. It is only a small pipe, but the sewer main is wide enough to walk in, and bears no heated water.

It will take the knights ten minutes to carve a hole around the drain to the sewer.

\#

name: 5. Trapped, Battle Dance - Blob 1
text:
Slowly, then gaining speed, a steel plate spins like a flashing diamond, down the central space of the pressure tower, reflecting lamps, humming, before it impacts the flat stone floor like a doom-tone shot from The Scratcher's organ lyre.

**The Tower Assault:**

The golems slam against the walls and  ceiling of your tower. Already they have scraped through one ruptured rivet.

You'll have to defend both the engineer-knights at the drain, and the tower's pressure control panel. The panel is beside the third landing, embedded in a wall of solid lead. (It's the one that starts with two wheels turned to three-o-clock).

If you fail to protect the panel the golems, driven by a greater mind, will use to overpressurize the tower pipes. The pipes running along the walls will rupture. You and the other survivors will be boiled in hotspring rain. You can, potentially ,turn the control panel to your advantage. Selective burst any pipe near where they breach the tower; the hot water will melt their waxen frames. You'll also have between six and fourteen knights available to help defend.

\#

name: 5. Trapped, Battle Dance - Blob 2
text:
"Well fought, sir knight! We're almost through.

"I can see the sewer by my lamp. Not so grimy. It's not for greywater, after all.

"Hehehe, I see this wax and bone trickly by my foot. Not so fright-

"Oh, god below, look!"

name: 6. The Master Appears - Blob 1
text:
The puddles of springwater cease to steam. The air clears. The pipes grow a lichen of white, fuzzy frost.

Through a broken wall of stone rubble, you spy a thing low on the ground. Not a golem. You cannot see it clearly between the thudding wax-and-bone feet. It looks man-shaped, crawling on hands and knees, dragging a white beard, like the trail of a comet.

Amidst the thuds, a toneless deadspeech chant: *harag naj, gonosz naj; égos at haruvá halj.*

name: 7. The Enemy Triumphs - Blob 1
text:
Your wool shirt hugs your skin like winter moonlight under your armor. The molten wax thickens over your Sabatons. Shiver if you must, pry your feet free, and act.

You only have a few minutes before The Crawling Silk penetrates the tower. There is no point defending the control panel now. Get to the exit.

But retrieve your Lady first. She is under the guard of two engineer knights, who are holding off a rib-bone spiked goelm. Your only option is to carve a path through the waxen foes. Fortunately the encroaching cold stiffens the golems. You can let your guard fall lax, and focus on heavy blows with your Steel.

Once you've collected your Lady and reached the drain, you need only defend until the crack of falling brickwork tells you that the way is open.

Help your Lady and the knights down the hole, then jump down after. There is a rotary seal a few yards down the black tunnel. The tower knights will use this to check your pursuers.

You are safe. But the station has fallen.

\#

name: 8. Introduction - Blob 1
text:
The heavy boot of a knight kicks loose a vertical round of rusted iron. It flies from the opening of the sewer, splashes down on the still water of the lake, manifests glimmering ripples. The distant disk of the underworld sun is cold and yellow.

You might, as you emerge from the sewer, almost mistake the scene for surface night, under a yellow Harridan's Moon. Almost; but for the absence of of a bullfrog's drone, or a chorus of chirring crickets, of the wierd songs of nightbirds. The underworld lake is silent.

You help your Blue Lady down from the ledge of the sewer tunnel. Her boots and the base of her cloak are flecked with mud, but her face is untroubled.

name: 8. Introduction - Blob 2
text:
One of the surviving knights has lost his ear in the battle; a thick gauze wraps his skull. He speaks at a shout, before a blow on his shoulder persuades him to whisper.

"Ten years I rubbed to a shine those pipes of steel and doors of iron. Ten years, my brother and I swept the dust from the flagstones and walkways of Archer.

"We have, with our mounted Fire Bassoons, and our hammers, and arbalests; sent off chimirae, men-of-scale, clouds of drinking bats. They could not fell Archer, nor any other station.

"This Crawling Silk... is an agony we have never known. To lose so much...

"Once, I recall, a necromancer came to Alsoburg. He had a golem; only one. And that was was flesh, and docile."

\#

name: 8. Introduction - Blob 3
text:
**The Crawling Silk:**

In the prior age, in The Time of Dying, a heptad of mancers set aside their differences, merging their spellpower in their desire to find the fabled Sickened Cradle. Certain mancers had, for decade after decade after decade, shared dreams. Dreams that come from The Thin Man; that god-necromancer who found the gift of eternal youth united to eternal slumber. In slumber, in The Cradle, he sends to his seekers dream upon dream, nightmare after nightmare.

This new heptad of mancers plunged the darkest caves and deepest graves that wind under The Endless Road. In their seeking they violated the Chrypt of the Fermented Princess, which was sacred to another necromancer-god, The Mother of worms. As punishment that goddess destroyed six of the heptad. She left one necromancer alive, a man with a beard long and painted white. As he groveled, listening at the stone, she blessed him with the eternal life, and cursed him to forever grovel, forever, listen, driven to ascendent madness by voices below.

\#

name: 8. Introduction - Blob 4
text:
*You know this enemy. It comes from the old song cycle: The Harmonies of Avda. The Harmony of False Seven.*

*Much force is in the deadspeech that issues from a throat which is hoary and flecked with grey.*

*You must find some route which climbs up and out of Spring-Under-Fjall. You have escorted Her into a cavern where the atmosphere is humid with threat to life. Bring Her out of it as quickly as you are able. That is your sole duty.*

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot - Blob 1
text:
You see a trio of sleek luminous eels plying their way up the current of a narrow but deep cave river, and your group marches along the bank soft with algae. The eels dart into hiding as one of the station knights with a crown of thin, ling, curling black hair speaks.

"Friends, I've just considered.

"Where Archer stood on the lower southmost bluff, The Silk must lead its army along only one fixed fixed path to reach the last heat towers at Station Glaznyst.

"That path runs of a narrow track, above a rough gorge which is sawtoothed of floor, beneath a long scree of unstable, loose rocks.

"Why not march hard for Glaznyst direct from Alsoburg? We might warn them; with them, dig at that loose rock; together, let tumble the landslide over this one who crawls and all his golems."

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot - Blob 2
text:
The knights will choose one of three routes from the exit of the sewers to Alsoburg. The route is chosen at random.

**The Narrow Lake Strand:** The ripples from the broken sewer grate have vanished. As you stare out over the bestilled lake, you will witness the slow, crystallizing transformation of the water. Your breath mists before your helmet.

**Ruins of Station :** If you duck inside the ruined tower with the blasted door, you can retrieve a small pouch of twelve Onyx Sunflower seeds, from beneath a splintered wooden bench.

**Mushroom Forest:** In the gossamer frills of these giant mushrooms, you will hear small shuffling and chittering noises. Bats. Proceed to the end without looking up, and you will be rewarded with a Flawless Summonable Squeaking Icon.

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot - Blob 3
text:
As your march proceeds nearer and near to Alsoburg you are gradually enveloped in the rising warmth of the sun disk.

You'll eventually come onto the shadow of a long hill of craggy stone. Bits of moss grow over the stumps of old mushrooms.

Your sense of danger piques.

One of the surviving knights is humming a marching tune. Too loudly. Another is suggesting that your group might corner and harness a wild Mildew Beetle from the nearby surface swamp, and turn it against The Crawling Silk.

Before you can suggest silence you will reach the top of the hill.

As you do you will stumble into another marching band; they, silent.

Hides. Skin clad servants of The Crawling Silk.

name: 10. The Second Conflict - Blob 1
text:
**Eleven Strokes with The Old Man Below :**

The hides (seven) and two golems first attack.

One close hide lashes with a spine-sewn flail.

You parry, sever the whip, cut high.

His head thuds and bounces downhill.

The station knights meet the crash.

Hammer, spear, daggers cleave.

One hide mimes plowing.

Chops a war pick.

Femur spiked.

Smiting.

*Thump.*

name: 10. The Second Conflict - Blob 2
text:
The knight drops forward as the hide rips the femur-spike through his clavicle. Before you may redress the killing another hide and a golem will step into your field-of-view.

This fight will be short. The surviving knights can hold only a few minutes against one golem and the force of hides. You'll have to help, and also beat your immediate foes. And, defend your Lady.

To achieve that end, act thus: Block the golem's heavy fist slantingly, step back, lash right once to distract the hide, block the second punch, cut down to sever the golems leg, let it topple downhill, parry the hide again, then brain him with your shield.

After, assist the knights to victory.

name: 11. The Relieved Breath - Blob 1
text:
"Sir knight, you have seen more of the world than we. What kind of a backworldman

flays and wears the skins from our bodies? Sharpens our bones for his weapons?"

"Look! Alsoburg ahead. The gate is asunder. Has the town capitulated already?"

"Day of disgusting sorrow."

"Please join us, sir knight. Let your lady rest at- No. Too late, here comes another company of our foe. Quick; guise ourselves. Take these skins of our fellows. Let's slip in with The Crawling Silk's man-slaves."

\#

name: 12. The Hero and the Master - Blob 1
text:
One iron-barred door of the Alsoburg gatehouse lays flat on the grassless grey dirt within the shadow cast by the sun-disk just above. The long golem-and-slave train of The Crawling silk presses the thin bars deeper into the soil as they tread into the town.

You take care to step between the bars yourself; the clink of your sabatons might reveal you under your skin-robe disguise.

**Card Houses:** Each home is made of two titanic stone slabs leaning against each other, with mudbrick fronts. The citizens have shut their doors and windows, cowing behind false shelters.

**The Tower of Invisible Fire:** Silence. The steel drum in the heart of Alsoburg shed's no warmth.

**The Mayor's Keep:** Two ladies, dressed in silver-and-sapphire silk robes which seem to glow in the yellow sun-disk's rays, weep from the third floor balcony of the Mayor's Keep.

**The Gate of Xyn:** The Gate of Xyn, in the Temple of Xyn, has been thrown open. In Alsoburg, this symbolizes an end of peace.

name: 12. The Hero and the Master - Blob 2
text:
From under the flayed hoods of the real hides surrounding you, that same deadspeech hymn issues, a united hiss.

"Harag naj, gonosz naj; égos at Haruvá halj."

*Day of wrath, evil sky; shall all the Road in blackness lie.*

"Ahoz Hjetes az élérn mondj."

*As Seven bid mens' souls to die.*

*TODOTODO*

"Malyan borznak kell megszáll mét; amizor a közalsa Bíry talát,"

*What horror must 'oercome the heart; when Necromancer Silk shall start,*

"Es rastáld átgész ember csontjat."

*And bids the bones by blackened art art.*

name: 12. The Hero and the Master - Blob 3
text:
**Abased Ontogeny:**

Once The Crawling Silk may walked as a human being. Now, as you look on from a few yard's distance, you know that dooming Time has changed this creature's biology. It protuberant spine has far too many vertebrae in far too many lines. The elbows and knees on which it walks have become like the hooves of a cow, with its forearms and lower legs rise like withered branches from each hoof. Its long and silky white forms the carpet of which it crawls.

The abased body swells. Doubles. Triples in size. The three-stranded spine rises like a white limb on a barkless tree. The Crawling Silk never raises its own shaggy head. But from under its man, the deadspeech hymn swells.

name: 12. The Hero and the Master - Blob 4
text:
You have been seperated from the other disguised knights. A quick glance from under your hood - they have taken up a place by one of their 'Fire Bassoon's, over the gatehouse.

The throng pressed close. You are only a few feet from the towering, Crawling Silk.

You hear the excited gurgle of flames swelling through a pipe.

\#

name: 13. The Finale - Blob 1
text:
As flames blast down from Fire bassoon, the sheltered militia of alsoburg and the disguised knights of Station Archer launch their unexpectedly coordinated assault upon the host of The Crawling Silk. The battle begins.

For a moment you should hold still while golems and hides make space. Then throw off your disguise and attack. Hack The Crawling Silk with a few solid strokes. Ignore  the chill spreading down arm. Step back only when this monster gives you its bald-headed regard.

You may feel your body stop on you for the span of a brief few seconds; an unconquerable hesitation. Think of your Lady and overcome it.

You can strike one or two of the nearest hides down. Or, fell a golem. Your personal fight will begin in earnest when The Crawling Silk finally captures you in its perception.
