name: 13. The Finale - Blob 2
text:
**Dream of the Winter Storm:**

With a nod of scalp The Crawling Silk shall draw you into one of its frozen dreams. Your breath mists through your visor. Your heart pushes blood like lard. Howling snowfall surrounds you and the monster alone.

On the bright side, golems and hides cannot enter the dream. On the black side, your own movement is slow within the storm - always the case with movement in a dream.

Your goal in the dream is to close and inflict a wound. If you remain trapped in the dream for five minutes, it will freeze your mind mind to ice.

Long serpents of white hair will slither through the snow, entangling your feet. You'll have to chop and hack them off. The entire time The Silk will be Crawling back and away from you.

If you can get in close and cut The Crawling Silk upon its scalp, a brazen gong will sound the end of the vision.

name: 13. The Finale - Blob 3
text:
You'll find yourself back in town square, surrounded by fire and madness. Your Lady, at least, seems safe behind the militia.

The Crawling Silk, bleeding, will call upon its remaining resources. Four golems turn on you. Additionally, The Silk's white hair surrounds its body in an oval cocoon. The cocoon is hard as marble; your sword cannot cut it. The only way to break through the iron defense is to lure the attacking golems into smashing it with their fists. Three blows will do for the shell; one will do for you.

When the cocoon breaks, finish off The Crawling Silk by lopping off its head.

\#

name: 13. The Finale - Blob 4
text:
The golems melt. The hides are burnt or beaten to death. Your Lady is safe.

A cobbler in red sandals approaches you.

"Not bad. Not as good as the six gaps I once felled in a single blow, you know.

"That Blue Lady told me a bit about you, you know.

"About why you're here. About that stepson you're looking for.

"I did see him pass through here, you know, long ago. He left. He was going to Hornwater, east on the road."

name: 13. The Finale - Blob 5
text: In the Time of Dying lies a Road of Graves.

name: 1 - Battle (Mars) - Blob 3
text:
**Corroding Triumph of Surveillance:**

A golem of riveted copper, as tall as twenty tall men, impends over the wide cliffside lane. It's crown of sharpened vents shines in the slate-colored sunrays above the lip of Middlemoss Gorge. One of its thousand pound rusted boot hovers frozen mid-stomp.

A skeleton of black bones, longer than twenty long serpents, lies on its back beneath the golem. The giant loops of its ribs have been fractured by the plate of the golems copper axe. Its fanged jawbone gapes. It is the skeleton of the Demon King, Surveillance.

\#

name: 1 - Battle (Mars) - Blob 2
text:
You sweep your arm up and hold your blade horizon, the flat facing out. Your foe does the same. The duel begins.

Your Steel is of an edge to wound the wind. Yet, landing a blow will be the trial. Wallcrest, Knight of the Leather Dragon, parries or ducks even your swiftest slashes. You'll quickly realize why his order carries their scarless reputation.

The knight usually attacks in the pattern: left, overhead, low jab, left. Use your shield for the left and overhead attacks, and parry the jab. If he ripostes one of your attacks, deflect the edge of his sword with your gauntlet.

The first blow of yours which lands will cut leather, but not flesh. When this happens the frequency of his attacks will double. You will be pressed back under the shadow of the monument's boot.

\#

name: 1 - Battle (Mars) - Blob 4
text:
To beat this Leather Dragon, accept a blow. You know how to take a wound. He does not. Let him break through your guard with one deep cut (though not too deep). He will open himself for retaliation. All it will take is one blow.

\#

As your foe falls to a knee, you should get yourself out from the golem's shadow. Deafen yourself to the clanging, and keep your eyes the fate of the bested duelist.

You have won; your foe knew the price of the challenge.

\#

name: 1 - Battle (Mars) - Blob 1
text:
*Must you entertain this terrible stranger-knight?*

*Wallcrest, he names himself. His plates are made from horse leather. A knight errant, of The Leather Dragon. You know this order. Scarless. Chivalrous.*

*Your Steel will cut that armor if it must.*

*He draws his own sword now. It is polished, see how it shines there, even in the shadow of the monument.*

*A small cut may snuff his hubris.*

*Those cheering onlookers. Your Lady stands among them, but she understands, and needs not your look.*

*Enough. Let the crowd be in the fog of sense. Engage this man, and if he is too strong for you, die.*

name: 2 - Rest (Venus) - Blob 1
text:
Where The Road runs north of Middlemoss, at the head of the gorge, runs a narrow boiling weir. The barrier molds a cliff-shaded pond, before releasing the water to crash soundlessly down the bottomless gorge.

A driftwood deadfall shapes a hollow beside the weir, just under the top of the waterfall. Mist swaddles it. Soft moss upholsters the sinuous and warped wood.

You sit with your Blue Lady at the back of the hollow. Your Cat's Eye Spear sheds a spare viridian light in the space. As you skin a whetstone along your Steel's reflective edge, your Lady hums a folk melody.

Her humming ceases. A branch cracks. Someone approaches.

name: 2 - Rest (Venus) - Blob 2
text:
**The Wicker Tyro:**

A young man walks quietly into the hollow on wicker sandals. His breastplate is resin and willow, his cropped hair and sideburns are blonde. He seems not to have seen you right away; he stares fixedly at your Lady.

"Good Lady, I hope you will not find me forward. A man, a fighter, must speak the truth of his heart.

"I heard your voice. Just now. And at the monument. I am Skimcalf, and I would pledge my arm to such a voice.

"oh. I did not realize another was here. The knight from the duel...

"You are her pledged servant? Sir, defend that honor!"

name: 2 - Rest (Venus) - Blob 3
text:
The young man will swing at you with a battered and pockmarked cutlass.

You needn't be daunted; this will be a shorter duel. The man swings in unmeasured, telegraphed, predictable patterns, mostly chops, though he may try to jab with his cutlass. Not one of his blows is likely to dent your armor, rusted though it is.

You can slay him. It would be just as easy, however, to disarm or disable him. A knock or two with your scabbarded Steel would lay him on the ground, unhurt of body.

\#

name: 2 - Rest (Venus) - Blob 4
text:
A tip: search under the sodden log on the ledge behind the weir's waterfall. You have a small chance of finding one of the following Oddboons.

**Leather Sack:** If you open the sack, you find a living infant with a Radiant Scalp. Turn it in to the Middlemoss Mayor to earn a reward.

**A Thumb and a Ring:** The ring is gold, and bears the signet of a wasp.

**Engraved Nautilus:** This shell is made of polished Ivory. It shows a map through The Tunnel of the Stillbreather.

\#

name: 2 - Rest (Venus) - Blob 5
text:
As he rubs his knee, and sits up, Skimcalf turns his fixed look to you.

"Well is your defending, sir knight. I only wanted-

"But, let me learn and serve as one! Teach me, sir, how you carries that sword within my strike before it could land. Teach me where to swing. And when.

"Teach me, I beg. I am in need. A duel of my own compels me in five daybreaks of our Time.

"You would not amuse me for nothing I warrant. When I heard your Lady's musical voice at Surveillance's Triumph, she mentioned a stepson? I thought her sigil looked familiar. If you would teach me..."

\#

name: 3 - Capers (Mercury) - Blob 1
text:
You will have four full days to train the young fighter. Each day, you may train him in one or more of the following aspects: stances and posture, legwork, tension and balance, grip, attack, and defense.

Skimcalf will pick up the basic of stance and legwork easily enough. Don't spend too much time on defensive techniques. Teaching him chop combinations and winning binds will see more rapid advancement.

You can train him at the Shellfish Museum, or at the Dry Millwheel. Your best bet however is to train where his duel will take place, under Surveillance's Triumph.

name: 3 - Capers (Mercury) - Blob 2
text:
*He did well to block that last strike. But you can see his temperament precludes easy grasp of parries or creating distance. His is a common weakness of the young.*

*You wish more days available. You wish that you could teach him the system of the fighter beyond the fight. The Virtue of Silence.*

*You should drive harder. You must be vicious. The mercy will be in victory and life.*

name: 3 - Capers (Mercury) - Blob 3
text:
The days wax; Skimcalf's tongue unfurls.

"The Oat Order's edicts state that their knights, on being admitted, must pass his next ten days in adventures..."

"I've only been beyond the gorge once. But, I hope one day to see all The Road..."

"Twins they were, these girls in The Coffin Rollers. I know, I know; you silently judge my tolerance. Yet, I shuddered at their stupidity..."

"My opponent? Hah! Nevermind him, sir. A common thug..."

\#

name: 4 - Prestige (Jupiter) - Blob 1
text:
Middlemoss boasts a variety of tradesmen, craftsmen, merchants, markets, and places of idiosyncrasy.

**Fogsea the Armorer:** A sooty, hairless, corpulent mastersmith. From him you may purchase a helmet for skimcalf. He Sells three styles: Knight's Watchtower Helm, Knight's Founder Helm, Knight's Stonetower Helm.\
**The Loweating Herd:** Roams on Middlemoss's lowest habitable ledge. From the livestock throats issues sound like that which issues from burning wood. A moisture as of tears trickles down gorge wall.\
**Chryptsacker's Lodge:** The chryptsackers never sell to customers directly. They do the scouting, digging, and cleaning, then broker their goods through licensed peddlers.

**Guilda the Scarab Seller:** Known affectionately among the local mancers as 'the six and a half foot scowl'. Sells a wide diversity of soul scarabs. His stock changes every daybreak.\
**The Cloth and Tailor Market:** Here you can purchase any cut, color, cloth, and style of graveclothes, all free of dirt, and with very few wormholes.

**Hall of Loving Shapers:** The best place for edged steel. Skimcalf's jaw swings loose if you present him his first sword from one of these consummate smiths.\
**The Shellfish Museum:** Here, in these resonant marble halls, in this former tomb of a priest of a god-necromancer, from a long ago Time; here you may see the layers of Times and Dyings. The curators have organized the different fossils and shells and bones from their excavations into the layers of graves in the Middlemoss gorge.\
**Ledge of the Twenty Mancers:** This long, majectic quarter is lined with guilds, workshops, laboratories, and school of necromancy. It is named for the twenty mancers who errected the death fence separating the livable lervels of the gorge from the mad souls of its unfathomed lower depths.\
**Suspicious Tile:** This alley tile may appear anywhere in town. When you step on it, the tile cracks. Peeling it up, you find an inscription predicting this exact Time and location in which the tile has been broken.\
**Thornwarren:** Take care if you visit this residential ghetto with Skimcalf. The tomb homes in this part of town seem almost unlived-in; rats skitter over the rotten wood threshholds; mildew chokes the air. Yet there are dwellers in the shadows. Many, if given the chance, will gladly trade their scruples for your worldly goods and bodies.\
**Ghostglass Shop:** Changes locations every seven and a half hours. If you can find it, the curving, gleaming, colored faces are surprisingly lifelike, and astoundingly free! For a favor...\
**The Long Well:** Supposedly goes all the way to the bottom of the chasm; anyone who brings the bucket all the way up is rewarded with a glass of The Time of Dying's purest water.

\#

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn) - Blob 1
text:
*The young man's will is not behind the spar today. He holds his tongue. You see plain temerity in that backwards step.*

*Tomorrow's combat cools his vigor? Something else? You cannot wholly suppress a misgiving.*

*Your Lady, too, has fixed her eye on Skimcalf this day. Why? She hopes that he lives, maybe. Hopes that he will join your company on The Road. You hope for it too.*

*You should control that foolishness. Hope and Want are not your duty; yours is to serve your Blue Lady's ends.*

*Moreover, The man has some good skill. You have slain many of less ability than he now possesses.*

\#

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn) - Blob 2
text:
You hear Skimcalf before you see him. His wicker sandals creak on the street, over this hiss of the rain beyond your sepulchral boarding chamber. A minute later he appears in the doorway.

"I thank you, master knight, for all that you have taught me of fighting with a sword. One day I will match you.

"I thank you as well, good Lady, for all your patience, attention, and kindness. I hope my improvement has pleased your discerning eye today.

"You will find your family member. But, not through any knowledge of mine.

"I never saw nor met nor heard-of this stepson. Please look differently at me! When I win my duel I will happily accompany-"

\#

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn) - Blob 3
text:
**Surveillance's Triumph:**

Surveillance was so named for his role and the greatest master of spies among all the Demon Kings. He elevated, from among the lists of man who all were slaves, some to be his crows and ravens and vultures.

He was the first of the Demon Kings to die.

The seven god-necromancers and their acolytes could not use their risen dead against Surveillance. For, the King's spikes reported any whisper of necromancy. The god-necromancers resorted to other craft. They enjoined the smiths and metalworkers to build the Lendendrung, The Golem, in secret.

When the signs in the comets and the heavens were propitious, the necromancers fed Lendendrung a flood of human souls. Thus was it alive.

Lendendrung fulfilled its purpose. It slew the demon king. It cost the golem every soul by which it had been animate. The Golem was forever dead.

And, Lendendrung could not destroy all of Surveillance. It could not destroy the Demon King's ear. The Demon King, though he is only a skeleton, still hears when the soul of an unworthy slave draws near his fanged jaw.

\#

name: 5 - Atrophy (Saturn) - Blob 4
text:
Your Blue Lady breaks her silence.

"Since the slate-colored sun broke the crack of the gorge, I have seen my daughter. Her white face has haunted your shoulder since morning.

"Still her lips keep shut. Still her eyes would burn me. Why? I would touch her hand, were she living. Perhaps her spirit saw your spirit, where my flesh sees only its like.

"I recall how my lord addressed lies.

"'Save beatings for animals,' he said. 'Never, however, let they without faith stain our floor.'"

You remain by your Lady's side - always - as Skimcalf's wicker sandals diminish creakily down the street.

\#

name: 6 - Spellcasting (Uranus) - Blob 1
text:
The next day is like night. The hissing rain remains. You lean against the cool marble. Your thoughts are warm and agitated.

Only your Lady's voice suffices to break the contemplation. She suggests you both, under cloak, attend the duel.

Getting there is not so easy.

Today happens also to be the beginning of Deep Jain, the summer festival. The Cult of the Material Souls has gathered to Middlemoss.

At the first bridge a throngs of snow-white bodies will grab your shield and pull you into into their wheel. Don't draw your Steel. Keep hold of your Lady's arm, let the dancer's carry you three-quarters of the way around, then jerk yourself free.

You may hear a disturbing rumor as you step off the bridge: "-coins are betting on the brute. Did you see that morningstar?"

Later, after entering Plaidside, a spontaneous choir will inflate amidst the crowd. Disjunct voices crash alternately in each of your ears, roll tumbling in your helm, spin you in dizzy circles. You'll find yourself caught in a moving maze of pedestrians.

To reach the opposite end of Plaidside swiftly, look-for and navigate-to the following landmark persons: blonde man with half-a-nose, towering ballerina, ancient grain seller, beggar in spectacles, burning dwarf.

By speeding toward Surveillance's Triumph, you will arrive in time to witness the duel's end, but not to affect its outcome. That is all you may hope for.

\#

name: 6 - Spellcasting (Uranus) - Blob 2
text:
Pressing through the audience, you finally see Skimcalf. He is pressing the attack against his foe.

R the Killer.

R is retreating, blocking Skimcalf's chops with a small buckler. You've never seen him use a buckler before.

Your Lady asks you to help the young apprentice. You can do nothing.

R retreats steadily. He is pressed back under the shadow of the monument's boot.

Skimcalf seems to remember your duel of days ago. He steps back, making space, not pursuing under the copper boot.

Today it is darker. The shadow is deeper.

The heavy buckler flies out. Skimcalf's face is smashed. He staggers. R lunges out. Swings.

The Horned Morningstars tears through Skimcalf's neck.

\#

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune) - Blob 1
text:
As you kneel beside the dying Skimcalf, his unfocused eyes slide in your direction.

"Sir. And Lady. I am quite- quite happy to see you.

"I thought I should put distance between him and I, sir. But that must have been wrong. I should have liked to serve in a knightly order. To serve you as well, Lady. I am sorry for my lie.

"The thug has torn my neck. For me, knighthood and a lady shall long wait.

"You should both step back now. I must accept the fate of the monument. Goodbye."

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune) - Blob 2
text:
*You must ignore that rising clamor. You knew this was a possibility. Think of your breath, the feel of your shield in your hand. You must distract yourself.*

*But you cannot. You know you cannot. You know you cannot ignore that sucking, whistling wind. Do you hear his body sliding on the earth. It slides towards those open fangs.*

*Look upon your Lady. She is all that should occupy your thoughts. You are her servant, your mind-*

*YOUR mind? You are a fool. You know that another shares your mind, here, under the bones of a KING. Two souls, one cracked cup. But, only yours is the soul of a slave.*

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune) - Blob 3
text:
The too-familiar voice of R the Killer cuts through the thoughts in your head.

"Steelclad. And the sorry widow. It always puts a little spring in my heel, shaking eyeballs with you two.

"Oh, did you know that body on the ground back there? Was that your - what was it? - stepcousin?

"I don't apologize. He did not need to die. He chose a fight and paid for it.

"There are remedies for all things but death."

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune) - Blob 4
text:
*LISTEN! Do you hear that Crunching and Tearing? Bones and Flesh. And what is that last chord in the music? Yes, the snapping of a pair of wicker sandals.*

*You did well to avoid such a fate yourself.*

*You know, as blissful as the consumption is for the fanged jaw of a Demon King, just so much is it a TORMENT for the slave that is being consumed. You try now to think of how hot your ears feel, but you cannot. You must hear the last creaking of those ancient black bones. They wait for the next victim. Ever vigilant.*

*Your mind is your now. Flee back to your Road. Your god-necromancers made it for men.*

name: 7 - Mystery/Resolution (Neptune) - Blob 5
text: In the Time of Dying lies a Road of Graves.

name: 1 - Prelude - Blob 1
text:
"Do you know, my knight; I saw the ghost of my daughter again.

"It was this morning, before I woke. I dreamed of the first angling sheet of slate light sliding over those stern masses of rock and snow ahead of us. I saw her then on reflected water by which we made our camp.

"Not as a reflection. She stood on the water, wearing only the short cotton shift in which we found her. After- But her face was what struck me. The shine in her eyes was none other but Hate. It is this changed look which is all I have seen in her ghost.

"Yet before then her face for me had begun to change. You recall those last years. Her *Metamorphosis.*"

name: 1 - Prelude - Blob 2
text:
As you lift your Lady over a fissure in the glacier, you study her expression and her tone.

*This is the first time you have heard her speak of a dream in many sunrises. The first since The Tower of Silence. Her speech is plainer to your ear now. But...*

*Yours not to question. Serve only, and that with doubts expelled.*

*It is good that you've come far from Middlemoss. That other Road traveler you met said the mountain air bulwarks the lungs. And the spirit.*

*If so, it is worth this little rime on your visor. This chill in your toes through your boots. And there are the translucent skies.*

\#

name: 1 - Prelude - Blob 3
text:
**Legends of the Meathook Mountains:**

The settlers who live near The Road, where it slithers among the rambling stone and ice of the Meathooks, propegate a suet of myth. They have ten thousand tales of undead things living in the mountain shadows.

One trapper of giant rats, with a mouth that is scarred, says that hidden somewhere in the Meathooks is The Massy Fane, the lost temple of The Old Man below. He says that god-necromancer built the temple when he stopped in the Meathooks on his journey from the bottom to the top of The Road. He says the Old Man left "reflections of death" in the ice on the mountains.

Another legend tells of the Green Firebeetle, which paints the aurora in the night sky when it flies.

\#

name: 1 - Prelude - Blob 4
text:
*It troubles you, however - this way you've picked. Through Old Soulwood. Up into The Meathooks. Already your Steel has been summoned in Her defense. Are the threats on this skein of The Road lesser than others you might have brought Her toward?*

*There is only one Road of Graves. Yet it has many cul-de-sacks.*

*You might have gone through Melodeep. That echoing pipe under the sea, full of slime-covered marble, and polyps. Or over The Leviathan Fields.*

*...*

*Ahead it gets steeper. How much farther? How many miles until...*

*#*

name: 2 - Opening Conflict - Blob 1
text:
You might have seen the shadowed entrance of the tunnel earlier in your climb. But these last three miles will be the most perilous yet.

There are two basic ways to get to the tunnel. One is to follow along the rugged slope of turf and boulders, along face of the glacier. It starts in a northward direction, but then curves back east. There is a random chance you'll run into either a pack of three to five feral wolves, or two pagan berserkers. Your Polished Steel Longsword will be called for in either case. Also watch out for the patch of quicksnow about a mile into this route. (If you fall in, you will certainly perish).

The other path you can take is through a narrow canyon, a crevasse, which cuts directly ahead through the glacier. While this route is more direct, you'll have to smash through several hundred yards of glazed cobwebs, and deal with the associated Crackling Spiders. While single spiders don't give your Steel too much trouble, take care not to let them swarm you. Eventually you'll reach a vertical cliff of ice at the canyon's end. Your Lady will suggest you climb it with a rope, while she waits below. This is the quickest course, but you'll need to climb fast, or your Blue Lady will fall prey to the host of crackling fangs.

\#

name: 2 - Opening Conflict - Blob 2
text:
**Whence Meathook's Glaciers?**

Orca, last of the indiginous slavemen of The Meathook Mountains, tells the story of abnormal glacial formations thus.

The mancers of the first Time of Dying wanted a freezer. A place to preserve their most important cadavers. Extinct species, elves, monsters, etcetera.

From Winter, which has always been Necromancy's season, they borrowed breath. The Winter wind. They installed this breath within their sturdiest un-living hosts. They buried the hosts in deep mountain tombs. From these issues always the cold breath of Winter. It freezes the rains. It forms the massive, endless glacial snakes of The Meathooks.

The practice of putting Winter wind in a body is not a lost one.

\#

name: 2 - Opening Conflict - Blob 3
text:
By the time you hear the first crack of ice, it will be too late. You have stepped onto a frozen pond, high in the mountains.

The ice was solid. It is what lay beneath it from the crack ensues.

Gasps.

The frozen dead crash rather than gasp up from the frozen surface of the lake. You'll have to fight your way through growing numbers of bones covered in wet silks and green mail. Some carry hurling stones; take care to shield your lady.

To survive, you must reach the frozen inlet on the opposite side of the lake. (Quickly, for many reasons, not the least of which is the shifting, splitting terrain).

If you reach the inlet, the gasps will retreat. Back under the ice.

You'll have list sight of The Road by now. You should proceed toward the risen sun.

\#

name: 3 - Mentor - Blob 1
text:
**Hueless on Kirk:**

Your Steel is already in hand as the grand beast with anchor pelt weaves out of the mist and rocks. The wolf is an older Time's brood; its gaze is prehistoric. A Fimbulwolf.

Atop the bristling fur of the spine sits a freakish creature. Like a toddler two years old in size and shape, this thing's texture is wax, but milk-colored. It's elongated forehead shines under a scalp of four thin hairs. It frowns from bloodless lips, and stares a bloodless stare.

At first you think the odor in the air is from the wolf; ancient petrichor. Then you realize the smell comes and goes, not with the wind, but with the breathing of the toddler-freak.

You hold your Steel foreward. The wolf growls.

After a moment the toddler-freak opens its blanched lips. It's voice sounds like water trickling in a library.

name: 3 - Mentor - Blob 2
text:
*Growl.* "Kirk says you're trespassing. He stirs this snow, these rocks. You must not be locals, the locals are more polite. Or scared.

*Growl.* "Kirk says he likes the hair of the lady. That bluish-midnight is a hue we have seen older Time.

...

*Growl.* "Kirk says he thinks he understands. A way through the Meathooks Peaks? The Road? It is only a little farther, but the crags like to lie.

*Growl.* "Kirk says he can show you. For a favor..."

name: 4 - First Transition - Blob 1
text:
Hueless on Kirk requests you to slay the tunnel's master, The Stillbreather. You may decline. If you do, Hueless on Kirk will tell you, leave.

\#

name: 4 - First Transition - Blob 2
text:
Hueless, the rider of fimbulwolf, is an old homunculus. A homunculus is created when a necromancer grows a fetus in a stoppered flask buried in cow entrails. He then uncorks it and nurses it on mammal blood. The greater the mammal, the stronger the resulting homunculus.

Though Hueless told you that Kirk liked your Lady's hair, the truth is that they both liked your breathing, its tonality and rhythm.

\#

name: 4 - First Transition - Blob 3
text:
You emerge from the Lost Glaciertop, and step onto The Road. The tunnel waits for you. You see two doors; one of bronze, one of moonstone. Erratic, cold gusts issue through the crack between them. The doors are unlocked. 

\#

name: 5 - New Lover - Blob 1
text:
"The air is sharper in here. Sharper even then those cutting winds on the faces of the mountains. And the gusts here carry as much weight.

Look, my knight. See the gleam of the ice upon the walls? See it gleaming like turquoise, without any shadow of the mountain stone.

"Why is it so clear? The entry is only a speck now, you carry no lamp, yet I see better than under the brightest moon.

"There is a sick ambient light in the air. I- Look there, my knight. A scrap of paper."

\#

name: 5 - New Lover - Blob 2
text:
**Exodus, Day 107, Thirteenth Hour:**

The servants of this tunnel-master, Kreicher, let their dogs loose upon us. My comrade Lerch was bitten on his hand. He will live, but is no longer scarless. I shall miss him, he was a credit to the order. I was fortunate to take no wounds.

These same servants collapsed the ceiling in the trunk corridor. It will not matter, we are already ferreting the roots and branches.

The natives say that Kriecher claims he cannot die. He has secreted his breath away in a seamless box, and while this is safe, he will persist in inhaling and exhaling. We shall soon see.

\#

name: 5 - New Lover - Blob 3
text:
As you walk the frozen tunnel you may find more pages from the outcast's journal. Some are half-frozen under the ice. If you find all the notes, you will learn a few things: that he was a warrior in a troop; that he fought with the servants of this 'Kriecher' over several days; that this siege took place about twenty years ago; and that he was a fine warrior, never cut in battle.

You'll eventually reach a distinct stretch of tunnel. The walls still gleam, but not with flawlessly deep translucence. There are men frozen in the walls. Maidservants, butlers, kitchen staff. They seem frozen mid-service. Your Lady stares into the stoic face one laundress, holding a tablecloth mid-jump. Your Lady says, after some time, that they remind her of castle life.

\#

name: 5 - New Lover - Blob 4
text:
**Exodus, Day 109, Sixteenth Hour:**

We conducted our final fight against Kreicher before the break of day this morning. Having no surise to embolden us in this cave, we struck up a burning bonfire on a sled, and dragged it with us to his Cave of the Standing Stone.

The master had five men with axes and chainmail. And potent necromancy. On our side were myself, Northur, Scour, and Wayd the Red.

I cannot say how long we filled these caves with the echoes of our crahsing weapons. We on our side spent all of our leather scrolls, the scrolls of vigor, the scrolls of killing. And, Norther and Wayd are dead. Only Scour and I survived.

But we won. I think. And we were not scarred.

I do not know if this Kreicher is completely dead. We found no 'box of breath'. But his body is chopped and burned, and we may proceed east. Toward Hornwater.

There are still some servants ahead in the tunnel. But I did not endure being stripped of my family's Pinecone Crest, cross the road, slay their master, only to be blooded by minions.

\#

name: 5 - New Lover - Blob 5
text:
"Pinecone Crest; my eyes must lie.

"My knight, can it be him who we seek?

"But twenty year... And we now-

"Let us loiter not."

name: 6 - Second Transition - Blob 1
text:
Until now you'll have followed the trail of letters. When you hit a wall of frozen rubble, however, it's time to find a byway.

Taking all lefts or rights won't work. There are offshoots upon offshoots, descents, rises. Nor will marking explored tunnels. The gusting air smooths away any imperfections in the frozen walls.

After some hours, your eyes will grow heavy. If you have chewing sponge, you can use it here. It will only delay the inevitable.

FLASH. A gust blast and green lightning-light wakes you. (Or puts you to sleep?) The dazzle lingers in your afterward, tinting vision's corners in green.

name: 7 - Wilderness - Blob 1
text:
**The Frozen Tree of Hanging Men:**

Have you been sleepwalking?

The broad and rimy oak fills the chamber with its barren limbs. From the four largest limbs, five dead men hang. (The biggest limb has room for two). With arms extended, each corpse points in a different direction.

The man without eyes points east.

The man without nose points north.

The man without ears point down a jagged seam in the floor.

The man without tongue points west.

The other man always points at you.

\#

name: 7 - Wilderness - Blob 2
text:
**The Frozen Fates:**

 Are you dreaming, or awake?

There seem to be *green* hallucination in the ice. As you through this long, tall, wide; as you cross the cyclopean vault, you see images of yourself underfoot. Not perfectly, but yourself after meeting terrible ends. There is your burned self, your drowned and bloated self, your headless self, etc.

These figures will eventually rise and attack. They wield arms like your Steel and Shield, but made of ice. They'll strike at you in your own techniques and patterns, but slower, having just come unthawed. This fight will not be too difficult. Use it as an opportunity to analyze your own style for weaknesses.

\#

name: 7 - Wilderness - Blob 3
text:
**The Frozen Power:**

From where comes this nightmare?

There lives an inherent facet of necromancy in the element of Ice. Some undead such as the gasp or the Seventh-House-Bell are stronger in colder environments. Ice has always been the preferred method of preservation among mancers; frequently it becomes a recurring motif in a mancer's spells.

It is not all that surprising. True death is cold, life is warm.

But some learned scholars (you know who) maintain that Ice has a specific connection with un-living; with making dead things alive again. One says that the demon kings could not stand to touch ice, and so the seven god-necromancers built their laboratories from the element. Another says that, at the core of Time is a glacier that is always sweating. When the glacier has become a lake, Time will cease, and all will drown in the flood.

\#

name: 7 - Wilderness - Blob 4
text:
**The Frozen River and Spillway:**

Will the vision never end?

You stand on the edge of the river, looking down the sloping angle of the spillway, into the void. The slope is jagged, lumpy, uneven, slick. You notice, however, four grooves here at the top of the slope, which snake down through the rough ice-hill.

Not all the slides go all the way to the bottom, and there are several stages of landings and slides you must progress through. The only way to learn is by trial and error. Each time you make a mistake, you run the risk of a fracture or sprain. You'll then have to climb back up the rougher terrain and try again. Don't try climbing or sliding down the jagged tracts; it will end mushily.

\#

name: 8 - Third Transition - Blob 1
text:
At last you begin to feel a progressive inflating in the irregular gale-blasts. Each step, the air glows brighter, rubs colder, smells older and more hostile. You see your Blue Lady wag her face, as if she too were waking from a trance.

The tunnel opens into a spacious underground hollow. You arrive on one shore of a petrified lake. The gravelly shore circles along the wall of the cave, around the edge of the lake. On the opposite side, you see the path leads into the portcullis of a somber, frozen castle.

Stepping along the shore, the crystal-crusted gravel crunches under you steel boots. Each breath of wind howls through the creases of your armor. The ambient light glows brighter and brighter through your visor; you soon realize that the green glow seeps through the castle's rime coated windows.

You step under a portcullis of icicles. You mount a broad stair of slabs of ice. You enter into a low undercroft of granite arches, and with tympana carved above each arch, and with ribbed pillars of ice.

The alcoves are lined with lidded stone box. Each box carries a familial name: Dornspace, Hollenregen, Bal-Khold. The boxes are sarcophagi. This is a chrypt.

name: 9 - Mysterious Master Foe - Blob 1
text:
One feretory, at the end of the central corridor of pillars, lies lidless. It shines like a sunken sun under a scummy pond surface. It is the source of the glowing winds.

Your Lady stays close behind as you approach. The side of the open tomb is painted, a flaking, frosted picture of a horn of plenty, spilling forth a bounty of colorful autumn leaves.

Nearer. Nearer.

As you peer over the edge of the box, you see it is occupied.

A man, his long apron-beard flecked with white, his spotted eggshell face pickled into imperishability, with his frozen-together fingers clasped over his coat, with his heavy and wild eyebrows dropped down over his shut eyes, and with his lipless mouth parted, breaths in fits. His emerald teeth tint each biting exhale.

You raise your Steel to strike.

The eyes snap open.

\#

name: 9 - Mysterious Master Foe - Blob 2
text:
No matter how you try you won't be able to strike The Stillbreather. Your Steel turns, as if by a magnet.

This swiftly piles on several factors in swift succession. You'll spend the opening moments learning First, you'll hear another sarcophagi burst open. A giant glowing firebeetle will hop from inside and buzz toward you. Keep yourself between it and your Lady. Use your shield to block its initial charge, then chop away at its shell. The carpace is tough, it will probably take a second block and recoil before you crack it.

A new beetle emerges from a sarcophagus every thirteen seconds.

name: 9 - Mysterious Master Foe - Blob 3
text:
At the same time, The Stillbreather will continue to exhale and inhale, exhale and inhale. Now, however, his breath is deadly steady. Every seven seconds you will hear a sound like a brazen gong. His breath will flash out from his teeth, and any active beetles will flash as well. Make sure to count the seconds and either avert or shut your eyes from any flash. Otherwise you'll be blinded for fifteen precious seconds.

You'll have to guard your Lady close throughout the fight. If she's left alone, the beetles will melt her skin.

Once you've entered fighting rhythm, and have some spare seconds, start examining the tombs. Your goal is to find the leather egg where The Stillbreather hid his breath. There are, however, hundreds of stone boxes...

The key's in the names. The first names are almost all unique, but the familial names are always one of six. The egg will be in the sarcophagus with the name of a family from which no firebeetle emerges. Moreover, the first name of on the sarcophagus with the egg will always be one of: Valk, Morvalk, Sunnblind, Wytch. Thus, with each beetle, you have a chance to narrow down the choices. Be warned: if you open a sarcophagi with an incorrect familial name, you will be struck by Vengeful Ice.

When you finally find the leather, The Stillbreather tries a last trick. His breath becomes an endless howl, and green light swells throughout the chrypt.

Mark your target. Clamp your eyes shut. Hack down with your Polished Steel.

\#

name: 10 - Fourth Transition - Blob 1
text:
The leather egg ruptures with a blast of sulfurous fumes. Dead man's breath. The reek billows like geyser spray past your blocking arm, rubbing the skin under your armor.

The stinking gale fades. You crack your eyes open. The green air that would have burned your sight is dim now, and dimming swiftly ever second. Your suggest a torch.

If you step back and examine The Stillbreather's stone casket, you find him almost the same. His body is still frozen the same, hands clutched over chest, lips apart, eyes open. The gleam in the eyes is glazed now, however, and is the gleam on the teeth.

Your Lady jolts her hand out; she snatches a piece of paper fluttering the in the dying air.

\#

name: 10 - Fourth Transition - Blob 2
text:
**Exodus, Day 109, Twenty-first Hour:**

One more steep tunnel, but we finally see the sky. Scour and I have survived.

Now, onward. To Hornwater we journey. We will present ourselves to The Order of the Leather Dragon, and make our claims. Though we are unscarred, they may wish to test us, not trusting our deeds. I am not afraid.

But that is many miles. First we must leave The Meathooks.

Already, the faces of my father, my half-sibling, seem as ghosts to me.

\#

name: 11 - Future and Finale - Blob 1
text:
The mountain air hits you as you step from the tunnel mouth. Your Lady shivers; but only in her body.

"The night is bright - see how my breathe clouds - but I see its lambency as pure. Not like the sick color of that cave.

"That last climb must have been the same the former traveller took; twenty years ago...

"Back there, I felt for a time as I do when I dream. I felt not the cold or the wind, and all that I saw was cloudy and prismatic. But I never once saw the ghost of- her. My daughter. And I always see her in my dreams.

"Not all we met was reality perhaps. But the letters: oh my knight I believe those. I still have this one here. Pinecone Crest. He *must* have been my stepson."

name: 11 - Future and Finale - Blob 2
text:
An aurora fills the starry night; swirls of white, blue, purple and gold; like a burning phoenix on the firmament. The ragged cliffs and hills make an invisible silhouette with the night sky. There the stars and aurora cease.

To your left, there is one silhouette of a shape irregular. It's outline is of a small being atop a large canine, limned by the purple and golden swirls. You feel all of Hueless on Kirk's eyes upon you.

Straight ahead of you are more of the curving mountain hooks. They cast a shadow that is deeper over the land below. In places they fall over The Snaking Road. (The epitaph stones pale against the turf).

A wind gusts past. But it is brisk, refreshing, natural.

name: 11 - Future and Finale - Blob 3
text:
*Your Blue Lady's spirit seems lighter. Is this the most she has said since The Tower of Silence? Yet she might have died back in that tunnel. That was an ill place to lead her.*

*You wonder what will happen with The Stillbreather truly dead. Will his tunnel be a warm place for other walkers on The Road? Will the frozen bodies thaw and rot? And have you acted rightly, in serving the ends of the homunculus that rides the fimbulwolf?*

*That is not your concern. Your duty now is to guide your Lady to Hornwater.*

*A far city. You were there, once. But that was long ago.*

name: 11 - Future and Finale - Blob 4
text:
You knew there are three main routes that go from the tunnel to Hornwater.

**The Road of Graves:** This time, strict adherence to The Road is not ideal. You can follow it east toward Hornwater for a hundred miles more. After that, before reaching Hornwater, it loops south along a thousand-mile stretch, through the frigid and friendless Limbichorous Dunes.

**The Scarab Mines:** Longer in miles. Along the way you may stop at the cave-town of Alsoburg for supplies.

**The Forest of Candles and Skulls and Roaches and Fungi and Ruptured Capillaries:** You've had enough of forests.

No matter which route you choose, and like your Lady's stepson, who passed this way twenty-odd years ago, your first step is to leave The Meathook Mountains.

You will notice a sheltered overhang, only a few miles perhaps along The Dark Road below. Lead your Lady there, where you may strike tonight's camp.

name: 11 - Future and Finale - Blob 5
text: In The Time of Dying lies a Road of Graves.

name: Setup - Blob 1
text:
*Whomp. Clang. Whomp. Clang.*

Upon the grassy hummock, above the mine's mouth, two veins of euhedral lead shine raw under the sky.

*Whomp. Clang. Whomp. Clang.*

The veins are polished by The Compass, local mancers, erudite rustics learned in the features of the mine. They are, at this very moment, operating.

*Whomp. Clang.*

Two stand under a tree of hanging men, chalking glyphs onto a door-sized onyx slate.

*Whomp. Clang.*

Two more straddle the vein, one applying polish, one laying out unguents beside the prepared flesh.

*Whomp. Clang. Whomp. Clang. Whomp. Clang.*

A group of mancers has already begun the spell; a communal address to summon a spirit through the lead.

As one they raise their mauls and pummel the earth: *Whomp.*

As one they hiss a deadspeech spell until, from a suspended nickel-silver gong, a sourceless vibration stirs: *Clang.*

One mancer picks a route downhill, around the mine's mouth, toward you.

name: Setup - Blob 2
text:
"Still going-in bent, you? Going-in is never The Compass, but through the convenient, soul-conductive lead only, spirits drawing.

"Spirits too-many.

"Miner's gone. Dead asylum for a while. Older Time. But started gathering any scarab, did keepers. Massing. Millions of mad souls lying. Seasons, who knows how many? Thousands?

"Try drawing your soul, when you die, from lead will The Compass."

name: Setup - Blob 3
text:
As the mancer, muttering, picks his way back up the slope, you turn your visor on your Blue Lady.

*Will you lead her there, that place more mad-chrypt than quarry, where even men whose minds spark about such subjects will not enter?*

*But, you must lead her to Hornwater. This way is easier than the desert or the woods, so you hope.*

*The Scarab Mine is storied with ghosts. Even as far distantly as home. Rumor often runs with erratic step, but regardless, it does you no harm to keep your Steel drawn an inch.*

name: Setup - Blob 4
text:
A strange phenomenon occurs. You see a low-gliding, small, detached cloud sliding across the sky, against the wind. Its shadow idles up the hummock. It drifts above the hanging tree. The shadow stops, settles, over one corpse especially, the corpse of a Compass mancer who was caught in sadism, and thus earned his current position.

The other mancer don't notice, but your Lady does, when the glassy eyes of the hanged man flick in your direction. As you and your Lady reach the roots of the tree, the smell of putrefaction brings water to your eyes.

name: Setup - Blob 5
text:
You meet the glazed eye. When the swollen mouth speaks, the voice is distinctly deep, distinctly resonant.

"It is Othelmedir, sir. I petitioned certain of those dead, some by that sword on your hip, and found you.

"I have cast this shadow remotely, and have no minutes for chaff. In This reservoir mineral and spiritual holds one scarab of personal interest, an ancestor, one who has languished, in the Stope of the Ninth Ozone, on a white stone shelf, in a red scarab.

"Shatter the scarab. All your Lady's debts shall be paid. Moreover you will notice a special stone near that shelf, a quite special stone, and further reward.

"A parting word: dawdle not inside. Too many lifetimes inside. Too many voices."

name: Setup - Blob 6
text:
The mouth is open, doorless, with lips black and square. Carved ebony frames the mine's throat. The two supporting beams are carved; one with a tall, thin man, with the head of a sparrow, and a long beard; the opposite with a tall, thin woman, with the head of a snail, and no compassion.

The architrave is painted amber and covered in glyphs. As you pass under, you feel static in your teeth.

\#

name: Conflict - Blob 1
text:
Your Lady begs a pause, her breath rasping, the momentum of her run carrying her to a kind of blunt parapet of natural stone teeth. You feel the sting in your own lungs. You look back down the decline tunnel from which you've come, however, and are loathe to stop. The light of your Cat's Eye Spear only extends a few yards; but in the black beyond, you still hear it.

The rhythm.

It is a pattern like footsteps. But each step sounds consequential, like a dropped weight in some higher left of a storied old church. And somehow each step sounds singular, as if it were that one isolated crack of a stick in an unfamiliar holt, which says, "You are not alone."

If you linger The Alien Knight and his ghosts will catch up and surround you. You can try and fight again, but you will lose. Your Steel cannot cut ghosts. Your only option is to find a place to hide.

The long gallery onto which you've emerged runs to your right for perhaps a hundred yards. It is one of several stepped galleries in this cyclopean stope. It is best to keep your eyes from dropping over the parapet. Worse than ghosts abides below, in earth's osseous stone.

To escape, pull your Lady along the parapet until you reach a broken portcullis, half-descended over a tunnel, with another ramp beyond. Halfway along, you many be tempted by circular stair. This is a deathtrap. One of the Alien Knight's ghosts will cut you off when you are halfway up, and the others will by then block your retreat.

After ducking the portcullis, follow the ramp for five hundred steps or so. You'll eventually spot a small arch on your left, with billet molding of sparkling stone cylinders. Pass through, turn around, draw the translucent lead curtain closed, and knot it tightly to the jade rod on the wall.

You've bought yourself some breathing room, though it won't last long.

\#

name: Conflict - Blob 2
text:
They gather on the other side of the curtain; the seven ghost-servants of The Alien Knight. There is the ghost in the coat of silent jingling bells. It dances up to the lead curtain. It presses its nose, ringed by a bell, against the reinforced cloth. Then there is the marmoset ghost, motionless, leering, sliding side-to-side, with stretched arms and fingers.

The seven sidle up to the lead curtain, but cannot pass.

Then comes The Alien Knight.

You cannot bring yourself to look at this nemesis, not directly, not all of its shape conjoined. In your brief earlier encounter, before you fled, you were able to follow the knight's two bent sword, blocking, parrying. You tracked its footwork, the lift and step of its black sabatons, each lift portentous, each step booming.

Now it is the same. You see The Alien Knight step thunderingly to the curtain. You see its swords hack at the translucent curtain. But still, no matter how you wish, you cannot behold its pith.

name: Conflict - Blob 3
text:
"The curtain bends to soak those cuts but, reinforced though it is, will not last I fear.

"My knight, we must find surer refuge. I know you would conquer, were the combat equal.

"Where has the fell knight chased us? Scarcely can I imagine we've walked a fraction of this mine's breadth, and already a disorder of twists lies behind.

"I cannot- No time! He sunders the curtain."

name: Conflict - Blob 4
text:
Travel quickly along the narrow access tunnel until you reach a long quadripartite vault, with a roof of brickwork and ribs of dull iron.

From here you can take one of a few routes.

**Left:** You'll eventually emerge onto the brink of an unfathomed quarry. An underground lighthouse perches off to your left, its beam of crystal moonlight making sweeps of the quarry's upper hemispheres, animated by necromancy. A dizzying screw stair within leads to higher tunnels via a connecting bridge. Beware: a skeleton with a sling guards the bridge.

**Right:** Use the 'all-rights' strategy to navigate a burg of hex houses, quarters for the inhabitants of old. In this Time the only dwellers in the homes are the ubiquitous scarabs. They watch you from their lead-lined cavities, only the deep-earth lead keeping the souls within from disintegrating.

**Straight:** The Miner's Stoa. A ceiling of dripstone impends above, where each distinct stalactite has bee carved with a bat's-head termina, and each bat's mouth holds one wooden scarab. If you happen to spot it, you might snatch the sack of stained-glass dust that sits in one of the mouths. It is worth fifty square coins.

No matter which route you choose, your destination is the same. The bank of a subterranean river. The opposite bank lies beyond your sight, nor is any crossing apparent; which is troubling, as you hesitate to swim this 'stream'.

It is a river of scarabs.

name: Conflict - Blob 5
text:
Baleful steps boom. You have only a second to look, vainly, for a crossing, before The Alien Knight attacks.

The knight slashes out from the tunnel, his curved sword arcing for your Lady. Caught with your eyes on the scarab river, you see your Lady stumble and away from the blow. She trips, sinking to a knee.

Get ready for a short but intense melee. You'll need to block or parry a scything torrent from this foe you cannot behold. The sequence of chops, jabs, and undercuts is entirely random. You only need to last for a half-a-minute, until your Lady rises. But your reaction-time must be perfect; his swords can cut your armor like cheesecloth.

Your Lady rises just in time - the seven ghosts wade out of the dark after their leader. You have no choice now. Enter the stream.

name: Conflict - Blob 6
text:
The wooden scarabs clink and clatter about your waist as you backpedal into the flow. The Alien Knight and ghosts pursue you to pebble-like edge. Then they stop. They will not enter.

At the river's deepest point its scarabs rise to your chin. You feel them slipping through gaps in your armor. Their polished wooden shells rub against your skin. Are these phylacteries full?

The footsteps are gone. The crossing is silent, but for the wooden clacking. You will never know the cost.

When you finally reach the other bank, your Blue Lady collapses to the stone. Wounded.

\#

name: Drive - Blob 1
text:
**Toke's Crossroads:**

The last long step of the stair final terminates, depositing you into a chamber with a roughly-flat quadrangle of stone floor. The stone roof, held a few feet above your helmet by splintered woodbeams, bows in the middle like a depressed arch.

You march a little to your right and, beside a low and uneven wall of dry rocks, settle your Blue Lady on the floor.

The room is diamond shaped, with diagonals of forty and sixty feet. At one of the acute vertices two sets of steps ascend from the room, meeting in a corner with an empty earthen pot. The other corner is the same. All four sets, one of which you entered by, are twelve times as wide and long as they are steep. Each step moreover holds a semicircular ground-niche on either side, too short to inspect entirely while standing, yet big enough to fit a man or worse. Each niche holds one of the following at random: a basin full of scarabs, a grotesque idol, noisy spiders, or something else.

A Mirror-calm Pond spreads over a large portion of the floor. The short wall beside which you laid your Lady surrounds this pond. You glimpse the reflection of your helmet in the pool. The water has too much lead to drink.

name: Drive - Blob 2
text:
As you tend her wound your Lady's voice is audible above the noisy spiders.

"There is little pain. It was only a cut, more shocking than catastrophic. I have known greater ruin.

"That river, or rather an ocean it seemed, of the wooden beetles, finding and caressing every goosebump of my skin - that was worse. I feel drained. As if I had stood for days before an assembly , always with at least one set of eyes upon on.

"That the strange knight has been detoured may be pearl midst the muck.

"I can go no farther now, my knight. The weakness shames me, but my eyeslids are too heavy. If you can watch... only wake me..."

name: Drive - Blob 3
text:
*There. The bleeding in her arm has stopped now. Let the medicine of rest work its good.*

*To what end? You are lethally lost under a mountain of rock and lead.*

*You brought her to this. Louse. She may starve, or fall to the bent swords of your pursuer.*

*Why can't you look at that knight?*

\#

name: Drive - Blob 4
text:
The shapes and natures and desires of the un-dead are many. Some shapes, natures, and desires are more terrible than others.

**Mailed Flayer:** A blessedly deathless knight. This armored and skillful creature acts as a resurrectionist's deterrent, in tombs and chrypts sacred to The Scratcher.

**Halo of Worms:** Though it can take many figures, this collection of undead flatworms most often forms a circle, around the plinths of statues. The worms do not feed, they kill for sport.

**Lectergast:** "...Wraithish, worthy and wroth, he runs; Eyelashily dancing, for strong sons; And under the ossuaries, there he dwells, and tells; Of the paladin's dulcet funereal bells..."

name: Drive - Blob 5
text:
You have some hours to recover, but not forever.

You may want to search some of the nearest stairway niches. One of the niches contains a marble statue, of a leering ballerina with a horned demon-head. If you pull the stone hand from the statue, a passage opens to a secret treasure room. There you will find a Ring of Perfect Leather.

Another alcove is of special interest. You will find, ringed by a circle of carefully laid scarabs, their antlers inward-turned, a book. It is a mancer's journal. It is worth your time to skim the pages...

\#

name: Drive - Blob 6
text:
...warding circles at regular intervals. They wont last though, removed from the lead. Must carry replacements, or replenish the soulmatter...

...from Toke's Crossroad and along the left-descending steps, until you reach that canyon of humming blackness. Then, crossing the bridge of withered wood...

...many scarabs, piles and piles, but all are empty. Stick to the wall of mildew-smelling bricks for at least a hundred notches, otherwise that beast will scent you. Cross the courtyard quickly, step into the third tower, and ascend the broken stair for...

...at least three ways through the basilica. The quickest is the corridor with the sky-mural floor, but my singing mushroom warns of at least one ghost in there. Better to take...

...Leper's Curse on Othelmedir. Whysoever did I accept...

...slipped past the colony. Days gone, but The Stope of the Ninth Ozone lies just ahead. There is the white stone shelf; on it...

\#

name: Finale - Blob 1
text:
*Clap.* With a noise like the beater of a wooden loom slamming against its beam, the red scarab, like a fat drop of blood against the marble shelf, splits under your Steel. You also hear a soft gasp; perhaps it was only your Blue Lady.

She steps off to your right. Her limp is gone, as is her bandage. The preceding days of travel have stitched her cut. She skirts one of the reflective, metallic pools that dot this dripping feretory. She extends a hand to a polished, flat stone the color of an olive pit.

**Shelley Stone:** From the first Time comes this relic of potent necromancy. Once and only once place it on the throat of one who is not too long dead, and he shall live again.

Water strikes your helmet. Looking up, you see the ceiling is a network of metal. Struts. Gears. Chains. One wide shaft ascends straight up. Another drop falls on you from this hole; rain which has trickled down miles of rock to wet your armor.

You see a lever on the nearest wall.

\#

name: Finale - Blob 2
text:
After you hear the first echo of a chain rattling somewhere miles above, you will hear, immediately on its heels, the *boom, boom, boom* of fateful steps.

The Alien Knight will enter the feretory anywhere between three and twenty seconds after you hear his steps. He attacks; his ghosts attack too. You still cannot look directly at him.

You should have formed a circle of the scarabs you took from the basilica. If you did not it is too late; you must die. If you did, step inside the circle with your Lady. It will protect you from the ghosts.

name: Finale - Blob 3
text:
The scarabs will not, however, protect you from the knight. He will stop for only a moment, then step into the circle.

The Alien knight fights with predictable attack sequences. Left cut. Right cut. Leap back. Lunge. Deflecting or averting these blows is straightforward. Your foe likes to strafe, and setting yourself between him and your Lady can be more of a challenge. Keeping her near the center of the circle will make it easier.

All this is sufficient for a standstill, but in no way can you land a blow. The Alien Knight parries every attack. You will tire before he does; you must find a way to see him...

name: Finale - Blob 4
text:
Your first clue will probably be the surrounding puddles. If you glance at one with a good angle, you will see the knight's whole reflection.

His reflection has no body. Only arms and legs. Even in a mirror, there is void.

There is a 'blind spot' necromancy at work. The power animating him will not show his heart to a naked pair of eyes.

But with one eye shut...

He suddenly appears entirely, his armor black and with a thousand facets like onyx, but seeming to shine with inner stars.

Once you can see your foe the fight turns in your favor. Time your attacks, block his, and wear The Alien down piece by pieces.

\#

name: Finale - Blob 5
text:
The ghosts vanish.

The chain lift arrives.

You step in the cage.

The air is like a glass of cool water - the sunrise, like a father's love - as you step onto the broad ledge on the eastern mountainside. Nearby is a scummy pond. A lone wooden tree, dead, almost branchless, just from the middle, the two twigs of its pinnacular fork framing a silver web. In the distance, through the web, you spy The Road.

\#

name: Finale - Blob 6
text: In The Time of Dying lies a Road of Graves.

name: 1. Title Card - Blob 1
text:
**The Nightmare of the Honey Locust:**

*The stones of The Road sink softly under your sabatons. Each one recedes slowly into the mortar, and each stone bends. Why? Where is this tract of the epiotaphs, and how did you come to it?*

*Where have you left your Blue Lady?*

*Ungodly is the stink in the air. Ungoldly: something of the ancient rot of vegetables sewn before the god-necromancers. Like grease the air seems to paint your throat-*

*Focus. You must find your Lady.*

*But how? The Road looks empty ahead, behind. To the left and right there are only these two dense walls of forest trunks.*

*Such trunks as well... Like sea urchins with their knots of black thorns.*

name: 1. Title Card - Blob 2
text:
*So hard it seems to ask the right questions - 'Where is this?' 'How came you here?" - with this sharp gnawing hurt in your stomach. Such a hunger you cannot remember. Those thorns, those honey locust trees-*

*Louse, stop your mind. That thought is lunacy; to drool over the tines on a tree.*

*Your steps already carry you to The Road's flank, almost against your will. Have you ever let your will crumble so?*

*The peat smell is stronger here, near the trunks, under the shade of the boughs. You wonder what bides in nature that could produce such an odor. And that sound: like continuous, deep gulping. But the trees are too densely packed to see.*

*There. You are close enough touch the spines now. You see that they are inedible.*

*Has your visor been up all this time?*

*Your stomach-*

*The thorns-*

*Don't-*

name: 1. Title Card - Blob 3
text:
*Pain. Pain. Pain.*

*Why man did you bite that cluster. But what is this? Your mouth does not bleed. Only the maiden acupuncture of your face and throat that stung. They seem to have left no wound. And your hunger is gone.*

*Wait. You feel something else now.*

*Pain.*

*Like a pressure building under your skin.*

*Pain.* 

*Was that a voice?*

*Your- ah, your face. Bulging. Something- pressing- up through- your skin-*

*That voice again. Saying... it will hold your hands...*

\#

Just before waking, you hear a chime.

\#

name: 2. Arrival of the Foe - Blob 1
text:
As your senses wake you hear your Blue Lady's voice.

"Rise, and look to those strings of cloud swimming on the sea of the sky. The slate-colored sun has mounted his vessel to sail that water once more; the heath is dawn-warm.

"You saw the nightmare?

"So many mancers must there be; vexing the seven god-necromancers each hour with their orisons; so many, and it amazes my heart that one of our gods should bend their hoary eye upon you, who are faithful, with their nightmare.

" We will find which has cursed you. We will bring you from this shadow, my cracked cup knight. I promise."

name: 2. Arrival of the Foe - Blob 2
text:
Here in the northernmost reach of the Middlemoss heath lie several of the gods' Pilgrim Tombs. 

**The Fourth Green:** The Mother of Worms is the fourth god-necromancer whose domain is 'wanting'. Her Pilgrim Tomb can be found beneath the ruined brickwork of a gutted brothel. The bouncer behind the tomb door halts you with a riddle. The correct answer is 'Ginseng'.

**The Leper Stone:** The Thin Man's ill mask looks down its flaccid cheeks of plaster from the upper middle of this menhir. Though it is a bad ideal, the colony of lepers camping around the shrine's base will not stop you if you take the mask.

**The Wandering Barrow:** This barrow's entry changes to one of five different locations every night at midnight. The bodies are different at each location, but the smell of old death is the same.

**The Chine Pit:** This is not really a shrine to Gallbladder, but to a famous Middlemoss butcher, who was a great friend among the local mancers. There is nothing here for you.

All four shrines are dissapointing. None of these gods gives you their sign of will, good or evil.

name: 2. Arrival of the Foe - Blob 3
text:
At some point in your day's pilgrimages, you will be stopped by a bent old man in a cloak, walking on two canes.

"In sooth, I came hither what time I marked thy tread upon the heather.

"I trow not thy errand, yet ken that thou seekst rede or remedy.

"Nettles and spit on the wights yclept 'Mancers'. Greater witching keeps in the wit.

"Get thee to Yellowmoor, yon manor. The hypnotist shall hew whate'er works thee woe."

name: 3. Enter the Arena - Blob 1
text:
The old man sets his right arm on the crook of his right cane, and points.

**Yellowmoor Manor:**

A mile-long slope unfurls down from where you stand, densely-woven in heather the color of mustard. At the base of this slope four dead trees stand, tall, crooked, like the hands of metronomes.

The single manor in the middle of the trees seems more like four or five manors piled together, a tumble of architecture. Half the structure is sinking and bent. The other half is sick with spores. Three clocktowers rise from the roof, each giving a different time, each spinning at a different rate. A chimney exhales two lines of smoke in a double helix, one black, one white. The roof is poorly shingles: wooden shingles, shingles of pure onyx stone, teracotta. One low mist cloud obscures the place; it is not enough to cover the acreage, but spirals round the manor.

A distant chime-carillon strikes up a complicated fugue in D-minor. It is the same chime you heard when you woke.

\#

name: 4. False Hopes - Blob 1
text:
Though you did not visit without inviation, the image of Yellowmoor clings like grapevine to your imagination.

Your nightmares are going to get progressively worse over the coming week. Your only recourse is enduring, the hypnotist will not see you before the week is out. The best that you can do is bury yourself in duty.

You can follow one of two courses: continue the search for your Lady's stepson, or handling her daughter's haunting ghost. If you spend the week on the former, you'll receive the following clues.

**From the Reed Seller:** That he has been in Middlemoss for ten years, and memorized every passing face, and never seen a face like your Lady describes.

**From the Frozen Skeleton in the Mancer's Guild:** That knightly orders from the East sometimes recruit from the town.

**From Lady Orca:** That you and your Lady should move on...

If you instead pursue the latter objective, you can purchase a Hauntshield ritual for your Lady's protection. It will keep her from seeing any ghost for ten days.

\#

name: 4. False Hopes - Blob 2
text:
*Why cannot you move your legs? Look down; they are rooted in this concentration of sallow mud.*

*This is the dream again. You should wake yourself.*

*These other trees have thicker thorns than yours. You must break a way out, and break your own roots and thorns. Remember your-*

*What is that lumbering sound?*

*Something heaves its way through the cracking thorns. That sucking noise must be its enormous tread in this much which roots you. Draw your Steel. But your arms will not bend.*

*There. It show through the trunks. What is this shaggy leviathan mammal with tusks? How the trees seem to lean away. Making space for it. Leaving your own bark exposed.*

*You must run. Run. Run. It comes to eat you. Run!*

\#

As you wake, you hear the same chime.

\#

name: 4. False Hopes - Blob 3
text:
Near the end of the week the nightmares will begin to bleed into reality. You will have to deal with any three of the following afflictions.

**Merchants ' Ill Welcome:** Every shop gate, when it opens, rings not with a bell, but with a chime.

**Prickly Cuisine:** Your black bread, your tubers, your sheepmeat, all burst with prongs. You cannot eat it. You must go hungry.

**Immobilized:** A test of patience. Your Lady will eventually find you; her words break the spell.

**The False Foe:** This statue appears like an enemy about to strike. It is an illusion, only a statue, but the fear lasts for many hours after.

\#

name: 5. Trapped, Battle Dance - Blob 1
text:
The hypnotist's servant who comes to convey you to Yellowmoor is striking. He is hairless down to his eyebrows. His skin is shiny smooth like a baby, but covered in beetle-colored tattoos.

When he tries to take you alone your Lady will object.

"Let us both glide over the names on The Road, the spokes of your cart walking the shadows of my cracked cup knight and I forward together."

The wagon is stranger than its driver. Two decks. You are sent my a thin stair on its left side up to the top, which leans. Three plushly-upholstered violet armchairs, bolted to the boards, face sideways on this deck. It is pulled by two oxen, each with a mutant set of horns ending in five prongs like grasping fingers.

"I will hold your hands."

The tassels and stones of Middlemoss Heath roll in fast motion. Entering your helmet-vision on the right. Exiting left. Every stone on The Road jostled the thorns in your stomach.

"Look at those swans in the sky, my cracked cup knight."

The sky is no harbor. A colossal vulture with swirling eyes swooped there.

"Breathe slowly. It will be-"

The wagon stops with such abruptness that you are thrown out, over the driver, over the oxen and their horns.

You land at the door of Yellowmoor.

It opens.

\#

name: 6. The Master Appears - Blob 1
text:
Down the crooked carpet steps, one step at a time, with a *thumpthump, thumpthump, thumpthump*, like a decrepit breast, comes the man with two canes.

The hypnotist.

His hood is thrown back now. Tongue-clored hair forms a coarse, stiff mane with his beard of four points. His face is painted white with big circular eyeshadows. His nightshirt dances with strings of beads.

When he reaches the bottom step the hypnotist looks at you and winks.

The squelch of his eyelid is stretched-

Bloodless-

Greedy-

\#

name: 7. The Enemy Triumphs - Blob 1
text:
Your insides will start to quiver as the faintest laugh begins to trickle from the hypnotist's hair-framed lips.

You can try to escape, but it will gain you nothing. Your doom is sure. Nor can you attack the hypnotist. Your feet refuse to approach the painted invalid. A better use of the remaining moments is to slay or study the hypnotist's lackeys, now converging from two acacia doors on your right. There are six at present. They wear straw hats, and though they carry no weapons, their hands are large for grabbing. Each is differently-shaped. One of them is a starving sadist. You should behead that one now, with a horizontal chop; it will spare your lady his torments when you are disabled.

You will notice your shadow wavering near the end. It is a vision worth committing to memory.

The laughing chime clangs louder. Louder. LOUDER. In your skull. In your brain. Your vision  narrows to a tunnel lined in thorns.

As Katabasis swallows you, your last unaltered perception is the master of Yellowmoor's swirling laughter.

\#

name: 8. Introduction - Blob 1
text:
**The Trance of Yellowmoor:**

*Ouroboros is the trance, cyclic and swallowing.*

\#

name: 8. Introduction - Blob 2
text:
**The Trance of Yellowmoor:**

*You must push forward. You must; each moment that passes makes you half as slow. Forward is the only path.. to what? Why you slog you cannot remember, nor where this place of darkness is.*

*But this is not the thorn dream.*

*Is it?*

*You have the Cat's Eye Spear, your fingers not remembering when they pulled it from your pack, only knowing the feel of its grain in your palm. Hold it lower, and see what this morass is that clatters around your waist.*

*What are these? Painted beetles. Scarabs. They clack like wood, but seem to scuttle like living beetles as your legs plow a path. There must be thousands, stretching all directions. How came you into this sea?*

*That banging sound in the distance awakens the cowardice you try to bury. It booms, booms, booms, more fateful every moment, in time with the half-life of your momentum, growing, but not manifesting.*

*Why can't you turn around to look?*

\#

name: 8. Introduction - Blob 3
text:
**The Trance of Yellowmoor:**

*How many miles have you walked over The Road? How many epitaphs? How much of your Time? You will not retrieve those years, not by walking in reverse.*

*Then why do you?*

*You must allow that there is something rousing in the deep gully dropping away on your left, and the red-flower hedge on your right, and in the epitaphs, underfoot. You love the taste of the air, for even it feels younger in reverse.*

*And yet the gully now seems shallower. The hedge shrinks. And stone by stone, the road loses its names. Now it loses even its stones.*

*What is this?*

*...*

*Now it is only a thin line of stones.*

*Yes, you feel vibrant and younger. And the spring in your step drives you backward faster.*

\#

name: 8. Introduction - Blob 4
text:
**The Trance of Yellowmoor:**

*You wish you could remove your helmet, for this grotto whose walls are lined in webbed fungi is humid and dense of air. But you know you mustn't. Your face is repulsive. Better for Her not to see it.*

*Why does she stare at you so fixedly?*

*Why does her blue-black hair fall so freely around her bare shoulders? Her bare-*

*This is a dream you must not dream. Fix your eyes elsewhere. Your oath. Your oath. Remember your oath to the clan's noble blood. Do you forget that it hers is pure flesh? It must remain so for her gravestone.*

*Keep your eyes shut. That touch is imagined.*

*Please, let this dream end.*

\#

name: 8. Introduction - Blob 5
text:
**The Trance of Yellowmoor:**

*The stars wink. Slowly, off, then back on. Do you believe that the god-necromancers prolonged Time, knowing it would waver at its end? Have you come to the end?*

*It is not the stars. Birds in the night cause the heavens to flicker. A parliament of owls haunts the sky in a drove as thick as the murmuration of starlings.*

*That moon is the smallest you have ever seen, isn't it? Impossibly small, but twelve times as bright. You want to stare at it? Well, it does seem to conjure a bright sleep.*

*A sleep of pure illumination.*

\#

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot - Blob 1
text:
As you bury your eyes, in the reflection of those eyes behind your visor, in a pool of silver water, you hear your Blue Lady.

"My Cracked Cup Knight, do you feel my hand on your hand? Do your eyes receive that golden sepulchral light through the circle attic window? Do you understand that we have been sealed in this loft?

"Please come back from this blind stupor. You are not in the cul-de-sac your glazed eyes see.

"every moment your breathing is shallower, your chest rises less and less, your helmet droops as over a table game. You sit in a weak wooden chair, my knight. Please, there are no shackles.

"Follow my voice. Let it paint for you a stairway back from the pit."

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot - Blob 2
text:
To break free of The Trance of Yellowmoor, you must believe in your lady.

You'll hear her same four sentences repeated in each of the endless trances you see from this point forward. If you listen carefully to each of the sentences, you can focus on one key word or phrase in each. Picking the correct key will open up specific trances to you.

**'Golden Sepulchral Light':** Opens the trance of Goldegrave.

**'Cul-de-sac':** Opens the trance of The Dead End.

**'A Game Table':** Opens the trance of Toad's Chessboard.

**'Stairway':** Opens the trance of The Sinking Marble Pillar of the Sky.

At the conclusion of each of these special trances, you will hear that old chime sound. Each chime thrums through your body like an inner earthquake, bringing you closer back to reality.

\#

name: 9. Foes Revel, Heroes Plot - Blob 3
text:
You stand once again on the mushy tract of The Road, where in curls between bristling honey locusts. You feel aware. There is only a little fog at the corners of your visor.

You hear the chime clang. Not between your ears, but ahead. Between the thorns.

As you follow The Roads, your footsteps disfiguring the putty-like epitaphs, the air clings to your skin, under your plate and wool shirt.

The thorns spread apart to reveal - and to encircle - a soggy piazza. A fountain trickles to the left, disgorging dollops of amber algaic water.

At the center of the clearing is The Upside-down Campanile of the Dream Chime. Somewhere deep underground, at its belfry, the chime echoes up again-

\-and when you push open the upside-down copper door, and step onto the inverted first-floor landing, the chime rings again. Louder.

name: 10. The Second Conflict - Blob 1
text:
**Eleven Strokes with The Old Man Below :**

The chime clangs on, vibrating your bones' marrow.

It builds, marches, a battlefield snare pace.

In pain you cross the upturned landing.

Chiming irradiates the air.

Reaching the edge, you look down.

The abyss gazes up.

crumbled steps descend.

Hot chime-noise climbs.

You stiffen.

Bracing.

Staid.

name: 10. The Second Conflict - Blob 2
text:
Your task is to destroy The Trancing Chime, the tiny gleam at the bottom of this inverted campanile. That is the only way to escape (and survive) The Trance of Yellowmoor.

You can reach the corner-landing below by walking on the bottoms of the stone steps. But soon the stairwell is too fragmented; a gap ten feet across and twenty feet down spans the next flight.

Step beneath the threshold of this lower-landing doorway. You'll find yourself on a rotted workman's scaffold. An earthen wall of worm-hands will try to strangle you. Cut them. Proceed down the angled ledge. Alternate between scaffold and inner stairs. Endure the radiating chime.

If you endure to the bottom, simply slash the lurid silver chime.

\#

name: 11. The Relieved Breath - Blob 1
text:
The cloying attic dust burns in your reclaimed breath like smelling salt. Through watery eyes you see your Blue Lady, her face long but not harmed.

The light is dim golden and dim through the attic's only window. Broken clocks are stacked in the corners. The window is barred, but you find the hatch, and from its rattle know that you can break free.

Before you break the latch, something shudders in the hall below. Examining your scabbard, you find your steel within. As you draw it, its oil grabs dust from the air. It burns with the golden light.

\#

name: 12. The Hero and the Master - Blob 1
text:
"I trow thy singing hewer wert the source of the clamoring tune, that east I cast my voice as now, discomfited the brow of my house?

"Dost thou think thee freed? Freed of Yellowmoor's Trance? The wit is not so readily delivered of its woes.

"Great is the witching that has once caught its hook on the wit. Greater than that mancy. Yea, even than that mancy of breath and bane.

"Let thee come down to me, then, from the crown to the foot of my house. Ha, ha, ha. See if thou dost not discover more trance than truth."

name: 12. The Hero and the Master - Blob 2
text:
There are thirty-seven distinct floors or wings across the architectures of Yellowmoor manor. From the south-wing attic (where you begin) there are four viable routes you can discover to the first floor foyer (where you leave).

One way is to climb the shelving south chimney stones, cross the flying buttress which receives no sun, drop onto the marble balcony just below, smash through the stained glass window, and descend through the frozen servant's wing. If you go this way, make sure to grab the Eldest Sun Ring on the ledge in the chimney.
